Summary: MATLAB? stops responding when opening a file with more than 2,000 variables.
Description: When you open a MATLAB code file in the Editor and that file has more than 2,000 variables, you might get messages similar to the following, and then MATLAB stops responding: # An unexpected error has been detected by HotSpot Virtual Machine:##  EXCEPTION_ACCESS_VIOLATION (0xc0000005)## Java VM: Java HotSpot(TM) Client VM (1.5.0-b64 mixed mode)

Summary: When a Simulink fixed-point data type name contains encoded slope but non-encoded bias, fixdt(DataTypeNameString) fails to return a Simulink.NumericType object. For example, fixdt('ufix16_t0Ps286t8JVo_Bn250') fails.
Description: The Simulink software generates data type names for fixed-point data types. In R2006b, the data type name length was limited to 31 characters. If necessary, Simulink encoded parts of the scaling information to limit the name to 31 characters while maintaining full precision of the data type's slope and bias. The MATLAB command fixdt allows Simulink data type names to be converted back to Simulink.NumericType objects. In R2006b, fixdt(DataTypeNameString) failed if the slope was encoded, but the bias was not encoded. For example, if the original data type was fixdt(1,123,exp(1),-250), Simulink generated the data type name 'sfix123_t0Ps286t8JVo_Bn250' Attempting to convert this data type name back to an object by using fixdt('sfix123_t0Ps286t8JVo_Bn250') failed. ?
Workaround: Download the attached file fixdt.m.Save the downloaded file in: matlabroot/toolbox/simulink/fixedandfloat/fixpt.m, where matlabroot is the MATLAB install directory.NOTE This overwrites the original version of fixdt.mAlternatively, place the downloaded file higher on your MATLAB path. This can be tested using the MATLAB which command: which fixdt -all

Summary: Incorrect code generation: Context-dependent inputs of function call subsystems.
Description: The Real-Time Workshop build process might generate incorrect code for a model under the following conditions: The model contains a function call subsystem and a merge block The output of the function call subsystem is connected to the merge block The output of the merge block is an input to the function call subsystem
Workaround: In the Connectivity subpane of the Diagnostic pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box, set the Context-dependent inputs parameter to Enable All before invoking the Real-Time Workshop. A model will not be affected by this problem if it updates successfully with this diagnostic enabled.

Summary: The add_block() command may incorrectly number an input or output port added to the top level of a model
Description: When copying an input or output port into the top level of a model, the add_block()  command by default preserves the port index of the source port. This index may be incorrect relative to the destination model's other ports. This problem does not occur when you drag-and-drop or copy-and-paste a port into a model. ? ?
Workaround: Use the add_block command's port numbering option to set the copied port's index explicitly to the correct value. For example, the following command ensures that the copied port's index is 1:>> add_block(<srcPortPath>, <dstPortPath>, 'Port', '1')???

Summary: Error when calling uigetfile with an empty title argument
Description: In R2006b, function uigetfile  could not accept an empty (e.g. '' ) title  argument. The code x = uigetfile('*', '', [pwd filesep], 'MultiSelect', 'on'); resulted in the error ??? Error using ==> native_uigetfileUnrecognized input arguments. ?
Workaround: Change the empty title argument to include a space:x = uigetfile('*', '', [pwd filesep], 'MultiSelect', 'on');should be changed tox = uigetfile('*', ' ', [pwd filesep], 'MultiSelect', 'on');

Summary: Conditional breakpoints containing variable(index).fieldname(end) terminate MATLAB? unexpectedly
Description: Conditional breakpoints that contain an expression of the form variable(index).fieldname(end)  cause MATLAB to terminate unexpectedly when evaluated. The stack trace starts with the following two lines: [0] libmx.dll:_mxIsScalar(0, 1, 0x0ef5aba0, 1) + 7 bytes[1] m_interpreter.dll:_inEvaluateConditionForBreakpoint(0x01317910 "...", 0x00d0e45c, 0, 0) + 124 bytes
Workaround: Avoid using the end index. Instead, use length, as indbstop in file if variable(index).fieldname(length(variable(index).fieldname))

Summary: Infinite loop during procedure translation
Description: In very specific cases, PolySpace will enter in an infinite loop when translating procedures (during the C to Intermediate language phase), causing the analysis to hang. Here is an extract of a log where the problem occurs : **** C to intermediate language translation 14 (P_PT)***** C to intermediate language translation 14.1 (P_SPP)****** C to intermediate language translation 14.1.1 (P_CSSIP)****** C to intermediate language translation 14.1.1 (P_CSSIP) took 0real, 0u + 0s***** C to intermediate language translation 14.1 (P_SPP) took 0real, 0u + 0s***** C to intermediate language translation 14.2 (P_TP) - translating procedure assert (1 / 6) - translating procedure memset (2 / 6) - translating procedure func01 (3 / 6) ? ?
Workaround: Please note that this problem occurs in version 4.1, and is fixed in the R2007a+.If you are experiencing this behavior with version 4.1, you should upgrade to the latest version of the product.

Summary: Error: "Chromatic inconsistency" when a false red (in grey code) is detected in level 1
Description: When a false red error (a red error which is?located in an unreachable piece of code) is detected in level 1, the analysis stops in level 2 with the following error message:----------------------------------------------------------------------@FFT: Type 3 Chromatic InconsistencyAn internal consistency problem has been detected.In order to avoid generating incomplete verification information,the computation has been halted.Thanks for filling a Polyspace Technologies Fact Sheet.----------------------------------------------------------------------

Summary: PolySpace verification can crash while generating the remote file at the end of the compilation phase, if the results directory uses a relative path.
Description: If you specify a relative path for the results directory, the following message appears at the end of the compilation log (before the remote analysis starts): "Generating remote filePolySpace : Read access problem on /home/user/test-c/resultsdir/resultsdir/PolySpace_4_1_1_6_polyspace_04_17_2007-10h45.log" In this analysis, the results directory is resultdir, and is relative to /home/user/test-c. The name of the results directory (here: resultsdir) appears twice in the message, which causes the problem.
Workaround: To avoid this problem, specify an absolute path for the results directory.

Summary: Real-Time Workshop fails to generate code and returns an internal error message
Description: Real-Time Workshop does not generate code for models that have continuous states and discrete sample times. The following error message is displayed: Error using ==> tlc_c  Error using ==> rtwgen Internal Error: '<block path>' has a different RTWIndex 0 != 1 than its first instance '<block path>'. To determine if your model has mixed states and is using discrete sample times:     Set the Configuration Parameters > Solver > solver menu to Discrete (no continuous states)      Perform an update diagram.     Simulink reports an error if a discrete sample time is selected for a model with continuous states.
Workaround: All continuous states must be replaced with the fastest discrete sample time in the affected model. Use the following MATLAB commands to replace continuous states.cBlks = find_system(<model_name>, 'Type', 'block', 'SampleTime', '0'); for i=1:length(cBlks)  set_param(cBlks{i}, 'SampleTime', get_param(<model_name>, 'FixedStep')); end

Summary: Crash in libmwm_interpreter.so:x86Code::Call(void*)
Description: MATLAB?may crash if the JIT fails to allocate or set the "execute" bit on a piece of memory containing JITed code.
Workaround: If you experience this crash, or?want to prevent the possibility of it occurring, we suggest that you turn the JIT off using?the command??? feature jit off

Summary: Can not simulate a model in Accelerator mode if it has a configuration set reference
Description: A model cannot be simulated in Accelerator mode if it has a configuration set reference. Attempting to do so will cause Simulink to issue?the following error message:A configuration set reference does not allow writing to parameters in the source configuration set .
Workaround: To prevent this error from occurring, you must either simulate in Normal Mode, or avoid using a configuration set reference.

Summary: Constant block value might become non-tunable
Description: The value of a Constant block might become non-tunable if it is connected to an S-Function block, a Model Reference block, or the IC-port of an Integrator block, and any of the following is true:    The Constant block is not connected to any other blocks.     The S-Function block is not inlined     The signal is a global signal.
Workaround: Insert a Gain block between the Constant block and the subsequent block.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code generation may not match simulation when model contains conditionally executed subsystems with specified Outport initialization value
Description: The simulation output signal value for a conditionally executed subsystem might be different than the generated code output signal value when all of the following conditions are met: The conditionally executed subsystem contains a Constant block connected to an Outport block with a specified Initial output  value. The outport of the conditionally executed Subsystem block is connected to an S-Function block, a Model Reference block, or the IC-port of an Integrator block.
Workaround: Insert a Gain block between the Subsystem and subsequent blocks.

Summary: Incorrect code generation: Code generated from an if branch that starts with a loop in Stateflow charts and Embedded MATLAB functions produces wrong answers
Description: Stateflow and Embedded Matlab generate incorrect code for if  statements where the if  branch starts with a loop ˇŞ such as while , do-while , or for  ˇŞ and the else  branch is not empty. The generated code moves the loop statement outside the if  statement.For example, consider this code from an Embedded MATLAB function: if (c1) { ???   while (c2) { ??? ??? block1; ???   } } else { ???   block2; }   Embedded MATLAB incorrectly transforms the <tt>if</tt> statement in generated C code for simulation, as follows: while (c2) {?? block1;}if (!c1) {   block2;} The example also applies to equivalent graphical flow graphs in Stateflow.
Workaround: 1. Download the attached file sfinit.m.2. Start R2007a MATLAB.3. At the MATLAB prompt, type:??? which private/sfinit.m??? This command should display a path such as:??? C:\Programs\R2007a\matlab\toolbox\stateflow\stateflow\private\sfinit.m? % Private to stateflow4. Back up this file to a different location.5. Save the downloaded file to the path displayed above.6. Restart MATLAB.

Summary: Segmentation violation in NWALIGN and SWALIGN?with very long sequences on 64 bit machines
Description: A segmentation violation error sometimes occurs when using NWALIGN or SWALIGN with?sequences longer than 16,000 bp on?64 bit machines.

Summary: Selector and Assignment blocks error out if indexing setting ends with special character
Description: In R2006a, building or opening a model generates the error  Syntax error: 'ElementIndices'  when the model has the following characteristics:  Created in R2006a or earlier Contained Selector or Assignment blocks with the following parameter settings: Elements , Rows , or Columns  ended with one of '\n' , ' ,' or ';'
Workaround: As a workaround,Open the dialog box of the Selector or Assignment block.As appropriate, remove the terminating '\n', ',', or ';' from parameter Elements, Rows, or Columns.

Summary: Serial callback functions can display previous warnings
Description: In MATLAB, a serial object call to instrcb.m can repeatedly rethrow the last warning. This has been observed even for warnings unrelated to instrcb.m.  A typical case of this behavior might occur when using the serial port with callback functions, as in the following code:   warning('This is a test warning.');   disp('You should not see test warning again.');   s = serial('COM1');   set(s,'OutputEmptyFcn',@instrcallback)   fopen(s)   fprintf(s,'*IDN?','async')   stopasync(s)   fprintf(s,'*IDN?','async')   stopasync(s)   fprintf(s,'*IDN?','async')   stopasync(s)   fclose(s)   delete(s)   clear s  This code results in the following output:    Warning: This is a test warning.   You should not see test warning again.   OutputEmpty event occurred at 17:32:42 for the object: Serial-COM1.   Warning: This is a test warning.   > In instrcb at 46   OutputEmpty event occurred at 17:32:51 for the object: Serial-COM1.   Warning: This is a test warning.   > In instrcb at 46   OutputEmpty event occurred at 17:32:55 f  Notice that the test warning is erroneously repeated, even though there are no subsequent warnings after the test warning.
Workaround: To prevent these erroneous warning messages, copy the attached file into toolbox\matlab\iofun as instrcb.m using the following instructions according to your platform: On Windows: Save the attached file into C:\TEMP. Then, in MATLAB, enter the commands  >> cd(matlabroot);  >> copyfile('C:\TEMP\instrcb.m', 'toolbox\matlab\iofun\instrcb.m');On UNIX: Save the attached file into /tmp. Then, in MATLAB, enter the commands  >> cd(matlabroot);  >> copyfile('/tmp/instrcb.m', 'toolbox/matlab/iofun/instrcb.m');

Summary: Incorrect error for nonvirtual bus fed with some continuous signals and some signals updated at the fastest discrete rate
Description: Simulink will incorrectly issue an error message when the following are all true for either a Bus Creator or Bus Assignment block:  the block outputs a nonvirtual bus all of the elements of the bus object specify an inherited (-1) sample time at least one input signal has a continuous sample time all other input signals have either a continuous sample time or the fastest discrete rate the model is using a fixed step solver The message Simulink erroneously issues is:Sample time mismatch. When a Bus Creator or Bus Assignment block outputs a nonvirtual bus, all of the signals driving its input ports must have the same sample time. This restriction applies even if the elements of the object defining the bus specify an inherited (-1) sample time. The sample time of the signal ([0.005, 0]) driving input port 1 of 'path_to_block_in_your_model' does not match the sample time of the block ([0, 0]).   Note:  The error message will include sample times appropriate for your model. The sample times shown in the above example are for illustration only. ? Tip:  Turn on Sample Time Colors  to help see the occurrences of this problem    On the Format  menu click Port/Signal Displays , and then Sample Time Colors
Workaround: The workaround depends on the version of Simulink you are using:    For releases prior to R2006b, place a rate transition block on the input signals with the fastest discrete rate. Set its Output port sample time parameter to 0    For R2006b and subsequent releases, either use a variable step solver or create separate buses for the continuous and fastest discrete rate signals

Summary: Selecting wrongly-enabled menu item can crash MATLAB
Description: If a block has an OpenFcn , the context menu item Mask Parameters...  is enabled even if no mask exists. Selecting the menu item can cause MATLAB to terminate abnormally.
Workaround: Do not select the Mask Parameters... context menu item if no mask exists.

Summary: Specifying particular block parameters might cause Simulink to crash on 64-bit Solaris systems
Description: On 64-bit Solaris systems, Simulink will crash if you:     Use the set_param  function to specify the value of a block's boolean parameter while the block's parameter dialog box is open. Here, boolean parameters refer to block options that you specify as either on  or off , typically by selecting or deselecting a check box on the block's parameter dialog box.     Open the Bitwise Operator block dialog box and specify the value of its Operator  parameter as NOT .  When Simulink crashes, it produces a bus error that displays a stack trace similar to the following: Stack Trace:  [  0] ffffffff34e195d0 /matlab/bin/sol64/libmwsimulink.so+14783952 (__1cVBoolean_set_check_fcn6FpkvpkcpnLmxArray_tag_pi_pnNslErrMsgQdDueue__+000048)  [  1] ffffffff34cb4dc0 /matlab/bin/sol64/libmwsimulink.so+13323712 (__1cRSLObsoletePrmDescSdialogSetInMx_Impl6kMpvpknLmxArray_tag__pnNslErrMsgQdDueue__+000360)  [  2] ffffffff34cb91c4 /matlab/bin/sol64/libmwsimulink.so+13341124 (__1cJSLPrmDescMsetValueInMx6kMpvpknLmxArray_tag_bpb_pnNslErrMsgQdDueue__+000196)  [  3] ffffffff34e1ab44 /matlab/bin/sol64/libmwsimulink.so+14789444 (__1cUString_set_check_fcn6FpkvpkcpnLmxArray_tag_ppc_pnNslErrMsgQdDueue__+002924)  [  4] ffffffff34e1ae04 /matlab/bin/sol64/libmwsimulink.so+14790148 (__1cUString_set_check_fcn6FpkvpkcpnLmxArray_tag_ppc_pnNslErrMsgQdDueue__+003628)  [  5] ffffffff348d43b0 /matlab/bin/sol64/libmwsimulink.so+9257904 (__1cJException2T6M_v_+015240)  ... Note that MATLAB terminates when using the Bitwise Operator block dialog to specify NOT  as its Operator  parameter.
Workaround: When interacting with the affected block parameters on 64-bit Solaris systems, use the workarounds described below.To specify programmatically the value of boolean parameters associated with blocks:    Ensure that the block's parameter dialog box is closed.    Use the set_param function to specify either off or on as the value of the block's boolean parameters.To specify NOT as the value of the Bitwise Operator block's Operator parameter:    Ensure that the Bitwise Operator block's parameter dialog box is closed.    Use the set_param function to specify the value of the block's logicop parameter as NOT.

Summary: Pro/ENGINEER-to-SimMechanics translator does not support custom units
Description: Custom units are available to Pro/ENGINEER users through the Edit > Setup  unit menu. But if you select a custom unit in a Pro/ENGINEER assembly, then the SimMechanics model you create by translating the assembly has wrong units, and the assembly values are not converted properly according to the units selected.
Workaround: There is no workaround.

Summary: Incorrect code generation and simulation: Incorrect results can occur for Embedded MATLAB functions that combine concatenation and transposition
Description: Simulating or generating code for Embedded MATLAB Function blocks ˇŞ or for C-MEX functions created using emlmex ˇŞ can produce incorrect results if the source contains any of the following functions: Transposes the result of a horizontal concatenation, and vertically concatenates the result of the transposition with some other matrices  or Transposes the result of a vertical concatenation, and horizontally concatenates the result of transposition with some other matrices  For example, the following Embedded MATLAB function generates incorrect code: ? function result = transpose_hcat(in1, in2) result = [[in1, in1]'; in2]; end
Workaround: Manually apply the following transform:[A, B]' ==> [A'; B'][A; B]' ==> [A', B']For example, rewrite the example as follows: function result = transpose_hcat(in1, in2) result = [in1'; in1'; in2]; end

Summary: Deployed application with no DISPLAY hangs.
Description: A Java application using an object created by MATLAB Builder for Java may hang if the variable 'DISPLAY' is not set in the application's environment.? The hang is likely to occur in a call to System.out or System.err after a significant number of characters have been writen to either stream.? This bug is only present on Unix platforms.
