Summary: Generated code for a Divide block with Simplest rounding might lack exception protection
Description: Suppose your ERT-based Simulink model contains a Divide block that Operates on signals of integer or fixed-point data types, and Specifies Simplest  as the value of its Round integer calculations toward  parameter.  If you execute the build process, Real-Time Workshop generates code that lacks exception protection for division, even if you requested such protection by deselecting the Remove code that protects against division arithmetic exceptions  option on the Optimization  pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box. Consequently, the generated code is unable to guard against division that triggers an exception, such as Division by zero Division of a signed intmin  by -1, where intmin  is the smallest value of an integer data type  If the generated code encounters such an exception during run-time, its behavior is determined externally, e.g., by the application, hardware, or operating system. As a result, the application might compute incorrect results or a hardware exception might abort the process and terminate the application.
Workaround: You can work around this issue by forcing Real-Time Workshop to generate division exception protection code. For each of the affected Divide blocks in your model, specify the value of its Round integer calculations toward parameter as either Floor or Zero, according to the actual method that your hardware implements. Note that this value should already be specified for the Signed integer division rounds to parameter on the Hardware Implementation pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box.

Summary: Code generation for Sign block?might fail
Description: The following Target Language Compiler (TLC) error can result when you generate code for a model that includes a Sign block:??%exit directive:?? Real-Time Workshop Fatal in block: "loop_roll_issue/Sign", block type "Signum": No parameters to roll.
Workaround: Replace the file matlabroot\rtw\c\tlc\blocks\signum.tlc as follows:Determine your MATLAB root directory by entering matlabroot at the MATLAB command line.Quit MATLAB.Rename matlabroot\rtw\c\tlc\blocks\signum.tlc to matlabroot\rtw\c\tlc\blocks\signum.tlc.old.Download the attached file and place it in the directory matlabroot\rtw\c\tlc\blocks.Restart MATLAB.

Summary: PDEDEMO5 does not?run in?PDE Toolbox (R2006b)
Description: When I run PDEDEMO5 in PDE Toolbox 1.0.9 (R2006b), the movie is not shown in the figure window.

Summary: National Instruments hardware Analog Output may stop generating data silently when the CPU is extremely loaded.
Description: National Instruments hardware using the NI-DAQmx interface analog output object may stop generating data silently, with no error message emitted, when the CPU is extremely loaded.For example, with this code: ao = analogoutput('nidaq', 'Dev1'); addchannel(ao,0)putdata(ao,ones(1000*100,1))start(ao)while 1 , disp([num2str(ao.SamplesAvailable) ' Output: ' num2str(ao.SamplesOutput)]), pause (2), end   If a high-usage CPU event (such as starting another copy of MATLAB) occurs while this code is executing, you may see the following: In queue: 865408 Output: 134592In queue: 863744 Output: 136256In queue: 0 Output: 136256In queue: 0 Output: 136256   A zero in the queue and the number of SamplesOutput being stagnant indicates that the AO object stopped, but as shown here, there was no error message.  Data Acquisition Toolbox is based on "Ring 3" Windows, which is a consumer operating system and is not a "real time" operating system. When a CPU-intensive process is launched, it is possible that Data Acquisition Toolbox and/or the vendor device driver will not get frequent enough CPU cycles, causing the data flow to pause for just long enough for the device to run out of data.

Summary: Incorrect code generation: Stateflow inserts incorrect boolean_T cast for condition expressions that are not boolean
Description: Stateflow incorrectly casts non-boolean condition expressions on transitions to boolean_T  type in: Generated C code Simulation code when debugging is disabled  For example, if a condition expression [expr]  returns a value of integer or floating point type, Stateflow generates the following relational expression with an incorrect boolean_T  cast:  ((boolean_T) expr) != 0  Furthermore, if the value of expr  falls outside the range that boolean_T  represents, Stateflow truncates the value to fit inside the range and may yield an incorrect logical result.  For example, if the range of boolean_T  is [0,255], Stateflow generates an incorrect logical result when the runtime value of expr  causes the condition (expr & 255) == 0  to be true. Stateflow generates the same incorrect result when expr  as defined above is passed as an argument to a function that expects a boolean argument.  To identify whether the issue occurs, compare the simulation result to the code generation result when debugging is enabled.  For more information, refer to technical solution .
Workaround: To workaround this issue, change the syntax of the condition to explicitly indicate logical state by using a relational operator, as in this example:[expr ~= 0]

Summary: Collecting model coverage data after generating code for a subsystem might cause Simulink to crash
Description: If your model contains any of the following blocks: Lookup Table MinMax Multiport Switch  and you perform the following sequence of steps: You enable (and optionally use) the Model Coverage tool in Simulink Verification and Validation to generate coverage data for your model. You use Real-Time Workshop to build an executable from a subsystem in your model, e.g., by right-clicking a Subsystem block and selecting Real-Time Workshop > Build Subsystem . You use the Model Coverage tool to collect coverage data for your model.  Simulink will crash, producing a segmentation violation that displays a stack trace similar to the following: Stack Trace:  [0] cv.mexw32:0x172a2d4d(0x17160344, 1, 0x17299fc4, 4)  [1] libmwsimulink.dll:struct slErrMsgQueue * __cdecl BlockInitializeCoverage(class slBlock *,class slSimBlock_tag *)(0x171601e8, 0x17299fc4, 0x13c83e00, 0) + 116 bytes  [2] libmwsimulink.dll:_BdSystemCoverageInitialize(0x1ed2abb0, 0x1f539320, 0, 0x1f1f7aa0) + 33 bytes  [3] libmwsimulink.dll:struct slErrMsgQueue * __cdecl ModelStart(class slModel *)(0x1f1f7aa0, 0x00ceaa10, 0, 0x1f1f7aa0) + 387 bytes  [4] libmwsimulink.dll:struct slErrMsgQueue * __cdecl sleInitModelConditions(class slModel *,struct mxArray_tag const *,bool,bool)(0x011f7aa0, 0, 1, 1) + 437 bytes  [5] libmwsimulink.dll:_sleInitModelForMenuSim(0x13c83e00, 0, 0x13c83e00, 0x00ceaa80) + 133 bytes  [6] libmwsimulink.dll:struct slErrMsgQueue * __cdecl sleModelExecutionInterface(struct slBlockDiagram_tag *,enum slEngineCmdOption,enum ECStatus *)(0x13c83e00, 0, 0x00ceaa7c, 0x13c83e00) + 99 bytes  [7] libmwsimulink.dll:enum ECStatus __cdecl sluiEngBdMenuCmdInterface(struct slBlockDiagram_tag *,enum slEngineCmdOption)(0x13c83e00, 0, 0x15986ba0, 0x13c83e00) + 39 bytes  [8] libmwsimulink.dll:struct slErrMsgQueue * __cdecl StartMenuSimIfNotStarted(struct slBlockDiagram_tag *)(0, 0x13c83e00, 1, 18) + 94 bytes  [9] libmwsimulink.dll:void __cdecl Menu_Simulation_Start(struct WinRec_tag *,void *)(0x15986ba0, 0, 0x17631324, 3015974) + 119 bytes  ...
Workaround: You can use either of the following workarounds to prevent Simulink from crashing when alternating between the collection of model coverage data and the generation of code for subsystems:Before building an executable from any of your model's subsystems, ensure that you have disabled the Enable Coverage Reporting option on the Coverage Settings dialog.After building an executable from any of your model's subsystems, close and then reload the model before collecting its coverage data.

Summary: Passing in non-string first argument to sscanf causes extra errors.
Description: The sscanf  function returns an unexpected, additional error if called with an argument list in which the first argument is not a string. This faulty calling syntax is expected to return an error, however the additional out-of-memory error is incorrect. For example, the following code returns an error message saying that the first argument must be a string, but it also returns an out-of-memory error:   temp = -1  temp = sscanf(temp,'%*2c %*2c %5d %4d %4d %2d %2d %*2c %f %f %d %d %d %d %d %d');
Workaround: When calling sscanf in MATLAB version 7.3, ensure that the first input argument is a string.

Summary: Memory leak issue when?a large number of blocks are connected outside of a conditionally executed subsystem that allows?execution context propagation across the subsystem boundary
Description: Simulink memory leaks might occur when model has the following? characteristics:     The model contains a conditionally executed subsystem that allows?execution context propagation across the subsystem boundary.     There are more than 128 blocks connected outside of the conditionally executed subsystem.

Summary: Memory leak issue using Bus Selector connected to root Inport or Outport in referenced model
Description: Memory leaks might occur in Simulink when the model has the following? characteristics:1. The model contains a Model block2. The referenced model contains a Bus signal3. The Bus signal? is connected in one or both of the following ways:??? a) A Bus Selector selects a bus signal element from the root Inport??? b) A Bus Selector selects a bus signal element and outputs it to the root Outport??

Summary: Hit Crossing block in an Iterator Subsystem might generate incorrect simulation results
Description: Hit Crossing blocks that reside in either a For or a While Iterator Subsystem might generate incorrect simulation results.  In this case, if the block detects a hit crossing, it outputs true  (i.e., a value of 1 ) for every other iteration of the subsystem.  This pattern continues until the next model time step, when the Hit Crossing block resets.

Summary: Multiport Selector block with ordered vectors does not generate correct HDL code
Description: The Multiport Selector block does not generate correct HDL code when used with ordered vectors (1xN, Nx1).
Workaround: To work around this problem, use unordered one-dimensional vectors with the Multiport Selector block.

Summary: FORTRAN MEX files must include fintrf.h
Description: FORTRAN MEX files on the Intel Macintosh platform fail to load due to errors like this one:dyld: lazy symbol binding failed: Symbol not found: _mxgetm_
Workaround: FORTRAN MEX files should include fintrf.h at compile time.  Modify your MEX file by adding the following line at the top of the MEX file's source code.#include "fintrf.h"Using this file requires the MEX file to run through the C pre-processor. This happens automatically when the source file name ends in the .F extension.Files with a lower-case .f extension are not run through the pre-processor. In this case, use the cpp option to mex. For example,mex -cpp filename.f

Summary: Image from Workspace block might return incorrect output
Description: The Image from Workspace block produces incorrect outputs for an M-by-N-by-P input image, when P is greater than 9.
Workaround: You can correct this problem by replacing the vipblkimsrc.m file in the $matlabroot/toolbox/vipblks/vipmasks directory with the contents of the attachment_359769_20070129.zip file attached to this bug report.

Summary: MATLAB?hangs when a Signal & Scope Manager viewer is connected to signals inside of a linked library which is updated.
Description: When updating a linked library, MATLAB may hang during model compilation if a Signal & Scope Manager viewer is connected to a signal inside the linked library. ?
Workaround: To prevent MATLAB from hanging, do the following before you update a library link:?        Disconnect all Signal & Scope Manager viewer connections            Update the library link            Restore the Signal & Scope Manager connections    ?

Summary: Trimming a polygon to a quadrangle can result in nearly-coincident vertices
Description: When maptrimp  or geoshow  trim vertices of a polygon to a cylindrical or conic map projection, the trimmed polygon can contain nearly-coincident vertices. The issue arises from a bug in the code for trimming a polygon to a latitude-longitude quadrangle that handles round-off inadequately. For certain inputs, the resulting nearly-coincident vertices can cause problems in subsequent processing. (In geoshow , polygon trimming can take place when displaying a polygon geostruct or when using a DisplayType  of 'polygon' .)
Workaround: To work around this issue, replace the joinPolygonsOnVerticalLine.m function file in your current installation with the newer version in the attachment, as follows:Exit MATLAB.Make a backup copy of the following file. matlabroot\toolbox\map\mapproj\private\joinPolygonsOnVerticalLine.mmatlabroot represents the name of your MATLAB installation directory, as returned by the matlabroot function.For example, rename the existing file, adding "old" to the file extension, joinPolygonsOnVerticalLine.m.OLD.Copy the version of joinPolygonsOnVerticalLine.m from the attachment into the directory specified in the previous step.Restart MATLAB.Run the rehash toolbox command at the MATLAB prompt.Also see bug report  336641 for information about a related polygon trimming issue.

Summary: Insert Text block's Font face parameter might not initialize properly
Description: If the only choice for the Font face parameter is FONTLIST, this parameter did not initialize properly. Consequently, the Insert Text block will produce an error.
Workaround: You can correct this problem by replacing the vippopfontlist.m file in the $matlabroot/toolbox/vipblks/vipmasks directory with the contents of the ZIP file attached to this bug report.

Summary: Model containing bad link does not render properly in Simulink Editor
Description: A model containing a bad library link may open with some elements rendered invisibly (i.e. they are invisible but respond to user input, selection, and so on.)

Summary: Noninlined S-functions: floating-point timer not updated for noninlined S-function that runs as asychronous task
Description: When a model includes a noninlined S-function that runs as an asynchronous task, Real-Time Workshop creates a floating-point timer, which provides the absolute time for blocks that run in the asynchronous task. However, the update function that Real-Time Workshop generates for the model fails to update the absolute floating-point timer.
Workaround: To work around this bug in release R14sp2, R14sp3, R2006a, or R2006b, perform the following steps:Note: In the following instructions, matlabroot represents your MATLAB installation root, as returned by the MATLAB command matlabroot.        In your MATLAB installation tree, rename the following files, for example, by adding .old to the end of each file name:    - matlabroot/rtw/c/tlc/lib/asynclib.tlc    - matlabroot/rtw/c/tlc/mw/subsystemlib.tlc            Download the attachments with _release suffixes that correspond to your release version (for example, for R2006b, download asynclib_R2006b.tlc and subsystemlib_R2006b.tlc).            Rename the downloaded files to asynclib.tlc and subsystemlib.tlc and place the files at the locations indicated below:    - matlabroot/rtw/c/tlc/lib/asynclib.tlc    - matlabroot/rtw/c/tlc/mw/subsystemlib.tlc            Restart MATLAB.    Note: Consider additionally downloading the fix for bug report 380630.

Summary: XLA-files not automatically packaged with
Description: Excel Add-In files (.xla)  residing in the project's distrib  directory are not automatically added to the package created by deploytool .
Workaround: To workaround this issue, manually add the .XLA files to the package as follows:In deploytool open the project settings. Click Project >  SettingsSelect the proper packaging settings.Add the .xla files to the list of files to be included.

Summary: Why do I experience dropped frames when using format 7 with a DCAM camera?
Description: I am using a DCAM-compliant camera in format 7 mode with Image Acquisition Toolbox's DCAM driver. When I measure the frame rate of the acquired images, it is not what I expect it to be. For example, the camera I am using is capable of acquiring 30 frames per second at a resolution of 640x480, but the data returned is only being acquired at 15 frames per second. This does not happen if I do not use the format 7 mode of the camera.

Summary: Stateflow syntax highlighting lost when printing to clipboard
Description: Stateflow does not support syntax highlighting when you print charts to the clipboard.
Workaround: You can print to an image file ˇŞ for example, in PNG image format ˇŞ to get the syntax highlighting.

Summary: S-function: code generation fails due to fatal error
Description: Code generation fails due to a fatal error if an S-function block contains all of the following conditions:Uses elapsed time Is in a triggered subsystem hierarchy Has a non-virtual parent subsystem that is unconditionally executed  The following error is generated: Error: File: C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2006b\rtw\c\tlc\lib\utillib.tlc Line: 1063 Column: 12Undefined identifier 'x' where x   can by any identifier name.
Workaround: Replace the file rtw/c/tlc/lib/utillib.tlc in your MATLAB installation area with the attached utillib.tlc file.Determine the MATLAB root directory on your machine by entering the MATLAB command matlabroot.Quit MATLAB.Rename the TLC file matlabroot/toolbox/rtw/c/tlc/lib/utillib.tlc to utillib.tlc.old, where matlabroot is the value returned in step 1.Download the attached file to the directory matlabroot/toolbox/rtw/c/tlc/lib.Restart MATLAB.Enter the MATLAB command rehash toolboxcache.

Summary: datcomimport function has problems reading certain DATCOM files
Description: When the datcomimport  function reads a DATCOM file that has a CASEID  containing the string configuration , it will generate the following warning: Warning: DATCOM file reader state inconsistent and the function output will not contain all the DATCOM data. In addition, using the undocumented namelist  value ALPHA  instead of ALSCHD  to define the angles of attack will prevent the datcomimport  function output from containing all the DATCOM data.
Workaround: Download the attached file. To use the update:Installation instructionsLog in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps.Exit MATLAB.Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB>/toolbox/aero/aero/private folder.Make a backup copy of each of the files listed in the .zip file: /toolbox/aero/aero/private/usafdatcom.pTo see the contents of the .zip file: Windows Systems*: Open the .zip file and look at the contents.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh):unzip -l <MATLAB>/toolbox/aero/aero/private<.zip file>Extract the files from the .zip file. Windows Systems*: Open the .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB>/toolbox/aero/aero/private folder.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh): cd <MATLAB>/toolbox/aero/aero/privateunzip <.zip file>Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB>/toolbox/aero/aero/private folder.*Notes for Windows Systems:If you see ˇ°Error: Access is deniedˇ± when attempting to extract the .zip file, you may need to start ˇ°WinZipˇ± in administrator mode. Do this by navigating to WinZip.exe or the WinZip Start menu entry, right-clicking on it, and selecting ˇ°Run as administratorˇ±.From within WinZip, find and open the downloaded .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB>/toolbox/aero/aero/private folder.

Summary: Incorrect code generation: compilation fails for models with Japanese block names
Description: When a Japanese block name ends with the character 0x5C, the Real-Time Workshop code generator incorrectly inserts a line break in the generated code. The line break causes compilation to fail.

Summary: Build fails if referenced model and top model use multiple precompiled S-function libraries
Description: If you build a model in which both a referenced model and the top model have multiple different precompiled S-function libraries specified in rtwmakecfg.m  files, the build errors out with the following message:MATLAB error message:Error using ==> horzcatCAT arguments dimensions are not consistent.

Summary: Embedded MATLAB functions do not support shared utilities feature in R2006b.
Description: Subfunctions and library functions generated from Embedded MATLAB functions are never shared between blocks or models that are generated with Real-Time Workshop, even when you enable generation of shared utilities. Generated code executes correctly and yields correct results, but may produce a larger executable than desired. This bug exists only in the R2006b release.
Workaround: There is no workaround.

Summary: Missing mam22.fis file
Description: The mam22.fis file, mentioned in Fuzzy Logic Toolbox documentation, is missing.
Workaround: Use mam21.fis file instead.

Summary: MATLAB crashes when executing a very long anonymous function
Description: MATLAB may crash when an anonymous function containing over 2800 characters is called.
Workaround: Turn the MATLAB JIT/accelerator off before running the function using the command   feature accel off

Summary: Incorrect code generation: Real-Time Workshop generates invalid code due to block output optimization settings
Description: Real-Time Workshop generates code that gives the wrong answer when, in the Optimization  pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box:    Enable local block outputs  is disabled.     Reuse block outputs  is enabled.
Workaround: The MathWorks strongly recommends that you avoid this bug by upgrading to a newer release. Otherwise do one of the following in the Optimization pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box:    Enable Enable local block outputs and Reuse block outputs    Disable Enable local block outputs and Reuse block outputs

Summary: Setting the "Disable JVM at runtime" option in DEPLOYTOOL has no effect
Description: Even though the "Disable JVM at runtime" checkbox is selected in the DEPLOYTOOL  GUI, JVM is not disabled at runtime in the generated application.
Workaround: There is no direct workaround for this problem.To workaround this issue, compile the application with MCC using the -R -nojvm option.  For example:mcc -m foo -R -nojvm

Summary: dicominfo returns "Index exceeds matrix dimensions" error for sequences with more than 300 items
Description: The dicominfo  function makes an assumption about the maximum number of items that a sequence of attributes will likely contain.  Using dicominfo  on files with more than 300 items in a sequence will produce this error: "Index exceeds matrix dimensions"
Workaround: To workaround this problem, edit the dicominfo function, changing a particular section of the code with the following updated version of the code. Follow this procedure.Open the file toolbox/images/medformats/dicominfo.m for editing.Find this section of code:function itemNames = getItemNames(numberOfItems)% Create a cell array of item names, which can be quickly used.persistent namesCellif (isempty(namesCell))      maxItems = 300;    namesCell = cell(1, maxItems);    for idx = 1:maxItems        namesCell{idx} = sprintf('Item_%d', idx);    end    end% Return the first n item names.itemNames = namesCell(1:numberOfItems);Replace this code with the following code:function itemNames = getItemNames(numberOfItems)% Create a cell array of item names, which can be quickly used.persistent namesCellif (isempty(namesCell))    namesCell = generateItemNames(50);end% If the number of cached names is too small, expand it and recache.if (numberOfItems > numel(namesCell))    namesCell = generateItemNames(numberOfItems);end% Return the first n item names.itemNames = namesCell(1:numberOfItems);function namesCell = generateItemNames(numberOfItems)namesCell = cell(1, numberOfItems);for idx = 1:numberOfItems    namesCell{idx} = sprintf('Item_%d', idx);end

Summary: imcontrast errors when updating image CData or CDataMapping more than once
Description: imcontrast  returns an error if you update the CData  or CDataMapping  of the target image more than once.
Workaround: To workaround this issue, replace the version of the getHistogramData function in your current installation with the updated version contained in the attached file.Exit MATLAB.Make a backup copy of the following file. matlabroot\toolbox\images\imuitools\private\getHistogramData.mmatlabroot represents the name of your MATLAB installation directory, as returned by the matlabroot function. For example, rename the existing file, adding "old" to the file extension, getHistogramData.m.OLD.Download the attached file, extract getHistogramData.m, and store it in the directory specified in the previous step.Restart MATLAB.Run the rehash toolbox command at the MATLAB prompt.

Summary: LCC generates wrong answer for repeated expressions containing global variables
Description: The LCC compiler generates incorrect code for expressions containing repeated  subexpressions of the form: (G OP Z)  where: G   is any signed or unsigned, global or static variable that is either  scalar, the first element of an array, or  the first field of a structure Z   is an integer constant OP   is any of the following operators: + , - , & , | , or >>  For commutative operators, (Z OP G)  also triggers the bug. For example, the expression  (g + 5) + ((g + 5) - 1)  triggers this bug, provided g  is a global variable satisfying the above conditions.
Workaround: There is no workaround.

Summary: Error message during code generation that refers to non-existent field 'errmsg'
Description: When you try to generate code with Target for TI C6000, Real-Time Workshop reports the following error: Error building Real-Time Workshop target for block diagram 'first'.MATLAB error message: Error using ==> callMakeHook.p>callMakeHook The call to ti_c6000_ert_make_rtw_hook, during the before_tlc hook generated the following error:     Reference to non-existent field 'errmsg'. If you do not have Code Composer Studio installed, or you have not configured a board or simulator in Setup Code Composer, you encounter this error message.
Workaround: Make sure that you installed Code Composer Studio (CCS) and used Setup Code Composer Studio to configure a board or simulator in CCS before you try to generate code with Target for TI C6000.

Summary: H5P.get_chunk and H5P.get_cache have incorrect help and syntax
Description: The syntax for the low-level HDF5 functions H5P.get_chunk  and H5P.get_cache  do not match their C API counterparts.  H5P.get_chunk  should return both the chunk's rank and dimensions.  The help for the H5P.get_cache  function also says that it accepts a second "name" parameter, which is incorrect.

Summary: Unpacking frame-based time series signal logging objects causes display of time series properties in the command window
Description: Unpacking frame-based time series objects from a Simulink signal logging object causes the corresponding partially built time series object(s) display method(s) to generate text in the Command Window. For example, a single frame-based time series may produce the following display: Events: [] Name: 'Signal_after_gain' Data: [] DataInfo: [1x1 tsdata.datametadata] Time: [] TimeInfo: [1x1 Simulink.TimeInfo] Quality: [] QualityInfo: [1x1 tsdata.qualmetadata] IsTimeFirst: 1 TreatNaNasMissing: 1 Length: 0   Note that the unpack  method subsequently populates the time series object with data, so that the properties after the method has returned will not be the same as those displayed. Subsequent calls to the unpack  method will not cause the same display.
Workaround: Download the file initialize.m from the attached zip archive to the following directory:(MatlabRoot)/toolbox/matlab/timeseries/@SimTimeseries If there is an initialize.p file in this directory, remove it.Alternatively, edit the file:(MatlabRoot)/toolbox/matlab/timeseries/@SimTimeseries/initialize.m and add a semicolon at the end of line 69.

Summary: ERT S-function wrapper generation can fail when model input/output uses nonvirtual bus
Description: Code generation for a Simulink model can fail if all of the following conditions are true: The signal connected to the root outport of the model has a storage class that is not Auto  The signal connected to the root outport of the model is a nonvirtual bus The model's System target file  is ERT (ert.tlc ) or based on ERT ERT S-function wrapper generation is selected using the Create Simulink (S-Function) block  option on the Interface  pane of the configuration Parameters dialog box The MAT-file logging  option is not selected on the Interface  pane

Summary: Structure of process model is not displayed correctly in some cases
Description: In case of a process model (idproc), which contains three poles and includes an underdamped pair, the contribution of the third pole is displayed as (1 * Tp3 * s) rather than (1 + Tp3 * s). This incorrect display appears in both MATLAB Command Window and the System Identification Tool GUI (in the Data/model Info dialog box).

Summary: FETCHMULTI fails to return second resultset or crashes when executing a stored procedure in MSSQL Server 2000
Description: For some databases, notably Microsoft SQL Server, fetchmulti returns data for a single resultset where data for multiple resultset's was expected.
Workaround: To work around this issue, download the attached version of the MATLAB file. Here are the steps:1. Exit MATLAB.2. Rename the MATLAB file:$MATLABROOT\toolbox\database\database\@cursor\fetchmulti.m (where $MATLABROOT is the MATLAB root directory on your machine as shown by typing 'matlabroot'at the MATLAB Command Prompt)Tofilename.m.old3. Download the attached file(s). Place the new file(s) in the directory mentioned in step 2.4. Restart MATLAB.5. After restarting MATLAB, issue the following command at the MATLAB Command Prompt:rehash toolboxcache

Summary: Bioinformatics Toolbox function seqpdist.m does not handle ungapped sequences of equal length without forcing a pair-wise alignment.
Description: Bioinformatics Toolbox function seqpdist.m  does not compute the pair-wise distances of ungapped sequences of equal length without forcing a pair-wise alignment. This occurs only when input sequences have same length, input sequences have no gap symbols, and the property PairwiseAlignment  is set to false.
Workaround: You can update the function seqpdist.m by using the following procedure.1. In the MATLAB Command Window type which seqpdist.mThis displays the location of the file seqpdist.m.2. Rename seqpdist.m to seqpdist.m.old3. Download the updated files attached to this bug report to the directory identified with the which command.4. In the MATLAB Command Window type clear functions

Summary: Sequence logo does not update X-axis labels
Description: The SEQLOGO function does not update the X-axis labels correctly when using the 'StartAt' and 'EndAt' options.
Workaround: There is no known workaround.

Summary: Block dialogs with disabled or broken library links incorrectly unmasked
Description: This problem arises when you attempt to customize a block from the SimPowerSystems library. In a typical case, you copy the block into a new library, disable or break the block's link to the library, make changes underneath the block mask, and save this customized block in a new library.  This customized block should not be unmasked by the above actions, but the bug causes the mask to disappear.
Workaround: Locate this file and replace it with the attached file:  $matlabroot/toolbox/physmod/pm_util/pm_util/@PMDialogs/@PMDefaultMaskDlg/getDialogSchema.p

Summary: Block dialogs with disabled or broken library links incorrectly unmasked
Description: This problem arises when you attempt to customize a block from the SimDriveline library. In a typical case, you copy the block into a new library, disable or break the block's link to the SimDriveline library, make changes underneath the block mask, and save this customized block in the new library.This customized block should not be unmasked by the above actions, but the bug causes the mask to disappear.
Workaround: Locate this file and replace it with the attached file:$matlabroot/toolbox/physmod/pm_util/pm_util/@PMDialogs/@PMDefaultMaskDlg/getDialogSchema.p

Summary: Code generation fails when C6000 UDP and C6000 TCP/IP blocks are used in the same model
Description: Use of C6000 UDP Receive / Transmit and C6000 TCP/IP Receive and Transmit blocks together in the same model may result in code generation to fail. Code generation failure exhibits itself as undefined symbols or variables in the MW_netcfg.c  file during project compilation stage.
Workaround: Download the attached ZIP archive patch_352988.zip and TIC6000 patch installer patch_installer.m. Start MATLAB and enter the following command at the prompt to install the patch:  patch_installer('patch_352988.zip', 'install')

Summary: Documentation: Building model for C99(ISO) math environment can cause link errors if compiler does not support ISO C math extensions
Description: If you select the C99(ISO) target floating-point math environment for your Simulink model (using the Interface  pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box), you should build the model with a compiler that supports the ISO C math extensions. If your compiler does not support the ISO C math extensions, building the model can cause link errors. The Real-Time Workshop documentation of the Target Floating-Point Math Environment  parameter needs to add the following note. Note : If you select C99(ISO) and your compiler does not support the ISO C math extensions, compile-time errors likely will occur.

Summary: MVTCDF and COPULACDF('t', ...) return Inf under some conditions
Description: The MVTCDF function may return Inf and a warning when computing the cumulative probability for a bi-variate or tri-variate Student's t distribution. This can happen when the coordinates of the point at which the CDF is being evaluated are nearly equal, or are nearly equal in magnitude but of opposite sign. The COPULACDF function uses the MVTCDF function to compute the CDF for the Student's t copula, so it is also affected by this bug. The problem can happen when the coordinates of the point at which the CDF is being evaluated are nearly equal, or are nearly complementary, e.g. at the point (a, 1-a).
Workaround: Replace the MATLAB-file mvtcdf.m in the MATLABROOT/toolbox/stats directory with the attached new version.

Summary: Callbacks are slower in compiled applications
Description: In compiled applications, callback functions may execute more slowly than they do in MATLAB after the initial MATLAB-code has completed. This is due to a bug in waitForFiguresToDie that causes callbacks to execute only every 200 milliseconds.
Workaround: To work around this issue, add code at the end of your main function which prevents the MATLAB-code from completing until the application is exited. Below are examples of how to implement this workaround.1. If you have written an MATLAB-function which displays a figure, and that figure will be visible until the application is exited, call WAITFOR at the end of your MATLAB-file. For example:function myfunf=figure();x=uicontrol('Style','pushbutton','callback',@foo);waitfor(f);2. If you have created an application with GUIDE, it is possible to wrap the MATLAB-file created by GUIDE in a 2nd MATLAB-function which launches the GUIDE application and then waits for it to close:function runGUIf=myGUI;waitfor(f);3. If your application is more complicated, having several figures which open and close or does not have a figure visible, for example, you can create a global flag which you set when the application should be exited.The following is a wrapper function that will keep the MATLAB thread active using the PAUSE function. Set the global variable ˇ°quitflagˇ± to ˇ°trueˇ± at all the exit points of your application. Quitting the application will have in this case, at worst, a one second delay.function runAppglobal quitflag;quitflag = false;myApp;while ~quitflag  pause(1);end

Summary: Typedef for alias data types generated even if HeaderFile is specified.
Description: In R2006b we added the ability to separately specify the Value and DataType for Simulink parameter objects.  This enabled using alias data types for tunable parameters configured via Simulink parameter objects. However, in the generated code we generated a typedef for these alias data types, even if the alias data type object was configured to be imported from a HeaderFile.

Summary: Color Space Conversion block might produce incorrect results for L*a*b* to sR'G'B' conversions
Description: If you set the Conversion parameter to L*a*b* to sR'G'B', the Color Space Conversion block might produce incorrect results.
Workaround: If you are using Video and Image Processing Blockset Version 2.2, you can correct this problem by replacing the svipcolorconv.* file in the $matlabroot/toolbox/vipblks/vipmex directory with the platform-specific file contained in attachment_352605_20061214.zip file attached to this bug report.

Summary: Generating model coverage data for vectorized Multiport Switch blocks might cause assertion failures
Description: Suppose you use the model coverage tool with a model that contains a Multiport Switch block whose Control input signal is a vector, and Data input signals are scalar.  In this case, Simulink Verification and Validation produces the following assertion failure: failed assertion in matlab/toolbox/slvnv/simcoverage/src/cv_slsfobj.cpp at line 46:indexbaseObjs.lengthStack Trace:  [0] cv.mexw32:0x1727f639(0x172a24bc "indexbaseObjs.length", 0x172a2484 "matlab/toolbox/slvnv/simcover..", 46, 0)  [1] cv.mexw32:0x17276bab(0x172ea420, 1, 146, 1)  [2] cv.mexw32:0x17276be8(146, 1, 1, 0x00ceb0d8)  [3] cv.mexw32:0x17261257(1, 1, 1, 0x7a3f1f40)  [4] libmwsimulink.dll:public: static struct slErrMsgQueue * __cdecl MPSwitchCoverage::Output(class slBlock *,class slSimBlock_tag *)(2, 0x159b2f4c, 1, 0x00ceb148) + 536 bytes  [5] libmwsimulink.dll:struct slErrMsgQueue * __cdecl MPSwitchOutputFcn(class slBlock *,class slSimBlock_tag *)(0x1512c180, 0x159b2f4c, 0x17340ca0, 0) + 29 bytes  ...failed assertion in matlab/toolbox/slvnv/simcoverage/src/cv_message.cpp at line 223:message->intParams.length>prmCountStack Trace:  [0] cv.mexw32:0x1727f639(0x172a0ec4 "message->intParams.length>prmCou..", 0x172a0df4 "matlab/toolbox/slvnv/simcover..", 223, 0)  [1] cv.mexw32:0x1726f22d(159, 0x00ce6220 "truncated input(", 1024, 2)  [2] cv.mexw32:0x17268016(0x00ce6220 "truncated input(", 1024, 154, 0xffffffff)  [3] cv.mexw32:0x1727be95(60, 1, 0x00ce6e24, 4)  [4] cv.mexw32:0x1728ae59(1, 0x00ce6e24, 5, 0x00ce6eb4)  [5] cv.mexw32:0x17265dfa(5, 0x00ce6eb4, 0x00ce6e24, 5)  [6] cv.mexw32:0x17265e5f(1, 0x00ce6e24, 5, 0x00ce6eb4)  [7] libmex.dll:_mexRunMexFile(1, 0x00ce6e24, 5, 0x00ce6eb4) + 139 bytes  [8] libmex.dll:private: void __thiscall Mfh_mex::runMexFileWithSignalProtection(int,struct mxArray_tag * *,int,struct mxArray_tag * *)(1, 0x00ce6e24, 5, 0x00ce6eb4) + 86 bytes  ...
Workaround: If your model contains a Multiport Switch block whose control input signal is a vector, you can avoid assertion failures only if you implement one of the following:Ensure that all of the Multiport Switch block's data input signals are vectors.Disable the model coverage tool for your model.

Summary: MPlay GUI might fail to export data to Image Tool
Description: If the AVI file name is not a valid MATLAB variable name, the MPlay GUI produces an error if you try to export the data to Image Tool.
Workaround: You can workaround this problem by changing the file name to a valid MATLAB variable name.

Summary: LOADLIBRARY fails to load functions with parameters that have calculated sizes
Description: If a C header file contains the following code:#define ARSIZE (20+2)int foo(int myvar[ARSIZE]);  then the function foo  will not be loaded because of the parentheses in ARSIZE .Note there are other issues that make it difficult to use a sized parameter in MATLAB versions prior to R2007a.
Workaround: Modify the header file to specify a pointer instead of an array. For example,int foo(int *myvar);

Summary: RSim target does not support -v7.3 (hd5-based) mat-files
Description: Rsim Target  does not support loading of parameter and input signal data that is specified as hd5-based mat files (created using the -v7.3  option to the MATLAB save command).The RSim executable crashes when hd5 based mat-files are specified via the -i  or -p  flags.
Workaround: Do not use the -v7.3 option to save mat-files if they will be used with Rsim Target.Note: The default opion is -v7, which does not exhibit this problem.

Summary: Test Results Viewer will not display test results with integer type test vectors.
Description: Test results that contain numeric test vectors with integer types (e.g. uint8, int8, int32, etc.) will not display in the Test Results Viewer. Attempting to open the Test Results Viewer in this case will generate a modal dialog with the following error message:Unable to load dataset. Error returned was:Error using ==> epsClass must be ˇ®singleˇŻ or ˇ®doubleˇŻ
Workaround: The only workaround is to convert the test vectors to single or double type. To do this, save the test results MAT file from the SystemTest desktop and load it into MATLAB. The test results structure will be represented as the variable 'stresults' in the MATLAB workspace. Look at the substructure 'stresults.testvector' and identify any numeric test vectors with integer type. Cast these as doubles by calling:stresults.testvector.intfield = double(stresults.testvector.intfield);for every testvector 'intfield' with integer type.Then load the modified test results into the Test Results Viewer by calling:stviewer(stresults);

Summary: Why does MATLAB return an error when reading some MZXML files using MZXMLREAD?
Description: The MZXMLREAD function gives the following error when reading some valid mzXML files:??? Error using ==> mzxmlread>doScanChildrenInvalid  in file:  Missing precision, byteOrder, or pairOrder attribute.
Workaround: Open the file mzxmlread.m in the MATLAB Editor.Change line 628 from this:  error( 'BIOINFO:mzxmlread:invalidElem', ...                        'Invalid  in file:  Missing precision, byteOrder, or pairOrder attribute.', ...                        elementType );to  warning( 'BIOINFO:mzxmlread:invalidElem', ...                        'Invalid  in file:  Missing precision, byteOrder, or pairOrder attribute.', ...                        elementType );Save the file.

Summary: Real-Time Workshop might generate unnecessary work data for Abs blocks
Description: When generating code for Abs blocks in a Simulink model, Real-Time Workshop includes unnecessary IWork vectors if all of the following apply: The Abs block operates on signals of signed integer data type. In the Signals  group of the Data Validity Diagnostics  pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box, the Detect overflow  option specifies warning .
Workaround: Before using Real-Time Workshop to generate code for a model that contains Abs blocks operating on signed integers:From the model window's Simulation menu, select Configuration Parameters.The Configuration Parameters dialog box appears.In the Select tree on the left-hand side of the Configuration Parameters dialog box, click the Data Validity category.The Data Validity Diagnostics pane appears.In the Signals group of the Data Validity Diagnostics pane, specify the Detect overflow option as either none or error.Click the OK button to apply this change and close the Configuration Parameters dialog box.Note that since overflow detection is not supported in the generated code, there is no penalty associated with this change.

Summary: MATLAB hangs when editing a file in nodesktop mode on UNIX
Description: After starting MATLAB with the -nodesktop  flag on Linux or Solaris, MATLAB might hang while editing a file if Ctrl+C  was previously pressed. For the hang to occur, the MATLAB Editor must be open when Ctrl+C  is pressed. The next attempt to open a file in the MATLAB Editor causes MATLAB to hang and must be restarted.
Workaround: The problem does not occur if MATLAB is started regularly with the desktop on.If starting MATLAB in -nodesktop mode, there is a patch available for the R2006a and R2006b releases. Follow these steps:Download RobotUtils.class from the attached zip file.Put RobotUtils.class in matlabroot/java/patch/com/mathworks/mwswing, where matlabroot is the directory in which MATLAB is installed. You can determine matlabroot by running the matlabroot function.Restart MATLAB.The only loss in functionality introduced by the patch occurs when entering split-screen mode in the Editor. After applying the patch, it is no longer possible, when entering split-screen mode, to click on the widget above the scrollbar and drag the divider. Instead, clicking on the widget causes an even, 50-50 split.

Summary: Event binding does not work for states whose ancestors have bound events
Description: Event binding does not work when an event is bound to a state whose ancestor also has a bound event. For example, assume state A contains substate B. Suppose function call output event E1 is bound to state A and function call output event E2 is bound to state B. When state A is exited, the function call subsystem attached to E1 gets disabled. However, even though exiting A implies exiting its child state B, the function call subsystem attached to E2 does not get disabled.
Workaround: Do not use event binding for multiple states that have an ancestor-descendent relationship.

Summary: Plot sometimes not displayed in a standalone application
Description: Compiled applications that display two or more figure windows at the same time may exhibit problems while drawing the second figure window. This can result in the second plot not being displayed.
Workaround: To work around this issue, place a DRAWNOW command after the SURF command, and the plot will display properly.  For example:%%%BEGIN CODE%%%surf(peaks);drawnow;%%%END CODE%%%

Summary: Get error on addline when using digital I/O on Measurement Computing USB-1408FS.
Description: When I attempt to use addline  with a Measurement Computing USB-1408FS digital I/O object, I get an error. For example:dio = digitalio('mcc',0) % device 0 is USB-1408FSaddline(dio,0:7,'out') %Set I/O port as output ??? Error using ==> digitalio.addline Port is not software configurable.
Workaround: This bug is caused by an error in the configuration file. As a workaround, you can modify the configuration file as follows: Quit MATLAB. Change to the directory $MATLAB/toolbox/daq/daq/private, where $MATLAB is the directory where you installed MATLAB. Back up the file mwmcc.ini to a another name such as mwmcc.bak. Open mwmcc.ini and find the device entry, [USB-1408FS]. In that section, find the line DPORT0CONFIG = 3, and change it to DPORT0CONFIG = 2. Find the line DPORT1CONFIG = 3, and change it to DPORT1CONFIG = 2. Save and close the file. Restart MATLAB.

Summary: MATLAB might stop responding when you change Simulink model options
Description: Simulink might cause MATLAB to stop responding when you do the following in an open model:In the model toolbar, change the value of Simulation stop time  and do not apply the change. Open the Configuration Parameters dialog box by typing Ctrl+E  or return control to an already open Configuration Parameters dialog box.

Summary: Digital Filter Design block errors when you change the filter order of an SOS filter
Description: When you make any change to parameters of the Digital Filter Design block that results in a change to the filter order of an SOS filter, the block errors and does not update.
Workaround: To work around this problem,Navigate to$MATLAB/toolbox/dspblks/dspmaskswhere $MATLAB is the MATLAB root directory on your machine, as returned by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt.Replace the file dpsblkfdatool.m with the attached file.

Summary: STEPWISEFIT encounters convergence problems with ill-conditioned data
Description: The stepwisefit  function may encounter convergence problems if the columns of the input matrix X  are of very different magnitudes. For example, if one column contains values on the order of 1e14 and another contains values on the order of 1, the function may loop repeatedly (adding and removing the same term) until you interrupt it.
Workaround: You can avoid this problem by dividing each column of X by its maximum value. This will not change the set of columns chosen by the stepwise algorithm. If you want the coefficients on the original data scale, you can fit the original X again using the subset of the columns selected by stepwise.

Summary: Function parameters with a size are not loaded correctly
Description: Function parameters that contain a size are not represented correctly in MATLAB. For example,  void foo(double data[10]); shows in MATLAB as foo(voidPtr data)  instead of foo(doublePtr data) .
Workaround: Edit the header file to replace the sized object with a pointer: void foo(double *data)Alternatively, generate a prototype MATLAB file using loadlibrary with the mfilename option, then edit the MATLAB file replacing #10 with Ptr in the foo function arguments.

Summary: MATLAB throws a floating point exception in m_interpreter:inJitInterp::MarshalIn
Description: This error can occur if an incorrectly written MEX-file or third-party library is used that changes the state of the floating point control word that MATLAB uses, or leaves values on the floating point stack.
Workaround: MATLAB Version 7.4 contains a feature called jitValidateFPU.  If enabled, this feature forces a consistency check of the FPU before any JITed code is run.  If an inconsistency is detected, MATLAB displays a warning message and resets the FPU to the expected state.

Summary: Error opening Data Acquisition Toolbox softscope files using National Instruments hardware.
Description: If I save a configuration using some National Instruments hardware in Data Acquisition Toolbox softscope application, I get the following error when I attempt to open it from the command line. For example:softscope('fname.si') ??? Error using ==> softscope Error using ==> analoginput.analoginput Error using ==> analoginput.analoginput Failure to find requested data acquisition device: nidaqmx
Workaround: To work around this issue:Open the .si file in a text editor. Find the text "nidaqmx". Replace it with the text "nidaq". Save the modified file.Softscope will be able to load this modified .si file succesfully.

Summary: Problem loading legacy Model-Based Calibration Toolbox projects.
Description: A bug introduced in R2006a prevents some old Model Browser project files loading.  When you attempt to load a project, an error dialog is shown with the message: Failed to load project "PROJECT_NAME".  Operands to the || and && operators must be convertible to logical scalar values. This bug only affects projects older than Version 2.0 of Model-Based Calibration Toolbox, with data that has been reordered by selecting the "Reorder records" option in the Test Groupings dialog box.
Workaround: To work around this problem, download the file DefineSweepSet.m from the Attachments and copy it to the location matlabroot/toolbox/mbc/mbctools/@sweepset. You may then need to restart MATLAB.

Summary: Generated code fails to compile when building ERT S-function for model containing Rate Limiter block
Description: The code generated by Real-Time Workshop for a Simulink model can fail to compile when all of the following conditions are true: The model contains a block that references absolute time, such as the Rate Limiter block The model's System target file  is ERT (ert.tlc ) or ERT-based The option Create Simulink (S-Function) block  is selected The option Generate reusable code  is selected The option Single output/update function  is selected
Workaround: To work around this bug, eliminate any of the five conditions listed under Description.

Summary: Phase/Frequency Offset block can output incorrect phase offsets
Description: The Phase/Frequency Offset block has a latency bug that causes it to incorrectly calculate phase offsets.  For input signals with a constant input phase (e.g., a static PSK or QAM constellation), this bug is undetectable.  However, for an input signal with a rotating input phase (e.g., a sine wave), the bug results in an incorrect phase offset.
Workaround: The attached model, workaround_phase_offset, shows a way to work around this bug for an input signal with a constant frequency.  The input signal to the Phase/Frequency Offset (PFO) block is a complex 500 kHz sine wave.  The PFO block downconverts the signal to complex baseband.  Note that the PFO block requires an 11.25 degree phase offset for the block's output to have zero phase.  This is because the phase offset output has a one-sample latency relative to its input.If you zoom in on the beginning of the plot of the phase of the input signal (the third plot of the Scope block), you'll note that the second phase value is 11.25 degrees.  The PFO block is mistakenly calculating the input phase to be -11.25 degrees, rather than 0 degrees.  Thus, it requires an 11.25 degree phase shift to output a signal with zero phase.

Summary: Cannot set Maximum to Inf in Simulink.Parameter dialog
Description: The Model Explorer does not allow you to change a Simulink.Parameter object's Maximum  value from a finite value to Inf  or -Inf .
Workaround: Set the Maximum property programmatically, for example,Param.Max = Inf;

Summary: Issues using indexing assignment of Runtime Object Data field in Level-2 MATLAB-file S-function
Description: In Level-2 MATLAB-file S-functions, partial assignments to block outputs, dworks, parameters, or states might not succeed. In these situations, the assignment will silently fail. For example, the following assignment statement block.OutputPort(1).Data(1:2:3) = [1 5]; might cause the block's output port to retain its previous values and prevent the assignment from taking effect.
Workaround: Avoid partial assignments to the output port, dworks, states, or parameters of a Level-2 MATLAB-file S-function. For example, if you want to do:block.OutputPort(1).Data(1:2:3) = [1 5];consider replacing the above with:a = block.OutputPort(1).Data;a(1:2:3) = [1 5];block.OutputPort(1).Data = a;

Summary: External mode: logging scope data in Structure format crashes MATLAB session
Description: If you run a model containing a Scope block in external mode, and if the Scope block saves data to the workspace in Structure  format, the MATLAB session terminates with a segmentation violation.
Workaround: To work around this bug, use either the Array format or the Structure with time format to log scope data during an external mode simulation.

Summary: To Video Display block might produce an error
Description: If the Video output device parameter is set to OUTPUTDEVS, the To Video Display block produces an error during simulation.
Workaround: Download the attached ZIP file and extract its contents into the matlabroot/toolbox/vipblks/vipmasks directory.

Summary: State logging order: difference in simulation and Real-Time Workshop code generation
Description: If you enable state logging for a model that reuses a function-call subsystem, the order in which the Simulink simulation logs discrete states can differ from the order in the loaded MAT-file of a Real-Time Workshop generated executable for the same model.

Summary: Copying a figure to the Windows clipboard using the Edit menu can cause MATLAB to terminate or segv
Description: If you use a figure's Edit  menu to copy it? to the Windows clipboard, MATLAB can terminate or segv. This is most likely to occur when the figure uses the OpenGL renderer.
Workaround: Use the print command with the -dmeta option to work around this issue, as shown in the following example:surf(peaks);print(gcf, '-dmeta');

Summary: TSGETRELATIVETIME gives incorrect results when the reference time occurs later than the input time
Description: The result of the function TSGETRELATIVETIME will be off by 24 hours if the time in the reference data string (the second argument) occurs earlier in the day than the time of the input data string (the first argument). This may have impact: i.) When calling the RESAMPLE time series method on time series with absolute time vectors for time vectors with intra-day values. ii.) When calling methods to extract time series using events from time series objects with absolute time vectors using the methods GETTSAFTERATEVENT,GETTSAFTEREVENT,GETTSATEVENT,GETTSBEFOREATEVENT,GETTSBEFOREEVENT, and GETTSBETWEENEVENTS. iii.) When extracting samples from time series objects with absolulute time vectors using the GETSAMPLEUSINGTIME method.
Workaround: Replace the TSGETRELATIVETIME method with the file tsgetrelativetime.m in the attached zip file. The path is:$matlabroot$\toolbox\matlab\timeseries\tsgetrelativetime.m

Summary: The ga function violates bounds when initial population range and bounds are not consistent
Description: When the bounds on variables are inconsistent with the initial population range, the ga  function violates those bounds when forming the intermediate solution and also the final solution. Here is an example to illustrate this behavior:    lb = 2;   ub = 3;   [x,f] = ga(@sin, 1, [], [], [], [], lb, ub, [])   Optimization terminated: average change in the fitness value less than options.TolFun.   x =       1.0294   f =       0.8570
Workaround: You can work around this bug by providing an initial population range that is the same as the bounds. Here is an example to illustrate the workaround:   lb = 2;   ub = 3;   options = gaoptimset('PopInitRange',[lb;ub]);   [x,f] = ga(@sin, 1, [], [], [], [], lb, ub, [], options)   Optimization terminated: average change in the fitness value less than options.TolFun.   x =       2.9998   f =       0.1413

Summary: Matlab hangs on switch from Simulink toolbar to Configuration Parameters dialog box
Description: Matlab can hang while you are editing Simulink model parameters under the following circumstances: A Simulink model and the Configuration Parameters dialog box are both open. You type a new value for Stop Time  in the Simulink toolbar. You click the Configuration Parameters dialog box before pressing Enter  to complete the stop time edit.
Workaround: Press Enter after editing the model Stop Time or click elsewhere in the model before clicking in the Configuration Parameters dialog box.

Summary: Update Diagram can be inefficient for a model that contains Model blocks
Description: If a model contains Model blocks, and you change the value of Configuration Parameters > Model Referencing > Rebuild options  from: Never rebuild   to either of the following: If any changes detectedIf any changes in known dependencies detected   then the time to perform an Update Diagram on the top model may increase even though nothing has changed in any of the referenced models.
Workaround: The workaround to use depends on your MATLAB version.Workaround for releases prior to R2006aFirst, perform a complete successful build of the top model and all referenced models, then set Rebuild options to Never rebuild. If a referenced model thereafter changes, set the parameter to If any changes detected, rebuild the model reference targets, then set the parameter back to Never rebuild.Workaround for releases R2006a and R2006bQuit MATLAB.Make a backup copy of the following file: MATLABROOT\toolbox\rtw\rtw\private\rtwinfomatman.pDownload the attached file appropriate for your release.Rename the file to rtwinfomatman.p.Save the file in the directory specified in Step 2.Restart MATLAB.Run the rehash toolbox command at the MATLAB prompt.MATLABROOT represents the name of your MATLAB installation directory, as returned by the matlabroot function.

Summary: Signal properties evaluate within the wrong context.
Description: Port parameters (signal properties) can erroneously resolve to variables in the mask workspace of the block that "owns" the port.  This block can also prevent port parameters from evaluating correctly if it does not permit hierarchical resolution (PermitHierarchicalResolution = 'None'). The root cause of both of these problems is that port parameters are evaluated within the context of the block that owns the port instead of being evaluated within the context of the block's parent system.

Summary: Deleting an object in a referenced model displayed in the Model Explorer may crash MATLAB
Description: Deleting an object in a referenced model displayed in the Model Explorer may crash MATLAB under the following conditions: The model that references the model containing the deleted object appears as a node in the Model Explorer's Model Hierarchy pane. The node is expanded to show the Model block that references the model containing the deleted object.
Workaround: Close the Model Explorer before deleting an object in a referenced model.

Summary: Bus signals are not completely logged in some cases
Description: A bus signal will not be completely logged during simulation if: The signal being logged is a virtual bus signal. One of the component signals is a bus signal that originates from a referenced model.  In such cases, only the first component of the bus signal originating from the referenced model will be logged.
Workaround: No workarounds are available.

Summary: Model Dependency Viewer prints node expansion symbols incorrectly
Description: Printing a view in the model dependency viewer prints '+'  signs instead of '-'  signs.

Summary: Generated code incorrectly optimized away for model with continuous rates and scope block
Description: Generated base-rate code for an atomic subsystem in a Simulink model can be incorrectly optimized away if all of the following conditions are true: The model includes a scope block The model has continuous sample times The model is running in multitasking mode  Under these conditions, the atomic subsystem code that runs at the model base rate may be missing from the generated code.
Workaround: To work around this bug, remove the scope block from the model before generating code.  Use of scope blocks is not recommended for real-time code generation.

Summary: MATLAB crash: Real-Time Workshop build process causes MATLAB to crash
Description: The Real-Time Workshop build process crashes MATLAB when there are unclosed nested comments in generated code. For example: /* Notes/* There are two "open comment" character sequences, but only one "close comment" character sequence.*/ ...
Workaround: Download the attached file. In MATLAB, cd to the working directory to which you downloaded the file.Run the file by entering fix348466 at the MATLAB command prompt. You must run the file fix348466.p each time you restart MATLAB.

Summary: mpctool does not find new operating point when nominal MV and MO values are present
Description: In the model predictive control design example given in Model Predictive Control Toolbox -> Getting Started -> Design Tool -> Nonlinear Plant , the step responses returned from the linear and nonlinear simulation tests differ from what is described in the documentation.  This inconsistency occurs because the operating point computation does not converge to a steady state solution. The resulting plant model is not correct and the closed loop simulation does not match the simulation described in the documentation.See EBR 347085  for details.
Workaround: See patch in EBR 347085

Summary: Assertion and stack trace reported after using set_param to update models containing Embedded MATLAB Function blocks or Stateflow charts
Description: Issuing the set_param function with the SimulationCommand parameter to update a model generates a stack trace under the following conditions: The model contains a subsystem that is linked to a library. The subsystem contains an Embedded MATLAB Function block, Stateflow Chart, or Truth Table block. The model does not contain any of the previously mentioned blocks at the top level. For example, consider the following command:  >> set_param('no_eml_Model','SimulationCommand','update')  This call to set_param produces the following assertion and stack trace: failed assertion in B:/matlab/toolbox/stateflow/src/fsm/fsm.cpp at line 389:  id = ( Object )*mxGetPr(prhs[0]) Stack Trace: [0] sf.mexw32:0x1b9f91b8(0x1ba512b4 "id = ( Object )*mxGetPr(prhs[0])..",0x1ba5120c "B:/matlab/toolbox/stateflow/src/..", 389, 0x1ba4d310"Invalid object id.")  [1] sf.mexw32:0x1b98a574(2, 3, 3, 0x00d1d97c)  [2] sf.mexw32:0x1b9b750b(255, 0, 0x00d1d8ec, 3)  [3] sf.mexw32:0x1b9fffa5(0, 0x00d1d8ec, 4, 0x00d1d978)  [4] sf.mexw32:0x1b98a33e(0, 0x00d1d8ec, 0x00d1d8ec, 0x14c1dda0)  [5] sf.mexw32:0x1b98a39f(0, 0x00d1d8ec, 4, 0x00d1d978)  [6] libmex.dll:_mexRunMexFile(0, 0x00d1d8ec, 4, 0x00d1d978) + 119 bytes  [7] libmex.dll:private: void __thiscall
