Summary: Maximum and Minimum blocks can produce incorrect results in Running mode
Description: The Signal Processing Blockset and Video and Image Processing Blockset Minimum and Maximum blocks can produce incorrect output when the Mode  parameter is set to Running .
Workaround: Use the files in the attached ZIP file to fix this problem. There is a file for each platform supported by MATLAB. The platform is indicated by the file extension. Copy the file for your platform into the directory  matlabroot/toolbox/dspblks/dspmex  where matlabroot is the path returned when you type matlabroot at the MATLAB command line.  If you are running MATLAB on multiple platforms or are not sure which file to copy, copy all the files into the same directory.

Summary: The Discrete-Time VCO library block?uses?an incorrect sample time when inherited 'Sample time' parameter values ("-1") are specified.
Description: The Discrete-Time VCO library block?uses?an incorrect sample time when inherited 'Sample time' parameter values ("-1") are specified.
Workaround: Use a?discrete sample time value greater than zero for the 'Sample time' parameter in the Discrete-Time VCO block.

Summary: Referenced model with bus object does not rebuild when bus object changes
Description: If a model contains a referenced model that uses a bus object defined in the base workspace, changes to the elements of that bus object do not automatically cause the submodel to rebuild.
Workaround: Open the Model Explorer and choose "Configuration > Model Referencing". Set "Rebuild options" to "If any changes detected".

Summary: Specifying Signal Builder block sample time as a MATLAB expression might create a multirate system
Description: Suppose you specify a discrete sample time for Simulink blocks in your model by entering the same MATLAB expression as the value of each block's Sample time  parameter. Simulink will treat your model as a multirate system under the following conditions:     One of the blocks whose Sample time  parameter you specify is a Signal Builder block.     The MATLAB expression you specify as the value of the Sample time  parameter for blocks in your model evaluates to an infinite decimal. This includes irrational (e.g., pi  or sqrt(2) ) and repeating decimal (e.g., 1/3 ) numbers.  In this situation, you might observe that your simulation executes quite slowly or that Simulink displays error messages associated with the specified sample times. Note that the Signal Builder block incorrectly handles its sample time when you specify the value as a MATLAB expression that evaluates to an infinite decimal. In this case, it replaces the MATLAB expression with a literal value that is not exactly equal to the original MATLAB expression.
Workaround: To work around this issue, specify the value of the Signal Builder block's Sample time parameter in the following way:    In your model editor, select the Signal Builder block.    From the Simulink Edit menu, select Look Under Mask to access the underlying subsystem.    In the subsystem that underlies the Signal Builder block, double-click the From Workspace block to access its block parameter dialog box.    In the From Workspace block parameter dialog box, enter your MATLAB expression as the value of its Sample time parameter.    Click the OK button to apply your change and close the From Workspace block parameter dialog box.Afterward, if you alter any settings on the Signal Builder block's Simulation Options dialog box, you must repeat this workaround.

Summary: Models with multiple sample times do not run correctly when compiled with Microsoft Visual Studio 2005 Professional Edition
Description: xPC Target models with multiple sample times cannot correctly compile with Microsoft Visual Studio 2005 (MSVC8.0 Professional Edition). Using this compiler can cause some blocks to generate NaN values at some output ports.
Workaround: As a workaround, use Microsoft Visual C++ .NET 2003 (7.1 Professional Edition) or earlier. The optimizer in Microsoft Visual Studio 2005 (MSVC8.0 Professional Edition) causes an x87 FPU data register stack overflow problem.

Summary: Logging bus signals in a referenced model may cause build failures
Description: Logging a bus signal in a referenced model may result in failures. The failures include: Model Reference build errors. Inability to log the bus signal even though it is marked for logging. Partial logging of bus signals: some of the component signals in the bus may not be logged.  The following must be true for these failures to happen: A bus signal (virtual or non-virtual) is marked for logging.At least one component signal of the bus originates in a different subsystem than the location where the bus signal is marked for logging.
Workaround: No workarounds are available.

Summary: Incorrect input transforms for local polynomials
Description: Incorrect transforms of inputs occur for local polynomialmodels, when 1) you clear the 'Transform input range to [-1,1]' checkbox (on the Linear Model Settings dialog box) and 2) you select 'Copy range' when selecting data.In the View Model dialog box the coding section reads:[a,b] -> [c,d]This should read [a,b] -> [a,b]that is, no transform should occur because you cleared the transform inputs check box.The actual coding is as reported in the View Model dialog box, and the fitted model can be used for tasks such as optimization, but the model parameters are transformed.
Workaround: Copy the attachments to the following locations:$MATLAB/toolbox/mbcmodels/@xregmodel/code.m$MATLAB/toolbox/mbcmodels/@xregmodel/setcode.m$MATLAB/toolbox/mbcmodels/@xregmodel/datum.m$MATLAB/toolbox/mbcmodels/@xregtwostage/TSsetcode.m$MATLAB/toolbox/mbctools/@mdev_local/updateInputs.mRename $MATLAB/toolbox/mbcmodels/@xregtwostage/TSsetcode.m to setcode.mRestart MATLAB.

Summary: The Simulink Summary Table component "Mask Description" property produces an empty table cell
Description: The Simulink Summary Table component "Mask Description" property produces an empty table cell.
Workaround: Copy the attached file to the following location:matlab/toolbox/simulink/components/@rptgen_sl/@propsrc_sl/getCommonPropValue.p

Summary: Incorrect result for LTI assignments of the form sys(58,58) = ...
Description: Due to a bug in the LTI assignment code, assignments of the form   sys(58,58) = rhs  % sys is a @tf, @ss, @zpk, or @frd modelor    sys(58,58,k1,..,kN) = rhswere interpreted as   sys(:,:) = rhs   sys(:,:,k1,..,kN) = rhsleading to reassignment of all I/O pairs rather than just the (58,58) pair.
Workaround: Replace the following filesmatlab\toolbox\control\control\@ltipack\@ssdata\setsubsys.mmatlab\toolbox\control\control\@ltipack\@tfdata\setsubsys.mmatlab\toolbox\control\control\@ltipack\@zpkdata\setsubsys.mmatlab\toolbox\control\control\@ltipack\@frddata\setsubsys.mby the versions in the zipped file below

Summary: Microsoft Team Foundation Server not appearing under Source Control preferences
Description: When Microsoft Team Foundation Server is installed, it does not appear under MATLAB Source Control preferences.
Workaround: Copy the existing registry entry for Microsoft Team Foundation Server MSSCCI Provider and give the copy a shorter name, e.g., MS Team Foundation Server. The registry entry is located inHKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\SourceCodeControlProvider\InstalledSCCProviders.

Summary: GF_TABLE1 and GF_TABLE2 created by gftable should have one column only
Description: The function gftable creates a .mat file to accelerate Galois field computations.  It is typically used when a nondefault primitive polynomial is specified by the user.  When used, it creates two global variables, GF_TABLE1 and GF_TABLE2.  These variables should be single-column vectors.  However, they are incorrectly created as matrices with multiple columns.  This results in incorrect numerical results for Galois field computations when gftable is used with nondefault polynomials.
Workaround: You can download one of the following files to work around this problem.If you are using R14SP3 or earlier, download gftable_R14sp3_and_before.m.  If you are using R2006a or later, download gftable_R2006a_and_later.m.In either case, copy the downloaded file to matlabroot/toolbox/comm/comm.  Then rename the downloaded file to matlabroot/toolbox/comm/comm/gftable.m, and perform the following MATLAB command:>>  rehash toolboxcache

Summary: Error occurs in FRD constructor when specifying an array of SISO frequency responses
Description: Creating FRD models from an array of SISO frequency responses may result in the following error: ??? Error using ==> ltipack.frddata.utCheckDataAmbiguous dimensioning of the response data array. For example, the following code produces the error instead of constructing a 1x6 array of SISO FRD models:freq = 1:100;resp = rand(100,1,6)sys = frd(resp,freq)
Workaround: Specify the leading singleton dimensions in the response array.? For example, use the following code to construct the 1x6 array of SISO FRD models:resp = reshape(resp,[1 1 100 1 6]);sys = frd(resp,freq)

Summary: Atomic Subsystems that minimize algebraic loops and that contain drivelines cause MATLAB to crash
Description: MATLAB crashes with a segmentation violation when you attempt to run a model containing a driveline within an Atomic Subsystem that minimizes algebraic loops.
Workaround: Deselect the Minimize algebraic loop occurrences check box on the subsystem dialog for all Atomic Subsystems in the hierarchy that contain drivelines.

Summary: Array Editor displays real, single-precision scalars as having an imaginary part
Description: The Array Editor displays real, single-precision scalars as having an imaginary part. For example, runx = single(5);openvar x The Array Editor shows x  as 5 + 1i . Arrays of single values are shown correctly.
Workaround: Display the values in the Command Window instead of in the Array Editor.

Summary: Signal dimension propagation fails if a Selector block, configured for matrix (2-D), is in a loop and the source block cannot determine the dimensions of the output port
Description: A model might fail to propagate signal dimensions when the following conditions are true: A Selector block, configured for matrix (2-D) input, is placed in a loop and the U  input port is on the loop The source block defines a vector output with width information only, for example, a Unit Delay block with a vector-valued IC No other blocks in the loop can determine whether this is a row vector, column vector, or unoriented vector
Workaround: As a workaround, insert a Signal Specification block before the Selector block and use it to explicitly specify the signal dimensions for the signal connected to the U input port.

Summary: Operating point searches fail for blocks with discrete state of sample time = 0
Description: In MATLAB R2006a and R2006b, when you search for an operating point for a model with blocks that have discrete state with sample time = 0, the operating point may not find a steady state solution.
Workaround: If you are using MATLAB R2006a, two files need to be replaced as follows:1. Go to (matlabroot)\toolbox\shared\slcontrollib\@slcontrol\@Utilities directory   2. Rename getDerivs.m to getDerivs_OLD.m 3. Rename getStateNameFromStateStruct.m to getStateNameFromStateStruct_OLD.m 4. Copy the attached (R2006a)getDerivs.m and (R2006a)getStateNameFromStateStruct.m files into the same directory 5. Rename the files back to getDerivs.m and getStateNameFromStateStruct.m respectively  If you are using MATLAB R2006b, follow the same steps but use (R2006b)getDerivs.m and (R2006b)getStateNameFromStateStruct.m files instead.

Summary: Export Functions build generates invalid code for root-level nonvirtual Bus Selector
Description: Real-Time Workshop Embedded Coder generates invalid code when an Export Functions  build is performed for a subsystem containing a root-level nonvirtual Bus Selector. The problem is illustrated in the attached model m346956.mdl . To observe the problem, follow these steps: Download m346956.mdl  to a working directory and open the model in MATLAB. Right-click the fcns  subsystem and select Real-Time Workshop > Export Functions . This brings up the Build code for Subsystem dialog box. In the dialog box, click the Build  button. In the generated HTML report, observe that the generated code is invalid, because the external inputs record fcns_U  is not accessed in the code.
Workaround: When the condition is encountered, you can fix the problem by moving affected Bus Selector blocks into the exported function-call subsystems.  For example, in the model m346956.mdl, the workaround for m346956/fcns is illustrated in m346956/fcns_workaround.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Wrong answer when using graphical functions that return vector/matrix arguments and access global data directly
Description: Stateflow may return incorrect results when a chart calls a graphical function that returns matrix or vector data. The bug occurs if both  of the following conditions occur: The chart assigns the return value of the function to a chart local vector or matrix data. The function reads or writes the same data.  For example, assume that a Stateflow chart declares x  as  a chart local vector or matrix data, and calls a graphical function foo  as follows: x = foo();  The chart may generate incorrect code if foo()  reads or writes x  directly.
Workaround: Do not access the chart local data directly in the graphical function.

Summary: Indexing error causes SEGV
Description: In some cases, an indexing error such as the one shown in the example below can cause a segmentation violation in MATLAB.     for x=0:1       k(x) = 1;    end
Workaround: First, disable the JIT/accelerator by entering the following command:    feature accel offWhen you run your code again, MATLAB should display the appropriate indexing error message. Once the error in your MATLAB-code has been fixed, you should no longer encounter the crash, even with the JIT/accelerator enabled.

Summary: fscanf takes considerably longer when using a scanset in the format string
Description: Calls to fscanf(fid, '...%*[^\n]')  take too long in Release 2006b if the amount of text being skipped is long.
Workaround: Avoiding the use of scanset notation, whenever possible, will improve the performance. For example, take the following command that uses the scanset notation [^\n]:   data = fscanf(fid, '%d:%d:%d%*[^\n]')If the purpose is to skip an additional 3 integers, you can rewrite this command as   data = fscanf(fid, '%d:%d:%d%*d%*d%*d')

Summary: Initializing parameters of blocks residing in a masked subsystem can cause errors
Description: Models that worked in previous releases may encounter an error like this: Error in 'model/SS1': Initialization commands cannot be evaluated. MATLAB error message: Error using ==> set_paramAttempting to resolve symbol 'SS2_MaskParam' in the uninitialized workspace of masked block 'model/SS1/SS2'. Thisproblem is probably caused by a set_param call on 'model/SS1/SS2/Blk' initiated from outside the masked block. Allsuch set_param calls should be done by the masked block's initialization code. This error occurs when a command causes a change to parameter values of blocks in a masked subsystem before the mask workspace has been initialized. The error results from a change in this release that disallows using set_param  to set the parameter of a block in a masked subsystem from outside that block's initialization code. This change is intended to prevent such problems as wrong answers and infinite recursion during initialization.
Workaround: R2007a introduces a model parameter that allows you to disable this error message while updating the model. To disable the error message, execute this command:set_param(mdl,'AllowSetParamOverMasks','on')The default for this parameter is 'off'. When set to 'on', the model reports warnings rather than errors, allowing work to continue on the model. This is a temporary workaround that will be deprecated in the R2009b release of Simulink. Note that turning AllowSetParamOverMasks parameter on may allow Simulink to incorrectly initialize some mask workspaces and this could result in wrong answers.

Summary: Setting DefaultFigureWindowStyle to docked can cause printing errors
Description: Setting DefaultFigureWindowStyle to docked  can cause errors to occur when printing Simulink  models or Stateflow charts. For example: >> set(0,'DefaultFigureWindowStyle','docked') >> vdp >> print -svdp ??? Error using ==> close Error using ==> close Error using ==> close Error using ==> close Error using ==> close Invalid figure handle.
Workaround: Set DefaultFigureWindowStyle to 'normal' before printing from Simulink or Stateflow by executing the following command in the MATLAB command window:set(0,'DefaultFigureWindowStyle','normal')

Summary: createClassFromWsdl doesn't recognize binding style in operation tag of WSDL
Description: A customer wanted to connect to a web service using the "rpc"  style binding, rather than "document" . Calling createSoapMessage  and explicitly specifying the style as "rpc"  works. However, when using createClassFromWsdl , MATLAB doesn't correctly recognize the binding style from the supplied WSDL file. For example, the definitions  tag of the customer's WSDL file looks something like this:<definitions .... >     <binding .... >         <soap:binding transport="uri"/>         <operation .... >            <soap:operation soapAction="uri"? style="rpc"/>         </operation>     </binding></definitions> Here, the "rpc"  style is being specified in the soap:operation  tag. But MATLAB doesn't recognize it and creates classes that use the "document"  binding style. MATLAB only recognizes the style if it is specified in the binding  tag.For example,<binding style="rpc"...../>
Workaround: There is no workaround.

Summary: Trigonometric Function block uses atan2 function from host compiler's C++ Math Library
Description: When specifying atan2  as the Function  parameter for the Trigonometric Function block, Simulink computes the arctangent for floating-point data using a function from the host compiler's C++ Math Library instead of the MATLAB library. Consequently, the block might produce results that are slightly inconsistent with MATLAB.

Summary: Unable to create a struct mwarray with more than two dimensions
Description: When creating an MWARRAY using the following syntax: mwArray(int num_dims, const int* dims, int num_fields, const char** fieldnames)  the struct array that is generated has only two dimensions, even when 'num_dims' is greater than 2.
Workaround: No workaround is available.

Summary: Objects inside UIPanel objects fail to move and resize properly inside Plot Edit Mode
Description: If I attempt to resize or move an Axes or UIControl object which is inside of a UIPanel object, as in the following code,h = figure; hp = uipanel('Title','Main Panel','FontSize',12,...              'BackgroundColor','white',...              'Position',[.25 .1 .67 .67]); hsp = uipanel('Parent',hp,'Title','Subpanel','FontSize',12,...               'Position',[.4 .1 .5 .5]); hbsp = uicontrol('Parent',hsp,'String','Push here',...               'Position',[18 18 72 36]);  the changes in size and position of the objects are only a fraction of the mouse pointer movement.

Summary: imwrite does not write transparency data for 8-bit RGB output
Description: imwrite  produces a warning when prompted to write transparency data for 8-bit-per-sample RGB images, and no transparency chunk is saved.  The message is "Warning: Ignoring attempt to write 16-bit tRNS chunk when bit_depth is 8"

Summary: Error using the Get function on a mwArray that has dimensions greater than or equal to 5.
Description: When the dimension of an mwArray is 5 or higher, calling a GET function on the array results in an error. This error does not occur if the dimension of the mwArray is 4 or less.
Workaround: None.

Summary: Unknown exception when minimizing algebraic loop is turned on for atomic subsystem
Description: Simulink may issue the following exception violation: Caught unexpected exception of unknown type  when an atomic subsystem contains both function call subsystems and other subsystems, and when the Minimize algebraic loop occurrences  check box is checked.
Workaround: To workaround this problem, uncheck the Minimize algebraic loop occurrences check box.To find the Minimize algebraic loop occurrences check box:Right click inside a subsystemClick on Subsystem ParametersCheck the Treat as atomic unit box to display the Minimize algebraic loop occurrences check box

Summary: Incorrect code generation: Real-Time Workshop incorrectly removes spaces in generated code
Description: The Real-Time Workshop code generator formats a line of code with length greater than 80 characters so that the code spans multiple lines, each line having a maximum of 80 characters. When the character sequence "- - " or "+ + " is at the end or break point of an 80 character line, the code generator removes the space between the minus or plus signs, causing the error in the code, rendering it uncompilable. For example, consider the following code: [78 character length expression] a - -b; In this case, the code generator creates the following code, which does not compile: [78 character length expression] a --b;
Workaround: To generate code for the affected model, do the following:Download the attached file.In MATLAB, cd to the working directory to which you downloaded the file.Run the file by entering fix346054 at the MATLAB command prompt.You must run the fix346054.p file each time you restart MATLAB.

Summary: Adding a timeseries to a tscollection with differing time units or start date can make the tscollection time vector invalid
Description: Adding a timeseries to a tscollection where the added time series has the same time vector, but where there is a differing TimeInfo.StartDate  property or TimeInfo.Units  property, will cause the tscollection  time vector to be set to an invalid value. Specifically, the tscollection  time vector will be inherited from one time series and the TimeInfo  properties from the other. The following two examples illlustrate the problem. Example 1:  % Create two timeseries with the same time vector but % different numeric times due to differing time units.ts1 = timeseries(randn(10,1),(0:9)','Name','initial');tsc = tscollection(ts1);ts = timeseries(randn(10,1),(0:1/60:9/60)');ts.TimeInfo.Units = 'minutes';tsc = addts(tsc,ts);% Note that the tscollection has inherited the time % vector from ts2 but the TimeInfo.Units from ts1  Example 2:  % Create two timeseries with differing numeric times % but the same absolute time vectorsts1 = timeseries([1;1],{'01-Jan-2002','02-Jan-2002'},'Name','ts1');ts2 = ts1;ts2.TimeInfo.StartDate = '01-Jan-2003';ts2.Time = ts2.Time-365;tsc = tscollection(ts1);% Note that ts1,ts2 have the same absolute time vectorts1.getabstimets2.getabstimetsc = addts(tsc,ts2,'ts2');% Note that the absolute time of the tscollection tsc % has been set back 1 year since it has inherited the % TimeInfo.StartDate from ts1.TimeInfo but the numeric% time vector from ts2.tsc.getabstime
Workaround: Before adding a timeseries to a tscollection, first modify its TimeInfo.Units and TimeInfo.StartDate properties to match those of the tscolleciton.

Summary: Digital Filter Block Coefficients Are Not Tunable in External Mode
Description: Any numerator or denominator coefficients entered into the Digital Filter block mask are hard-coded in the generated code. These parameters are therefore not tunable in external mode.
Workaround: If you are using a previous version, there are no known workarounds at this time.

Summary: Editor/Debugger: truncated filenames in document bar
Description: Filenames in the Editor/Debugger's document bar are sometimes truncated when the bar is actually big enough to display the complete filenames. This is particularly noticeable when the document bar is on the left or right side of the window. The problem typically occurs when the longest filename currently displayed becomes longer, either because you rename the file, or modify it (which appends an asterisk to its name). The filename is truncated in the document bar, as expected. However, if you restore the previous filename, for example, by undoing modifications to the file, the filename remains truncated in the document bar.
Workaround: Close the file whose name is incorrectly truncated and reopen it, which restores the full filename in the document bar. Note that after you end the MATLAB session, the problem does not recur when you next start MATLAB.

Summary: Embedded MATLAB debugger displays past values of persistent variables.
Description: If you do not initialize persistent variables in Embedded MATLAB, the debugger displays the value of the persistent variables from past runs when you start a new run of a model. Note that the model will run correctly, and the persistent variable will be re-initialized. This bug occurs when debugging Embedded MATLAB Function blocks in Simulink and Embedded MATLAB functions in Stateflow

Summary: Solvers do not stop from output or plot functions
Description: When output functions are specified in a cell array via the OutputFcns  option, only the function in the last element of the cell array can stop the solver, by setting its output to stop = true . Setting stop = true  in any of the other output functions has no effect. The same is true for plot functions using the PlotFcns  option.

Summary: gaminv can fail to converge for probability values near 1
Description: For probability values very close to 1, the root finding algorithm in gaminv  can fail to converge. In those cases, gaminv  issues a warning and may return an inaccurate result.

Summary: MAT file containing a configuration set object may grow if loaded and resaved
Description: If you store a configuration set object in a MAT file, the size of MAT file may increase if you load the configuration set object from the MAT file, then save it back to the MAT file.
Workaround: Instead of saving the original configuration set object back into the MAT file, make a copy of the configuration set object and save the copy into the MAT file. For example:>> load('your_file'); %  The MAT file contains a configuration set object 'cs'>> cs_copy = copy(cs); % Make a copy of 'cs' on the base workspace>> save('your_file', cs_copy); % The MAT file is the same as before the load

Summary: Canceling a Simulink Finder search hangs MATLAB
Description: Canceling a long search in the Simulink Finder could cause a hang.

Summary: The MATLAB Compiler documentation specifies an incorrect path for Java on a 64-bit Linux platform
Description: The MATLAB Compiler Documentation for R2006b states that the path for Java on Linux x86-64 is //sys/java/jre/glnxa64/jre1.4.2/lib/amd64/  This is not correct.
Workaround: The correct path should be: matlabroot/version_number/sys/java/jre/glnxa64/jre1.5.0/lib/amd64/

Summary: MATLAB crashes for a certain combination of inputs to the quantizer object constructor
Description: MATLAB crashes when the following command is issued at the command line.quantizer([6 8], 'float')  An error message is expected in this case.
Workaround: To work around this problem, specify the data type first in the argument list. For example, the following command returns the proper error message, and MATLAB does not crash:quantizer('float', [6 8])??? Exponentlength must be less than wordlength.

Summary: Wrong answer: Simulink Accelerator generates wrong answer
Description: Simulink Accelerator uses temporary variable names to generate code that might be the same as output variable names.  Simulating a model in Accelerator mode therefore can result in a different output than when simulated in normal mode.
Workaround: None.

Summary: General TCM/M-PSK TCM/Rectangular QAM TCM Decoder blocks error out incorrectly in terminated and truncated modes
Description: Three blocks are affected by this bug: (1) General TCM Decoder block, (2) M-PSK TCM Decoder block, and (3) Rectangular QAM Decoder block.The reference pages of these blocks state that the traceback depth parameter (D) must be less than or equal to the vector length of the input when the operation mode parameter of these blocks is set to Terminated or Truncated. A bug in the parameter validation process causes these blocks to throw an error in these modes if the traceback depth parameter (D) is less than or equal to the vector length of the input but greater  than (vector length of input / log2(M)), where M is the M-ary number parameter or length of the signal constellation parameter values.
Workaround: To work around this issue, adopt one of the following suggestions:1) Set the traceback depth (D) to less than or equal to (vector length of the input/log2(M)), where M is the M-ary number parameter or length of the signal constellation parameter values. OR2) Make the vector length of the input equal to or greater than (the traceback depth (D) * log2(M)), where M is the M-ary number parameter or length of the signal constellation parameter values.

Summary: Stateflow charts containing backslash character ("\") cause compilation failure

Description: When the input to the Resize block is single-precision floating point data type and the Resize factor in % parameter is an integer multiple of 100 (e.g. 300%, 500%, 600%, 700%, 900% etc.), the block outputs incorrect values.

Setting stop = true  in any of the other output functions has no effect.? The same is true for plot functions using the PlotFcns  option.

Summary: ClearCase source control requires registering project for each file
Description: You must select Register ClearCase Project with MATLAB  for every file you want to perform a source control action on. The expectation is that you should only need to perform the register project action once for all files in a project.

Summary: Double click does not work when using uigetfile or uigetdir on Solaris.
Description: uigetfile  and uigetdir  do not work as expected on Solaris platforms. Interactively double clicking a file or directory to open it does not have an affect. That is, the clicked file or directory is not opened.
Workaround: There is no workaround.

Summary: Why am I unable to read a logical value from a single cell in my Excel file using XLSREAD?
Description: This is caused by a bug in the xlsread  function. xlsread  in MATLAB 7.3 and earlier does not import scalar logical values.
Workaround: To work around this issue, you can import the scalar value as part of a vector using xlsread, and then convert the element of interest in the imported vector back to logical.For example, to import the scalar logical value at A1:A1, use xlsread to import a 2-element vector A1:B1, and then convert the first element of the vector to logical:   A = xlsread('myfile.xls', 'A1:B1');   A = logical(A(1,1));   whos A     Name      Size            Bytes  Class      Attributes     A         1x1                 1  logical

Summary: I receive errors when creating separate instances of my object in separate threads
Description: I create two threads in my Java code. Each thread creates its own instance of the Java class created by MATLAB Builder for Java 1.0 (R2006b). I have specified that the object instances do not share the MCR. I am using the following code where mypackage.myclass is the class built with MATLAB Builder for Java.import mypackage.myclass;public class multithread {    public class Worker extends Thread {        public void run() {            myclass t = new myclass();            t.dispose();        }    }    public void createThreads(){        for (int i=0; i <2; i++) {            Worker wi = new Worker();            wi.start();        }    }    public static void main(String[] args) throws Exception {        multithread t = new multithread();        t.createThreads();    }} When I run the code, I receive the following error printed to the command line??? The feature 'lmfeaturelist' has already been registered.and a "Microsoft Visual C++ Runtime Library" dialog box with the messageRuntime Error!Program: ...java.exeThis application has requested the Runtime to terminate it in an unusual way. Please contact the application's support team for more information.
Workaround: To work around this issue, you can either specified a shared MCR or you can use Java thread locking to ensure that the first instance of the MCR is finished initializing before creating the 2nd object.    To specify a shared MCR, select "Object instances share MCR" in the Settings -> General tab in DEPLOYTOOL, or use the -S flag to the MCC command.    Implement thread locking with a simple synchronized factory method that returns new objects.For example, the above code could be modified as follows:import mypackage.myclass;public class multithread {    public class Worker extends Thread {        public void run() {            myclass t = multithread.newInstance();            t.dispose();        }    }    public synchronized static myclass newInstance(){        return new myclass();    }    public void createThreads(){        for (int i=0; i <2; i++) {            Worker wi = new Worker();            wi.start();        }    }    public static void main(String[] args) throws Exception {        multithread t = new multithread();        t.createThreads();    }}

Summary: Bloomberg fetch calls cause MATLAB segmentation violations
Description: Bloomberg fetch calls, including GETDATA calls that request bulk data fields, TIMESERIES, and HISTORY calls; intermittently cause MATLAB segmentation violations or crashes.
Workaround: Using the R2007a function $MATLAB/toolbox/datafeed/datafeed/bbdatafeed.mexw32 solves this problem.

Summary: Figure keypressfcn callback might not execute if the figure Renderer or Visual property is changed.
Description: If the figure Renderer  or Visual  property is changed, the figure's client area might loose focus even though the figure window has focus. This can prevent execution of the  KeypressFcn  callback when a user presses a key while the figure window has focus.
Workaround: Click on the figure's client area to give it focus before pressing a key. This needs to be done only once after changing the Renderer or Visual property.

Summary: Error when calling plot function with certain properties from debugger
Description: Calling plot  from within the MATLAB debugger with any of the properties  DisplayName XDataSource YDataSource ZDataSource  passed as input parameter-value pairs, as in the following code,test = magic(10);plot(test,'YDataSource','test','DisplayName','test'); generates the following error:??? Undefined function or variable "x".Error in ==> getcolumn at 18  x = strtrim(x);

Summary: Editor on Macintosh: some non-English characters do not appear correctly
Description: On the Macintosh platform, after saving a MATLAB file in the MATLAB Editor, certain non-English characters (such as ¨¦,¨¤,¨¨ or ¨ş) appear as white space or '?' the next time the file is opened.
Workaround: As a workaround, use a different editor.

Summary: MATLAB crashes when using an invalid array subscript
Description: Subscripting a variable by 0 may result in a segmentation violation instead of an error. For example:for x=0:1 ??? k(x) = 1; end
Workaround: Disable the MATLAB JIT/Accelerator using feature('accel','off')

Summary: Maximum and Minimum blocks can produce incorrect results in Running mode
Description: The Signal Processing Blockset and Video and Image Processing Blockset Minimum and Maximum blocks can produce incorrect output when the Mode  parameter is set to Running .
Workaround: Use the attached files to fix this problem. There is a file for each platform supported by MATLAB. The platform is indicated by the file extension. Download the file for your platform into the directorymatlabroot/toolbox/dspblks/dspmexwhere matlabroot is the path returned when you type matlabroot at the MATLAB command line.If you are running MATLAB on multiple platforms or are not sure which file to download, download all the files into the same directory.

Summary: mpctool fails to launch when plant model is not detectable from measured outputs
Description: An error occurs and mpctool  fails to launch when you have a plant model (G ) that is not detectable from measured outputs, build a model predictive controller using that plant as input (MPC?=?mpc(G) , and try to launch the Model Predictive Control design tool (mpctool(MPC) ) using that controller.  The error that occurs is:  Undefined function or variable "wb".   Error in ==> mpctool at 103      if ~isempty(wb) && isa(handle(wb),'figure')
Workaround: To work around this bug, launch mpctool without any input argument and then, import the plant from the mpctool GUI.In general, you should always make the plant detectable from measured outputs before starting a model predictive control design. Refer to the Model Predictive Control Toolbox documentation for information.

Summary: FDATool generates analysis error when loading a session
Description: If you load a saved session into a new FDATool, which sets the analysis to an impulse response or a step response, FDATool produces an error.

Summary: finverse function errors when solution contains commas
Description: The Symbolic Toolbox function finverse  errors unnecessarily when the solution contains a comma. For example,syms xf = 4.9/100000*x^4 - 1.1/1000*x^3 - 1.1/10000*x^2 + 1.6/10*x; finverse(f); returnsError using ==> mapleat offset 119, `;` unexpectedError in ==> sym.finverse at 48g = sym(maple('subs',['_tmpy =' x],g));

Summary: smith method doesn't put Smith Charts in subplots
Description: When you call the smith  method to plot a Smith Chart in a figure after using the subplot  function, the smith  method deletes any previous additions to the figure, and the resulting Smith chart covers the entire figure window. In addition, if you call the subplot  function again, the Smith Chart will then be deleted. This behavior occurs because the smith  method does not resize the Smith Chart to match the dimensions of the rectangular pane created by the subplot  function.
Workaround: Use one of the attached MATLAB files as a guide to creating figures that include one or more Smith Chart subplots. To include one Smith Chart in a figure, use smith_subplot.m as a guide. To include multiple Smith Charts in a figure, use smith_subplots.m as a guide.

Summary: Reading or writing 32kB or more data to/from processor memory fails
Description: Read and write operations fail when you try to transfer 32kB or more data in one operation.Writing 32kB or more data to processor memory fails and returns a cryptic error. Here is one write  example.cc = ccsdsp;data = int16( ones(256,256) );write(cc, address_of_symbol, data); ??? Undefined function or method 'address' for input arguments of type 'double'.   Reading 32kB or more data  from the processor memory fails as well and returns a cryptic error, shown in the following example.cc = ccsdsp;data = read(cc, address_of_symbol, 'int16', [256 256]); ??? Error using ==> zerosMaximum variable size allowed by the program is exceeded.
Workaround: Break the read or write operation into smaller transfers so that your program always reads or writes less than 32kB of data per operation.

Summary: cdfread is very slow reading large datasets
Description: The Common Data Format (CDF) import function cdfread  is very slow.  In particular, it reads only one record at a time instead of reading all of the records for a variable at once.  The use of cdfepoch  objects to store time values also hurts performance.  For large datasets, the code takes too long to run.

Summary: When a variable step solver is used, an enabled subsystem that contains an Integrator block might behave improperly
Description: When you configure a model with a variable step solver, an enabled subsystem that contains an Integrator block might behave improperly and produce the wrong results if none of the following are true: The system resets its states on enable and has an Init  method. The system contains blocks with enable or disable methods. The system contains outports that reset their values on disable.

Summary: Why is an infeasible initial population accepted by GA?
Description: If I supply an infeasible initial population with respect to linear and bound constraints, ga  accepts that population and proceeds. Sometimes this results in an infeasible final solution.

Summary: Warnings when checking the Print UIcontrols checkbox in Print Preview
Description: The following warnings are received when checking or unchecking the Print UIcontrols  checkbox in the Print Preview Advanced  panel:Warning: Some objects may not render properly. Use 'print' instead orcall 'noanimate' to set normal drawing modes.> In graphics\private\ppreview>getCData at 503  In graphics\private\ppreview>updateImage at 539  In graphics\private\ppreview>refreshGUI at 632  In graphics\private\ppreview>refreshTimer at 614  In timer.timercb at 31  In timercb at 14  In SegSelect2 at 83
Workaround: The warnings are being caused by the presence of the fullcrosshairpointer on the figure. They come from the attempt to erase that pointer by redrawing in XOR EraseMmode.To work around this issue, do not use the 'fullcrosshair' figure Pointer property.

Summary: deploytool in MATLAB Compiler/Builder suite for MATLAB 7.3 (R2006b) does not save project's Toolbox settings
Description: If you create a project using deploytool , change Toolboxes on Path , then save and re-open it, none of the toolboxes are selected.
Workaround: There are no known workarounds for this issue other than using the mcc command from the MATLAB prompt with the -N and -p flags. Refer to the documentation for more information on these flags.

Summary: When a library fails to load using LoadLibrary or System.Load, Windows pops up an error dialog box.
Description: When loading a library using LoadLibrary or the Java method System.Load, an error dialog will appear if an error occurs.
Workaround: If you are using a version of MATLAB prior to R2007a, perform the following steps to workaround this issue:Download the attached .m and .mat files.Locate your MATLABROOT directory. To retrieve this information from MATLAB, type matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt.Exit MATLAB.Make a backup of the existing files by renaming any .m, .p, and .c files in the matlabroot\toolbox directory with an extension of .old. For example, rename matlabroot\toolbox\filename.m to  matlabroot\toolbox\filename.m.oldCopy the files you downloaded into the directory >matlabroot\toolbox. Restart MATLAB.Issue the following command at the MATLAB Command Prompt:rehash toolboxcache

Summary: Multiple clicks on masked blocks in the library browser can cause a segmentation violation
Description: Occasionally clicking more than three times on masked blocks such as PhysMod or Signal Processing in the library browser to open its dialog box can cause a segmentation violation. Sometimes this can also result in a MATLAB crash.

Summary: Code executes in debug mode but crashes with glibc error in execute mode
Description: When running an application on a Linux machine, it crashes?with the following message *** glibc detected *** free(): invalid next size (fast): 0x090cbb40 *** The crash no longer occurs if the application is executed in debug mode.This is a bug in MATLAB 7.2 (R2006a) and 7.3 (R2006b)?that affects the way the?MATLAB Accelerator handles certain combinations of conditional statements and logical operations. The MathWorks strongly recommends that you upgrade to a newer release.
Workaround: To work around this issue, turn the MATLAB Accelerator off with the following command, and then execute?your program:???feature accel offIf you want to?turn the MATLAB Accelerator back on, enter the following?command at the MATLAB prompt:?? feature accel onIf you have identified which function causes the crash, you can disable the MATLAB Accelerator for just that function by including the following line at the beginning of the function:?? feature scopedaccelenablement off

Summary: Error in uistack: Error using ==> vertcat
Description: When multiple objects are passed to uistack  as a row vector, uistack  fails with the following error: ??? Error using ==> vertcat CAT arguments dimensions are not consistent.
Workaround: To work around the problem, tranpose the row vector, so that it is a column vector.For example, the following code fails:>>uistack([h1 h2 h3],'bottom'); But passing the objects as a column vector does not cause an error. For example:>> uistack([h1;h2;h3], 'bottom');or>> uistack([h1 h2 h3]', 'bottom');

Summary: Errors in the Cylinder Friction block equations
Description: There is an error in the way the first equation, computing the friction force, is implemented in the Cylinder Friction block. As a result, the block produces discontinuities and should not be used in models. In addition, there is an error in the block reference page concerning the second equation, which computes the Coulomb force. The Coulomb force is actually proportional to (p_a + p_b) , not to (p_a - p_b) .
Workaround: Instead of the Cylinder Friction block, you can use the Translational Friction block.

Description: If a user's DICOM data dictionary contains multiple attributes with the same name and dicominfo  is presented with a file that contains both of those attributes, dicominfo  will put information in incorrect fields. For example, the data for "TableSpeed" will go into another field in the MATLAB structure. The errors will cascade from that point on. dicominfo  does not produce any errors. When the data is passed to dicomwrite , it may produce errors. The actual errors that appear vary but frequently look like this: ??? Error using ==> medformats\private\dicom_add_attr>validate_dataAttribute (0020,000D) has wrong data type. or ??? Undefined function or method 'log2' for input arguments of type 'int32'. A number of warnings often accompany calls to dicomwrite : Warning: Attribute (0043,1045) has wrong number of values.Warning: Attribute (0043,1046) has wrong number of values.Warning: Attribute (0043,1043) has wrong number of values.Warning: Attribute (0043,1044) has wrong number of values.Warning: Attribute (0043,1042) has wrong number of values.Warning: Attribute (0043,1041) has wrong number of values.Warning: Attribute (0043,104E) has wrong number of values.Warning: Attribute (0043,1026) has wrong number of values.Warning: Attribute (0043,1016) has wrong number of values.Warning: Attribute (0043,104D) has wrong number of values.Warning: Attribute "TableSpeed" is multiply defined.Warning: Attribute "TemporalResolution" is multiply defined.Warning: Attribute (0043,1040) has wrong number of values.
Workaround: To workaround this issue, replace the version of the dicominfo function in your current installation with the updated version contained in the attached file.NOTE: Your current version must be R2006b.Exit MATLAB.Make a backup copy of the following file. matlabroot\toolbox\images\medformats\dicominfo.mmatlabroot represents the name of your MATLAB installation directory, as returned by the matlabroot function. For example, rename the existing file, adding "old" to the file extension, dicominfo.m.OLD.Download the attached file, extract dicominfo.m, and store it in the directory specified in the previous step.Restart MATLAB.Run the rehash toolbox command at the MATLAB prompt.

Summary: Java exceptions thrown from MATLAB code in a JavaBuilder component are not displayed
Description: When MATLAB-code is compiled into a Java component and executed using Builder for Java, a Java exception may be thrown as a result of some use of the MATLAB Java Interface. The proper MATLAB error message that should be output is not displayed.
Workaround: There is no workaround for this issue.

Summary: Problems running mcc on Solaris 10
Description: The following error occurs when using MATLAB Compiler on Solaris 10:#error: "Compiler or options invalid; UNIX 03 and POSIX.1-2001 applications require the use of c99"
Workaround: To workaround this issue, do the following:    Rename matlabroot/bin/mbuildopts.sh to matlabroot/bin/mbuildopts.sh_original.    Download the attached file mbuildopts.sh in the /bin directory.    Ensure every user runs mbuild -setup from either the shell prompt or MATLAB.This solution allows all users of MATLAB (if installed on the network) to receive the fix by running the last step.If you do not have privileges to write to the MATLAB directory, download the attached file to your $HOME/.matlab/R200XX directory, where XX represents the last two digits of the MATLAB release number.

Summary: applycform: inaccurate output for neutral colors with some profiles
Description: For ICC profiles using a three- or four-dimensional color lookup table, applycform  sometimes produces inaccurate results for neutral (gray) colors.

Summary: Lookup Table (2-D) block: code generation error occurs when using multiple instances of a parameter object
Description: Suppose your model contains a Lookup Table (2-D) block whose Table data  parameter specifies the same Simulink.Parameter  or mpt.Parameter  object that another block's parameter specifies, e.g., the Constant value  parameter of a Constant block. If the Storage class  of the parameter object specifies any setting other than Auto  when you generate code for this model, Real-Time Workshop displays an error similar to the following: Error using ==> rtwgenVariable 'tqrlr_map_orig' is used in conflicting fashionsin run-time parameters of: 'TestTable_first/inherit2,TestTable_first/tqrlr_map'. One of the run-time parametersis specifying that the variable should be output as a matrix,while another is specifying that the variable should beoutput as a vector
Workaround: Avoid using multiple instances of a parameter object associated with Lookup Table (2-D) blocks.

Summary: Too many variables in a workspace causes MATLAB to crash
Description: Too many variables (more than approximately 65535) in a workspace may cause MATLAB to crash.  This can happen in very large files, or with a loop in which eval'ed commands cause the creation of many distinct variables.
Workaround: Use indexed cells or arrays rather than many different variables.

Summary: Error running geneontologydemo
Description: The demo geneontologydemo in Bioinformatics Toolbox returns an error. While running the demo, the following error occurs: %%%BEGIN ERROR%%% ??? Subscript indices must either be real positive integers or logicals. Error in ==> geneontologydemo at 181     goid = getrelatives(GO,goid); %%%END ERROR%%%

Summary: It is not possible to constrain pan, zoom or rotate3d modes on a vector of handles
Description: The documentation for zoom  indicates that it should be possible to use the setallowaxeszoom  function with a vector of axes handles:  setAllowAxesZoom(h,axes,flag)  Calling the function setallowaxeszoom  on the zoom object, h , with a vector of axes handles, axes, and a logical scalar, flag, will either allow or disallow a zoom operation on the axes objects. Using a vector of handles yields the following error:??? Error using ==> handleCannot convert to handle. This problem also exists for the setallowaxesrotate  and setallowaxespan  functions.

Summary: Buffer block can return wrong results on TI 2000 processors and other targets
Description: The Buffer block makes incorrect assumptions about the results of the sizeof function on different targets. For example, it assumes that sizeof(int16) = 2, which is incorrect for TI 2000 processors. The result of these assumptions is that buffer pointers can point to incorrect memory locations and thereby return wrong results.  This problem can occur for any target that has a sizeof function that returns a number of minimum addressable units that is different from a byte, such as the TIC2000 and TIC5000 targets.
Workaround: To work around this problem in R2006b, use the attachments to replace the following installed files: MATLABROOT/toolbox/dspblks/dspmex/sdsprebuff2.mexw32MATLABROOT/toolbox/dspblks/dspmex/tlc_c/sdsprebuff2.tlc

Summary: pdist segfaults for sparse data input
Description: Calling pdist  with a data input argument that is a sparse matrix produces a segmentation fault.
Workaround: You can convert your data matrix from sparse to full before calling pdist. For example:D = pdist(full(X),'Euclidean');This may not be desirable if the data are high-dimensional, because the resulting memory requirement may be too large.

Summary: Selecting "zero initialization" optimizations when generating S-function wrappers can cause unexpected simulation results
Description: The options Remove root level I/O zero initialization  and Remove internal state zero initialization  on the Optimization  pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box allow you to generate code without explicitly initializing root-level I/O data structures and global data structures to zero, which avoids redundant code on targets or in applications that provide zeroing through some other mechanism. However, if an ERT S-function wrapper is generated for code with either or both of these options selected, states will not be initialized.  This can produce simulation results that are different than expected.
Workaround: You can work around this bug in either of two ways:Clear the Remove root level I/O zero initialization and Remove internal state zero initialization options when generating code for an S-function.Issue the MATLAB command clear mex between simulation runs.  This unloads the shared library and causes the states to be initialized.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Embedded MATLAB Function blocks generate uncompilable code for the TI C6000 or Tornado targets
Description: When used with Asynchronous Task or Idle Task blocks, Embedded MATLAB Function blocks generate code (with Real-Time Workshop Embedded Coder) that calls functions before they are declared. This causes the C compiler to generate an error.This bug only impacts users of TI C6000 or Tornado targets.

Summary: Removing all samples from a uniform Simulink time series produces an error
Description: Using time series tools (tstool ) or the delsample  method to remove all samples from a Simulink time series will fail with the following command line error: ??? Error using ==> timeseries.timeseries>timeseries.set.Time Length of time and data do not match
Workaround: From the command line:Modify the Simulink time series (ts) so that it becomes non-uniformly sampled by reassigning the time vector:time = ts.Time;time(1) = time(2)-ts.TimeInfo.Increment/2;ts.Time = time;Then remove all the data using,ts.delsample('Index',(1:ts.TimeInfo.Length)');From time series tools (tstool):Display the Simulink time series in a time plot, enter time interval selection mode from the figure, and right click to remove all the points.

Summary: Compiled applications with TIMER object exit prematurely
Description: I want to create a timer object inside a MATLAB application using MATLAB Compiler.  However, the application quits even though the timer is still running.  I receive the following warning:Warning: The following classes still have existing instances that cannot be cleared: timer (1 instance)
Workaround: When the main function finishes in an application that has been generated with MATLAB Compiler, the program will terminate unless a MATLAB figure windows is visible.If you are running a MATLAB version earlier than R2007a, workaround this issue by keeping the TIMER object running. Insert a loop at the end of the main function as shown in the following example:function timerTesti = 1;t = timer('TimerFcn', @time_cbf, 'Period', 1, 'ExecutionMode', 'fixedRate');start(t);if isdeployed    while(isvalid(t))        pause(0.5)    endend    function time_cbf(obj, event)        t = obj;        disp(i);        i = i + 1;        if i>10            stop(obj);            delete(obj);        end    endendCompile this program using the following command:mcc -m -v timerTest.m

Summary: M-Lint does not consistently display message "Terminate statement with semicolon to suppress output"
Description: For MATLAB-file scripts, M-Lint does not display the message Terminate statement with semicolon to suppress output  when the MATLAB-file has three or more cells (that is, lines that begin with %%, which are used for publishing and rapid code iteration).

Summary: Large sequential fwrite transfers to UDP objects might lose data
Description: UDP does not guarantee the delivery or order of datagrams.  There are precautions that can help improve the reliability of UDP communication. Instrument Control Toolbox drops some of the received UDP packets with larger fwrite  sizes. If the fwrite  transfer size is approximately 500 bytes or less, this behavior is not observed.While you might observe the dropped packets during communication with other UDP sources, the problem is also observable between two MATLAB sessions as follows:Receiving session:  u = udp('192.168.0.1','LocalPort',3000,'InputBufferSize',50000);  fopen(u); Sending session:  u = udp('192.168.0.2', 3000,'OutputBufferSize',50000);  fopen(u)  a = uint8(ones(1,2048));  for i=1:10,      fwrite(u,a,'uint8');  end
Workaround: To avoid data loss, use shorter transfers with fwrite, as shown in the following code:  u = udp('192.168.0.2', 3000,'OutputBufferSize',50000);  fopen(u)  a = uint8(ones(1,512));  for i=1:40,      fwrite(u,a,'uint8');  end

Summary: Incorrect code generation: Error when building code for model with Stateflow chart and function-call subsystem
Description: The Real-Time Workshop build process might generate uncompileable C code for models with Stateflow charts under all of the following conditions: The Stateflow chart is in a reusable subsystem. The chart has a function-call output event bound to a state. The function-call subsystem contains a Function-Call Generator block which drives a nested Function-Call Subsystem block. The Real-Time Workshop system code  menu of the nested function-call subsystem is set to Reusable function .  The generated C code does not contain function-call arguments for the nested function-call subsystem. The Real-Time Workshop build process fails to compile the incorrect code and generates an error similar to the following: model_name .c:242: error: 'model_name _M' undeclared (first use in this function) model_name .c:242: error: (Each undeclared identifier is reported only once model_name .c:242: error: for each function it appears in.) make: *** [model_name .o] Error 1
Workaround: Set Real-Time Workshop system code menu of the nested function-call subsystem to Inline or Function.

Summary: Enable function: model initialization incorrectly calls enable function
Description: Real-time Workshop Embedded Coder calls the enable function of a block inside an enabled subsystem during both the model initialization and step functions. The enable function should only be called in the model step function if the parent enabled subsystem is transitioning from disabled to enabled.
Workaround: If you are using a version earlier than R2007a, there are no known workarounds.

Summary: Segmentation fault using PowerPC MATLAB on Intel Mac with Mac OS X 10.4.8
Description: After upgrading to Mac OS X 10.4.8, attempted to start a PowerPC version of MATLAB using matlab -nojvm . A segmentation fault (segfault) occurs while loading the Apple X11 library, libXinerama.dylib .
Workaround: The MathWorks does not support PowerPC versions of MATLAB on Intel-based Macs.  Some customers have successfully used the -nojvm flag to run PowerPC versions of MATLAB in more a limited fashion.A workaround is to run MATLAB with both the -nojvm and the -nodisplay flags.

Summary: The Selector block can make MATLAB hang during Simulink model compilation
Description: The Selector block might hang MATLAB when it is in Matrix Selection mode. This can occur when the following two conditions are met: The index for one of the matrix dimensions (row/column) is set to -1  (i.e. select all elements). The index source for the other dimension (column/row) is set to  external  and the dimensions of this external index port are not easily deducible from the block connected to this port. For example, the port is connected to a Ground block.
Workaround: To work around this issue, use one of the following:Consider connecting a Signal Specification block to the external index port of the Selector block and specify the signal dimensions. For the dimension that has an index of -1 (i.e. select all elements), consider modifying the indices to 1:5 (assuming that the dimension has a size of 5).

Summary: Referenced model may fail to build when logging certain virtual bus signals
Description: Within a referenced model, if a virtual bus signal is marked for logging, the model may fail to build if one or more component signals originates from a root Inport block that is not testpointed.
Workaround: Testpoint any signal that originates from a root Inport block that is connected to a virtual bus signal that is marked for logging.

Summary: Real-Time Workshop builds fail on Windows when matlabroot is UNC path
Description: When MATLAB is started from a UNC path (such as when matlabroot  is a UNC path of the form \\server \path \matlab ) on a Windows system, Real-Time Workshop builds error out with a message that includes the text: Error using ==> dos DOS commands may not be executed when the current directory is a UNC pathname.
Workaround: To work around this bug in R2006b, perform the following steps:Note:  In the following instructions, matlabroot represents your MATLAB installation root, as returned by the MATLAB command matlabroot.        In your MATLAB installation tree, rename the following files, for example, by adding .old to the end of each file name.            matlabroot/toolbox/rtw/rtw/@RTW/transformPaths.m        matlabroot/toolbox/rtw/rtw/@RTW/transformPaths.p                    Download the attached files and place them at the locations indicated below:            matlabroot/toolbox/rtw/rtw/getPathName.mexw32        matlabroot/toolbox/rtw/rtw/getPathName.mexw32.csf        matlabroot/toolbox/rtw/rtw/getPathName.mexw64        matlabroot/toolbox/rtw/rtw/getPathName.mexw64.csf        matlabroot/toolbox/rtw/rtw/@RTW/transformPaths.m        matlabroot/toolbox/rtw/rtw/@RTW/transformPaths.p                    Restart MATLAB.            After restarting MATLAB, issue the following command at the MATLAB prompt:    rehash toolboxcache    ?

Summary: KSDENSITY may enter infinite loop when evaluating inverse CDF
Description: For some combinations of input data, using KSDENSITY to evaluate the inverse cumulative distribution function with the default normal kernel results in an infinite loop, and the function does not return.
Workaround: Use the Epanechnikov kernel instead of the default normal kernel. For example, [f,xi] = ksdensity(x,'kernel','epanechnikov')

Summary: Incorrect warning message about empty source code file
Description: If a subsystem specifies "Real-Time Workshop file name options", but any child system does not specify this option, the following incorrect warning message appears: Warning:  Real-Time Workshop: The subsystem 'System Name' specifies Real-Time Workshop file name options. However, no code is generated for this subsystem. The specified source code file will not be created.
Workaround: To prevent the incorrect warning message, use the attached TLC file to replace:matlab/rtw/c/tlc/mw/commonbodlib.tlc.

Summary: Autocorrelation block does not apply Unity at zero-lag correctly for multichannel signals
Description: The Autocorrelation block does not work correctly for multichannel signals when Unity at zero-lag  is selected for the Scaling  parameter. When Unity at zero-lag  is selected, the block is supposed to normalize the estimate of the autocorrelation for each  channel in the signal so that the zero-lag sum is identically 1. However, the block only performs this action for one channel in a multichannel signal.
Workaround: To work around this issue, use Submatrix blocks to split a multichannel signal into single-channel signals, and then feed each signal into separate Autocorrelation blocks. For example, for a two-channel input signal, add two Submatrix blocks to your model. Configure the first Submatrix block to output the first column of the signal and the second Submatrix block to output the second column. Then feed each of these signals into two separate Autocorrelation blocks.

Summary: The Pad block does not accept NaN for a pad value
Description: The Pad block does not accept NaN for a pad value. This is a regression from previous releases.
Workaround: Use the attached files to fix this problem. There is a file for each platform supported by MATLAB. The platform is indicated by the file extension. Download the file for your platform into the directorymatlabroot/toolbox/dspblks/dspmexwhere matlabroot is the path returned when you type matlabroot at the MATLAB command line.If you are running MATLAB on multiple platforms or are not sure which file to download, download all the files into the same directory.

Summary: Edge detection, Surveillance and Lane Detection demos that use RTDX error out when load after build options is checked on Code Composer Studio
Description: Edge Detection, Surveillance and Lane Detection demos that use RTDX to communicate with the host computer error out when Load Program After Build option is set on Code Composer Studio. These demos have a "Build Reload & Run" button on the body of the respective Simulink models that activates a script automating the build and run process. The script assumes that the Load Program After Build options is not checked so it explicitly loads the excutable to the target after build. When the Load Program After Build option is checked, the sequence of operations performed by the script causes the executable to be loaded twice. This in turn results in RTDX channels being enabled while the executable is being loaded to the target. The net result is that the demos script times out while waiting for an RTDX read or write operation.
Workaround: To run demos using RTDX or High Speed RTDX, make sure that the Load after Build option is not checked on your CCS window. To verify, click  Option -> Customize -> Program/Project Load

Summary: Query Instrument block might require LocalPort configuration to read from UDP devices
Description: The Simulink Query Instrument block does not provide a direct way to specify the LocalPort , which might be required to read from some devices. When a LocalPort  setting is required, it appears as if the instrument never responds and the query eventually times out.
Workaround: To work around this problem, set the LocalPort at the MATLAB command line. Create the UDP object at the MATLAB command line with the desired RemotePort and LocalPort property values. >> u = udp(host, remotePortValue, 'LocalPort', localPortValue);Double-click the Query Instrument block in Simulink to open the block parameters dialog.In the dialog, select the option Use interface object from MATLAB workspace, and type u in the Workspace Object text field.

Summary: Peak Finder block does not propagate signal dimensions correctly
Description: The Peak Finder block does not propagate signal dimensions from its output to its input correctly. When the input signal dimensions for this block are not specified, the block might throw the following error:Error reported by S-function 'sdsppeaks' in 'buffer_sigprop/Peak Finder': Output must be 1-D or 2-D.
Workaround: To work around this issueInsert a Simulink Signal Specification block at the input to the Peak Finder block in the modelSpecify the expected dimensions for the input signal to the Peak Finder block in the Dimensions parameter of the Signal Specification block

Summary: Running bwtraceboundary produces Out-of-memory error
Description: If you specify an initial step direction that is inconsistent with the trace direction, and if you are tracing an object with a hole, then bwtraceboundary  may cause an out-of-memory error.This example illustrates the problem:B = bwtraceboundary([ 0 1 0; 1 0 1; 1 1 0], [3 1], 'E') The default trace direction is clockwise, but the specified initial step direction of 'E'  is in the counterclockwise direction.  Normally, bwtraceboundary  automatically detects such a problem, but in this case, the 0-valued pixel at (2,2) prevents the detection.  As a result, the trace operation does not terminate correctly, resulting in the out-of-memory error.
Workaround: To workaround this issue, specify the trace direction explicitly.  For example:B = bwtraceboundary([0 1 0; 1 0 1; 1 1 0], [3 1], 'E', ...                    8, Inf, 'counterclockwise')

Summary: Synchronous Machine block issues error when Simulate Saturation option is selected
Description: If you try to run a model containing a Synchronous Machine block with the Simulate Saturation  parameter checked, you could get an error similar to: Error in 'MyModel/Synchronous Machine pu Standard': Initialization commands cannot be evaluated.  MATLAB error message: Operands to the || and && operators must be convertible to logical scalar values.
Workaround: To work around this issue, download the attached version of the SynchronousMachineParam.p file. Here are the steps:   Quit MATLAB   Rename the following file:  $MATLAB\toolbox\physmod\powersys\powersys\private\SynchronousMachineParam.p (where $MATLAB is the MATLAB root directory on your machine, as returned by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB Command Prompt.)  Rename the file to SynchronousMachineParam.p.old  Download the attached file. Place the new file in the same directory mentioned in step 2.  Restart MATLAB.

Summary: The output voltage of the Ideal Switch block is not square when controlled by a Pulse Generator
Description: You may notice that the falling edge of the Ideal Switch block voltage is not sharp when the switch device starts conducing, especially if you control the Ideal Switch block with a Pulse Generator block. Instead, the fall time appears to be spread out over the remainder of the cycle. Note that the simulation results are correct, even if the measured voltage does not look square. The fact is that two simulation points need to be generated by the simulation around the falling edge of the output voltage signal to make it look perfectly square.
Workaround: You can connect a Derivative block in series with a Hit-Crossing block to the Pulse Generator block that is controlling the Ideal Switch block.

Summary: Preferences directory created in incorrect (and usually shared) location on Windows
Description: In certain cases, MATLAB creates its preferences directory in an incorrect location, which is typically the shared directory above the startup directory. When that occurs, users share a preference file instead of each having their own.MATLAB tries to create the preferences directory in the Application Data directory within a user's profile location. If the path to the Application Data directory points to a server and contains a UNC pathname, MATLAB does not create the preferences directory in the Application Data directory as expected.
Workaround: To work around this problemMap the server\directory referenced in the UNC pathname to a drive letter.Define the registry entry for the Application Data directory using the drive letter instead of the UNC pathname.Alternatively, create the preference directory on the server and MATLAB will use it. For example, create\\server\users\username\Application Data\MathWorks\MATLAB\R2006b

Summary: Opening models that contain library links may cause Simulink to crash or hang
Description: Opening a model that contains a library link may cause Simulink either to hang or to crash. If Simulink crashes, it produces a stack trace similar to the following: Stack Trace:  [0] libmwgui.dll:_get_any_object_type(0, 0x0dec9c60 "??$_Copy_backward_opt@PAU?$pair@..", 0x00cdbff8, 0x7a026ca0) + 6 bytes  [1] simulink.dll:bool __cdecl sluPortSupportsSigProp(struct slPort_tag const *)(0x0dec9c60 "??$_Copy_backward_opt@PAU?$pair@..", 0x00cdc0b8 "X:;", 0, 0x00cdc018 "???") + 16 bytes  [2] simulink.dll:struct slErrMsg_tag * __cdecl sp_SigPropStr(struct slPort_tag *,char const *)(0x0dec9c60 "??$_Copy_backward_opt@PAU?$pair@..", 3881560, 0x0de4ca40, 0x0dee33e0) + 16 bytes  [3] simulink.dll:struct slErrMsg_tag * __cdecl OutportGetIconDisplayStrLabelPart(struct slBlock_tag const *,char * *,bool *)(0x0de4ca40, 0x00cdc0b8 "X:;", 0x00cdc0c6, 0x0de4545c) + 146 bytes  [4] simulink.dll:struct slErrMsg_tag * __cdecl DrawInputOutputPortLabels(struct slBlock_tag const *,struct WinRec_tag *,struct mwrect const *)(0x0de45420, 0x0de4545c, 0x0dee33e0, 0x0de4545c) + 1371 bytes  [5] simulink.dll:struct slErrMsg_tag * __cdecl SubsystemDrawIconFcn(struct slBlock_tag const *,struct WinRec_tag *,struct mwrect const *)(0x0de45420, 0x0dee33e0, 0x0de4545c, 0x0fdf0e20) + 201 bytes  [6] simulink.dll:struct slErrMsg_tag * __cdecl BlockDrawIcon(struct slBlock_tag *,struct WinRec_tag *,struct mwrect const *)(0x530405b3, 0x0dee33e0, 0x0de4545c, 0x0fdf0e20) + 355 bytes   ... This issue occurs only if the library block to which your model links Contains Inport or Outport blocks whose Icon display  parameters specify either Signal name  or Port number and signal name  Propagates its signal names (i.e., the Show propagated signals  option on the Signal Properties dialog box specifies either on  or all )
Workaround: In library blocks linked to your model, specify Port number for the Icon display parameter associated with Inport and Outport blocks.

Summary: Using TriggerRepeat = 1 with NI USB-9201, USB-9215A, or USB-9221 generates runtime errors and NI-DAQmx errors
Description: If you use a National Instruments USB-9201, USB-9215A, or USB-9221 with the following code:ai = analoginput('nidaq','Dev1');addchannel(ai, 0);ai.SampleRate = 5000;ai.SamplesPerTrigger = 5000;ai.TriggerRepeat = 1;start(ai);wait(ai,3);data1 = getdata(ai); % Get first trigger's worth of data, 5000 samplesdata2 = getdata(ai); % Get second trigger's worth of data, another 5000 samples  A popup error message saysWindows C++ Run time library run time errorThis application has requested the Runtime to terminate it in an unusual way. Please contact the application's support team for more information. or one of these errors in the MATLAB window saysError event occurred at 15:59:56 for the object: nidaqmxDev1-AI.NIDAQmx error : Physical channel specified does not exist on this device.Refer to the documentation for channels available on this device.Task Name: MWDAT0004Status Code: -200170 orNIDAQmx error : The software has entered an unknown state - usually as a resultof a cascade failure induced by an unexpected series of state inputs. Theoperation could not be completed as specified and you should immediatelyterminate all further transactions if you are able to do so. Task Name: MWDAT0004  Status Code: -50150
Workaround: The workaround is to set TriggerRepeat to 0, and set SamplesPerTrigger to the number of samples you would have gotten with a higher TriggerRepeat value. For example,ai.SampleRate = 5000;ai.SamplesPerTrigger = 10000;ai.TriggerRepeat = 0;start(ai);wait(ai,3);data= getdata(ai); % Will get all data, 10000 samples

Summary: Selecting an unavailable signal for the Floating Scope results in seg fault.
Description: Selecting a signal from a Floating Scope while a simulation is running can cause MATLAB to generate a segmentation fault under Linux.
Workaround: Uncheck the Signal storage reuse box to prevent the segmentation fault from occurring. This is found in the Simulation and code generation pane of the Optimization menu.From the Simulation toolbar, select the Configuration Parameters menuClick on Optimization to display the Simulation and code generation paneUncheck the Signal storage reuse boxClick OK to accept this change and close the dialog.

Summary: Subsystem functions: sometimes inlined when specified as noninlined
Description: Under certain conditions, Real-Time Workshop inlines the enable, disable , and initialize  functions for a subsystem, even if the subsystem's Real-Time Workshop system code  block parameter is set to Function .

Summary: Repeated simulations of a model that contains Model blocks may take progressively longer
Description: Each simulation of a model that contains Model blocks may take longer than the previous simulation. This problem occurs when Simulink checks referenced models for structural changes that would cause the simulation target to be rebuilt, but finds no structural changes.
Workaround: The following steps avoid this problem by disabling the check for changes to referenced models:Perform an Update Diagram on the top model to make sure that all simulation targets of referenced models are up to date.Make no subsequent changes to referenced models that would require their simulation targets to be rebuilt.Set Configuration Parameters > Model Referencing > Rebuild options to Never.Note: Simulink will ignore changes to referenced models until you perform an Update Diagram with Rebuild Options set to a value other than Never.

Summary: For large scale models, xPC Target build process is slower than in previous releases
Description: For very large Simulink models, using xPC Target to build an xPC Target executable takes significantly more time.
Workaround: To work around this issue and improve the time to build an xPC Target executable for large scale models:  Quit MATLAB.  Rename the following files $MATLAB\toolbox\rtw\targets\xpc\target\build\tlc\biosignew.tlc $MATLAB\toolbox\rtw\targets\xpc\target\build\tlc\ptnew.tlcto $MATLAB\toolbox\rtw\targets\xpc\target\build\tlc\biosignewOld.tlc $MATLAB\toolbox\rtw\targets\xpc\target\build\tlc\ptnewOld.tlc(where $MATLAB is the MATLAB root directory on your machine, as returned by typing   matlabroot   at the MATLAB command prompt.) Download the attached file. Place this file in a temporary folder.Unzip this file.Place the new files (biosignew.tlc and ptnew.tlc) in the same directory mentioned in step 2.  Restart MATLAB.

Summary: Data Acquisition Toolbox Simulink Blocks do not work with Advantech boards.
Description: The Data Acquisition Toolbox Simulink Blocks do not work correctly with the Advantech boards. Trying to load the Data Acquisition Toolbox block library produces an error if Advantech boards are used.
Workaround: Please contact Technical Support for details on the workaround.

Summary: Assertion when loading MAT-file that contains anonymous function handles without having the function declaration present
Description: If you have an MATLAB-file program that saves its workspace to a MAT-file and this workspace contains one or more handles to anonymous functions, MATLAB generates an assertion failure if you attempt to load from that MAT-file without the MATLAB-file that created the handles being in the same directory or on the MATLAB path. For example, the following function saves the handle to anonymous function sqr  to a file sqrmat.mat  in the current working directory:    function save_sqrfun   sqr = @(x)x^2   save sqrmat If you run the function, saving the anonymous function handle to the sqrmat  MAT-file, move the function save_sqrfun  that created the handle to the next higher directory, and then attempt to load from the MAT-file,    save_sqrfun   movefile save_sqrfun.m ..   load sqrmat MATLAB displays this warning:    Warning: Could not find appropriate function on path loading function handle C:\matlab\testdir\save_sqrfun.m>@(x)x^2 This bug may also manifest itself as a seg-fault.
Workaround: There is no workaround.
