Summary: Switching between multiple installations of MATLAB and Link for CCS causes timeout errors
Description: If you have multiple installations of MATLAB and Link for Code Composer Studio, only the most recently installed version of MATLAB and Link for Code Composer Studio works.  For example, if you had R2006a+ installed and then you installed R2006b, only R2006b would work. Commands like reset  and build  in R2006a+ cause timeout errors.
Workaround: To fix this problem, this workaround to each of your 2006a+ and 2006b MATLAB installations.Note This patch applies only to MATLAB R2006a+ and R2006b.Download attachment_339263_20061024.zip and extract the following patch files: - lfc_patch_installer.m- lfc_patch_339263.zipClose all versions of MATLAB and Code Composer Studio.Start one version of MATLAB (R2006a+ or R2006b).Change the directory to the location of the patch files.Enter the following command at the MATLAB prompt to install the patch: lfc_patch_installer('lfc_patch_339263.zip', 'install')Close MATLAB.Open the other MATLAB version and install the patch using the same command.After successfully installing the patch, close MATLAB.Installing the patch fixes the problem of switching between MATLAB installations by automatically registering the plugins when you switch between versions.Note When you switch between versions of MATLAB (R2006a+ and R2006b), this patch attempts to register the plugins. To accommadate the registration process, you will be asked to close all instances of Code Compose Studio (CCS).

Summary: Problems using fopen in a japanese named directory.
Description: Calling fopen from a compiled application may fail when run on a non-English installation of Windows.  The error given is that one or more outputs have not been assigned.
Workaround: There is no workaround for this issue.

Summary: MEX -setup and MBUILD -setup save default options files in the?wrong PREFDIR
Description: There is a bug in MEX and?MBUILD where these programs incorrectly determine the location of PREFDIR.? Consequently, default options files are not saved in MATLAB's?PREFDIR.?   ? MATLAB determines PREFDIR using the environment variable APPDATA, while MEX and MBUILD use the environment variable USERPROFILE.??Also,?"Application Data" was hard coded in MEX and MBUILD and this causes problems with International versions of Windows that translate the string "Application Data".

Summary: Real-Time Workshop omits saturation code for fixed-point casts with particular non-power-of-two slopes
Description: Real-Time Workshop incorrectly omits saturation code for fixed-point cast operations in which all of the following apply: Saturation is enabled. The net bias  is zero. The idealized net slope  is slightly less than specific powers of two, which Simulink Fixed Point quantizes by rounding toward those powers of two. The following table shows the quantized net slope values and the corresponding signedness of the input and output data types that trigger this issue.  Input Output Net Slope  Unsigned Unsigned 2.0  Unsigned Signed 1.0  Signed Unsigned 4.0  Signed Signed 2.0     Net slope is Si/So , i.e., the input slope divided by the output slope. Net bias is (Bi - Bo)/So , i.e., the difference between the input and output biases divided by the output slope. If all of these conditions apply, Real-Time Workshop omits the saturation code that should be generated for a fixed-point cast. Note that this issue might affect many blocks that perform cast operations. For example, Sum blocks and Data Type Conversion blocks frequently perform casts. A very common type of fixed-point scaling is binary-point-only scaling, for which the bias is zero and the slope is an exact power of two. If a block uses only power of two scaling, its net slope will be an exact power of two. Note that such net slopes avoid the bug described here.
Workaround: To work around this issue, avoid any of the necessary conditions that the previous section describes. For instance, consider implementing either of the following:Try slightly adjusting the slope of the data types involved. A modest adjustment in the input or output slope can increase or decrease the idealized net slope. If the idealized net slope increases enough to the exact power of two, you will avoid this bug. Alternately, if the idealized net slope decreases enough, it will quantize to a value that is below the exact power of two, enabling you to avoid the bug.For example, suppose a signal has a data type with a slope slightly less than an exact power of two. You should investigate whether you can increase that signal's scaling to the exact power of two. Otherwise, determine whether you can decrease the slope farther away from the exact power of two.Try increasing the word length of the input signal. Best precision scaling is used to quantize the idealized net slope to the same word length and signedness as the input signal. A larger input word length means the net slope can be represented with greater precision.Suppose a particular idealized net slope quantizes to an exact power of two for a given word length. If you increase the word length enough, that same idealized net slope will quantize to a more precise value that is not an exact power of two.The attached files include an example function that demonstrates the methods by which blocks subject to this bug can be identified.

Summary: When Simulink HDL Coder is not installed, changes to Configuration Parameters dialog settings cause errors
Description: If a Simulink model is created in a MATLAB session that has Simulink HDL Coder installed and then loaded in a MATLAB session that does not have Simulink HDL Coder installed, any changes made to the Configuration Parameters dialog settings (for example, Solver settings) will result in an error.
Workaround: To work around this problem in R2006b, replace the file:MATLABROOT/toolbox/shared/hdlshared/@hdlcoderui/hdlconfigDlgAction.pwith the attached version of this file.

Summary: Zooming on log-scale plots can work incorrectly
Description: When zooming in on an axes whose 'XScale'  or 'YScale'  properties are set to 'log' , such as plots generated using the loglog , semilogx , or semilogy  functions, zooming in by single clicking or by typing the syntax zoom(factor) will result in incorrect zooms. Symptoms can include failure to zoom, blank plots, or incorrect scaling.
Workaround: To work around this issue, use the following steps:Rename the file $MATLAB/toolbox/matlab/graph2d/zoom.m to $MATLAB/toolbox/matlab/graph2d/zoom.m.old, where $MATLAB is the root directory of your MATLAB installation as returned by typingmatlabrootat the command prompt.Download the attached file zoom.m to the above location.At the MATLAB command prompt, type the commandrehash toolboxcache

Summary: Digital DATCOM Forces and Moments block creates pitching moments that are twice expected values
Description: The Digital DATCOM pitching moment already contains the cross product contribution of lift forces and their distance from the center of gravity. However, the Digital DATCOM Forces and Moments block calculates the cross product and adds it to the pitching moment. This creates a pitching moment that is twice the expected value.
Workaround: Change the Digital DATCOM structure in the Digital DATCOM Forces and Moments block as follows:Save existing values of xcp in the Digital DATCOM structure.digdat.xcp_old = xcp;Set the xcp values in the structure to zero.digdat.xcp = zeros(sizes(x.xcp));

Summary: Simulink may not save an R2006b model correctly as an R2006a model when the model contains a Reshape block.
Description: Simulink does not save an R2006b model correctly as an R2006a or earlier model when the model contains a Reshape block having the Output dimensionality  parameter set to Customize , and the parameter Output dimensions  is defined by a variable.The reshape block will be missing when the model is opened with a version of Simulink earlier than R2006b.When saving, the following message appears in the MATLAB command window:Warning: Unable to fully convert model.
Workaround: Replace the file <matlabroot>/toolbox/simulink/simulink/slsaveassup.m with the one attached. Note: This file provides a fix for R2006b only. It may not work correctly with any other version of Simulink.

Summary: Expression too complicated error: compilation can fail during Real-Time Workshop builds
Description: Real-Time Workshop might create expressions with a depth greater than 8 while the lcc compiler needs a depth of 8 or less. When this condition occurs, the lcc compiler runs out of registers causing compilation to fail with the error Expression too complicated."

Summary: Plots generated by plotyy inside a UIPanel may not align properly
Description: When creating a plot using the plotyy  function in an axes inside a UIPanel, for example,hPanel = uipanel('Units','Normalized','Position',[0 0 0.5 0.5]);hAxes = axes('Parent',hPanel);[ax,chil1,chil2] = plotyy(hAxes,1:10,rand(1,10),1:10,rand(1,10)); the second plot may not be aligned with the first.
Workaround: Manually set the 'Parent' property of the axes returned by the plotyy function, as follows:hPanel = uipanel('Units','Normalized','Position',[0 0 0.5 0.5]);hAxes = axes('Parent',hPanel);[ax,chil1,chil2] = plotyy(hAxes,1:10,rand(1,10),1:10,rand(1,10));set(ax,'Parent',hPanel);

Summary: Using xPC Target command objects to upload large data is very slow
Description: Using xPC Target command objects to upload large data is very slow. For example, using the xPC Target FTP object to upload a large file takes a very long time.

Summary: Why does MATLAB crash when attempting to use frame grabbers from Dalsa Coreco?
Description: I am trying to use a frame grabber from Dalsa Coreco, such as the x64-iPro. I am able to acquire data from the device using start  or preview data from the device using preview , but sometimes I get a warning: Warning: Message from the Sapera low-level driverSapProcessing::Execute => Cannot be called before the Create method  After I receive this warning MATLAB crashes. This is a bug in the toolbox.

Summary: Asynchronous subsystems: TLC error occurs when used inside an enabled subsystem
Description: If a multirate model includes an asynchronous function-call subsystem that is nested inside an enabled subsystem which has a single periodic rate, Real-Time Workshop treats the enabled subsystem as a single-rate subsystem and fails to build the model due to a TLC error. The following error messages are displayed:  Error: File: /rtw/c/tlc/mw/commonbodlib.tlc Line: 2204 Column: 40 The argument for an [] operation must be a repeated scope symbol, a vector, or a matrix Error: File: /rtw/c/tlc/mw/commonbodlib.tlc Line: 2206 Column: 38 The == and != operators can only be used to compare values of the same type Error:
Workaround: You can force the code generator to recognize that the enabled subsystem is a multirate subsystem by adding a periodic sample time that is different than any other block sample time in the enabled subsystem.

Summary: Video Viewer window does not remain at true size
Description: If you right-click a Video Viewer window and select Set Display To True Size, the window does not always remain at true size if you select other options from this shortcut menu.
Workaround: Download the attached attachment_338348_20061006.zip file and extract its contents in the matlabroot/toolbox/vipblks/vipblks directory.

Summary: Get error when using putvalue on National Instruments PXI-2566.
Description: When using putvalue  on a PXI=2566, I receive an error:dio = digitalio('nidaq','PXI1Slot4') % for PXI-2566 addline(dio,0:15,'out') putvalue(dio.Line(9),1) ??? NIDAQmx error : Physical channel specified does not exist on this device. Refer to the documentation for channels available on this device. Device: PXI1Slot4 Physical Channel Name: port1  Task Name: MWDAT0041  Status Code: -200170
Workaround: The workaround is to change the configuration file for your device. To change the configuration file of the device: Quit MATLAB. Change to the directory $MATLAB/toolbox/daq/daq/private, where $MATLAB is the directory where you installed MATLAB. Back up the file mwnidaqmx.ini to another name such as mwnidaqmx.bak. Open mwnidaqmx.ini and the following entry:[PXI-2566]Change the lines: digitalPortSize = 8,8 digitalPortCap = 2,2to:digitalPortSize = 16 digitalPortCap = 2Save and close the file. Restart MATLAB.

Summary: Error using 'wgtfun' argument in STATSET
Description: STATSET may produce an error when using a function handle for the 'wgtfun'  argument.
Workaround: Use a function name, rather than a function handle, for the 'wgtfun' argument.

Summary: Bug fixes for External Mode support of xPC
Description: Several bug fixes were made to External Mode for xPC.
Workaround: Copy the attached file to matlabroot/toolbox/rtw/targets/xpc/xpc.

Summary: The time series object plot method does not display events correctly for absolute time vectors
Description: If a time series object with an absolute time vector contains events with a non-empty StartDate  property, those events will not be displayed by the time series object plot method. Conversely, if a time series object with an absolute time vector contains events with an empty StartDate  property, those events may be displayed for certain values of the event Time property. For example the following code will create a time series object with an absolute time vector and add an event with a valid start date. When the time series plot method is called, the event does not display: time = {'9/22/2006 9PM',...        '9/22/2006 10PM',...        '9/22/2006 11PM',...        '9/23/2006 12AM',...        '9/23/2006 1AM',...        '9/23/2006 2AM'}; data  = (1:6)'; ts = timeseries(data,time); ts = addevent(ts,tsdata.event('Event1','9/22/2006 10PM')); plot(ts);   Conversely, in the following code the time series plot method shows an event, even though the event has an empty StartDate: time = {'9/22/2006 9PM',...        '9/22/2006 10PM',...        '9/22/2006 11PM',...        '9/23/2006 12AM',...        '9/23/2006 1AM',...        '9/23/2006 2AM'}; data  = (1:6)'; ts = timeseries(data,time);ts = addevent(ts,tsdata.event('Event1',datenum('9/22/2006 10PM')));
Workaround: The following work around applies for MATLAB 7.2 and later.When adding events to time series with absolute time vectors, the events should have an empty StartDate property, a Units property set to 'days', and a Time property equal to the serial date number of the event.For example, to add an event at '9/22/2006 10PM' to the time series object in the bug description:e = tsdata.event('Event1',datenum('9/22/2006 10PM'));e.Units = 'days';ts = addevent(ts,e);plot(ts);

Summary: Editor/Debugger: repeated cell evaluation of anonymous function silently fails
Description: In the Editor/Debugger, when you repeatedly evaluate a cell that includes an anonymous function declaration, the cell does not actually evaluate. The failure occurs at the line where the anonymous function is declared. Any subsequent lines in the cell are not executed, and therefore variables in the cell are not updated.
Workaround: There are two workarounds you can use:Run clear all in the Command Window before evaluating a cell, orPut the function definition in a separate cell, as in this example:%%a=12%%y=@(x)x+1%%a = y(a)

Summary: MATLAB Function Wizard provided by MATLAB Builder for Excel does not work properly
Description: The message "Error in loading DLL" appears when attempting to use the Excel add-in MATLAB Function Wizard (located in $MATLAB/toolbox/matlabxl/matlabxl, where $MATLAB is the root MATLAB directory).
Workaround: As a work-around, use the attached version of mlfunction.xla in place of the version currently on your system. Do the following: 1) First, backup your existing copy of mlfunction.xla. 2) Copy the attached file into the $MATLAB/toolbox/matlabxl/matlabxl directory and replace the existing mlfunction.xla file.

Summary: Entering a multiline string in Model Explorer's dialog view can fail
Description: Changes are not always recorded when using an embedded dialog in the Model Explorer to enter multiline text. For example, entering a multiline description for a workspace variable in the Model Explorer fails.
Workaround: Try using a standalone dialog to enter multiline text. To launch the standalone dialog from the Model Explorer, right click and select Properties from the context menu.

Summary: DEPLOYTOOL fails when using long path names
Description: DEPLOYTOOL fails to compile/package a Compiler project if any of the following paths are long (>~50 characters) and have spaces in them.Intermediate directoryOutput directoryOptions file path, if one is specifiedMATLABROOT, if specifying toolboxes in the Toolbox to search settingMCRInstaller path, if including an MCR with in the packageAny additional files added to the package
Workaround: To workaround this problem, do the following:1. Download the attached file, patch337817.zip, in your MATLABROOT directory.2. Unzip the downloaded patch archive in your MATLABROOT directory.

Summary: Generated code can fail to compile when configuration parameter IncludeERTFirstTime is set to 'off' and SuppressErrorStatus?parameter?is set to 'on'
Description: Code generated for an ERT-based Simulink model can fail to compile when both of the following conditions apply:     The IncludeERTFirstTime  configuration parameter is set to off      The Suppress error status in real-time model data structure  configuration option is selected on the Interface  pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box
Workaround: To work around this bug, set the IncludeERTFirstTime parameter to on or clear the Suppress error status in real-time model data structure option.

Summary: Incorrect code generation: C MEX S-function generated with Legacy Code Tool can produce incorrect output
Description: If both of the following conditions exist for a function specification that you specify with the Legacy Code Tool, the function can return incorrect output:  Specifies multiple outputs  The existing (legacy) C function returns one of the specified outputs with a return  statement  For example, consider a model for which y1  connects to output port 1 and y2  connects to output port 2. Suppose the function specification for the existing function is defined as follows and returns output 2.  def.OutputFcnSpec = 'double y2 = foo1(double y1[1])'; The generated code for the S-function will appear as {  /* local block i/o variables */  real_T rtb_o1;  /* Outport: '/Out1' */  Y.Out1 = rtb_o1;  /* Outport: '/Out2' incorporates:   *  S-Function (sfun_foo1): '/sfun_foo1'   */  Y.Out2 = foo1( &rtb_o1);} In this case, the output is updated with rbt_o1  before the local (stack) variable is assigned a value.

Summary: Casting single scalar values to chars can cause crash or incorrect result
Description: Using the char  or setstr  functions to convert a scalar single value can result in either a crash or an incorrect result.  For example, the following statements can result in a crash or an incorrect value for y :   x = single(58);   y = char(x);
Workaround: A simple workaround is to wrap all uses of single scalar values in the char or setstr functions in a cast to double.  For example,   x = single(58);   y = char(double(x));

Summary: Archive_CCS_Library build action results in a CCS compilation error
Description: When Archive_CCS_Library  option is selected as the build action in the Simulation Configuration Parameters->TIC6000 Code Generation pane, the generated project does not compile under CCS. A file containing chip support library calls, MW_c6xxx_csl.c, is erronously included within project files and causes the build error.
Workaround: To work around the problem, you can manually remove the MW_c6xxx_csl.c file from the generated project and recompile. This way you can generate the CCS library from your Simulink model and use it in the scope of another project.

Summary: Link for Incisive commands matlabtb, matlabcp, and matlabtbeval do not differentiate between module instances or signals with identical names
Description: Link for Incisive commands matlabtb, matlabcp, and matlabtbeval do not differentiate between module instances or signals with identical names. For example, calling matlabcp top.foo1.bar  in ncsim will replace a previous call to matlabcp top.foo2.bar  even though the associated module instances are not the same. Similarly, values read or set to different signals with the same name are read or set to only one of those signals.
Workaround: To fix the problem you need to replace the following files:MATLABROOT/toolbox/incisive/&ltarch&gt/liblfihdlc_05.50.soMATLABROOT/toolbox/incisive/&ltarch&gt/liblfihdlc_05.60.soMATLABROOT/toolbox/incisive/&ltarch&gt/liblfihdlc_05.70.sowith the corresponding attached files, where &ltarch&gt is:glnx86 for Linuxglnxa64 for Linux 64-bit sol2 for SolarisRename the files you download according to your MATLAB version and platform. The attached file names are in the form of: modelsim_MatlabVersion_platform_liblfihdlc_IncisiveVersion.so.

Summary: Real-Time Workshop build procedure overrides LIBPATH environment variable when using MSVC 8.0
Description: The build procedure for Real-Time Workshop overrides the environment setting for LIBPATH when using the Microsoft Visual C (MSVC) 8.0 compiler.  This can result in link errors due to required libraries not being found.

Summary: Atomic subsystems containing one variable-sample-time block can have poor performance or incorrect results
Description: Simulink Accelerator and Real-Time Workshop generate incorrect code for atomic subsystems in models using variable step solvers under the following circumstances: The atomic subsystem contains a nonvirtual block with variable sample time, i.e., a block whose sample time can vary during simulation. Examples include the Pulse Generator block and S-functions that specify variable sample times. The block is the only nonvirtual block in the atomic subsystem.  The update and output functions generated for such a subsystem fail to include a ssIsSampleHit  guard around the call to the variable-sample-time block. This results in the block being called more often than it should be, leading to poor performance or wrong simulation results, depending on the nature of the block.
Workaround: Add a nonvirtual block to the subsystem.

Summary: unzip and untar do not retain original timestamps of archived files
Description: The MATLAB unzip  and untar  functions do not retain the original timestamps of the archived files.  After extracting the archived files using unzip  or untar , the timestamp for the extracted files is set to the current time.

Summary: Issues with the From Multimedia File block and MPEG-2 video codecs
Description: The From Multimedia File block has issues with the following MPEG-2 video codecs:Cyberlink video Decoder (clvsd.ax) Intervideo Decoder (ivivid.ax)
Workaround: You must use a different MPEG-2 video codec, such as the MainConcept codec mcdsmpeg.ax, to play MPEG-2 multimedia files.

Summary: Simulink Accelerator builds error out if current directory name contains space
Description: Simulink Accelerator builds error out if the current directory (pwd ) is under the MATLAB installation root (matlabroot ) and the directory name contains a space.
Workaround: To work around this bug, start Simulink Accelerator builds from a directory without a space in its name.

Summary: MCC -v reports version 4.4, while VER reports compiler version 4.5
Description: The commands mcc -v -[options] [inputfile]  or mcc -?  report incorrect version number 4.4.The correct version should be 4.5.
Workaround: There is currently no workaround for this problem.

Summary: Polygons constructed by mapshow or geoshow are incompatible with zdatam
Description: When zdatam  is applied to adjust the elevation of a polygon constructed with either mapshow  or geoshow , the edge lines of the polygon are left behind at the original elevation. This problem is only noticeable in a 3-D view.

Summary: The 3/4 FanSpeed property value for the QImaging adaptor has been misspelled.
Description: The 3/4 value for the FanSpeed for the QImaging adaptor is misspelled as 'ThreeQuater' instead of 'ThreeQuarter'.

Summary: rethrow function displays the stack from where the error was caught, not from where it was originally thrown.
Description: The rethrow  function shows the stack frames from where an error was caught, not from where the original error is thrown.? For example, when you call the following function ??? function foo??? level2;??? function level2??? try?????? level3;??? catch?????? rethrow(lasterror);??? end??? function level3??? level4;??? function level4??? level5;??? function level5??? error('id:id', 'message'); MATLAB displays this message: ??? >> foo??? ??? Error using ==> foo>level5 at 18??? message??? Error in ==> foo at 2??? level2; In this example, rethrow omits frame numbers 2, 3, and 4 from the error.? In general, rethrow  omits frames 2 to N, where N is defined by placement of the?rethrow  function on the call stack.
Workaround: A?new error-handling feature called?MException is available with release R2007b. The rethrow method of an MException preserves the original stack of the error.

Summary: Models containing Stateflow charts or Embedded MATLAB Function blocks do not work with MSVC 8.0 for C++ code generation
Description: For C++ code generation, you cannot simulate a model that contains a Stateflow chart or Embedded MATLAB Function block if you choose Microsoft Visual Studio 8.0 as your MEX compiler. You will receive the following error:---- Error using ==> stateflow\private\slsf Error using ==> stateflow\private\construct_error The current Real-Time Workshop target is configured to generate C++, but the C-only compiler, LCC, is the default compiler.?? To specify a C++ compiler, enter 'mex -setup' at the command prompt.?? To generate C code, open the Configuration Parameters dialog and set the target language to C. ----
Workaround: Use Microsoft Visual Studio 7.1 as your MEX compiler in R2006b and earlier.

Summary: Link for ModelSim commands matlabtb, matlabcp, and matlabtbeval do not differentiate between components or signals with identical names
Description: Link for ModelSim commands matlabtb, matlabcp, and matlabtbeval do not differentiate between components or signals with identical names.For example, calling matlabcp /top/foo1/bar  in ModelSim will replace a previous call to matlabcp /top/foo2/bar  even though the associated components are different component instances.This limitation also applies to different signals with the same name. Values read or set to these same-named signals are read or set to only one of those signals.
Workaround: Replace MATLABROOT/toolbox/modelsim/&ltarch&gt/matlablink.&ltext&gt with one of the attached files (where &ltarch&gt is one of win32, glnx86, glnxa64, or sol2, and &ltext&gt is dll or so).The file to download and rename depends on your MATLAB version and platform.The attached file names are in the form of modelsim_MatlabVersion_platform_matlablink.extwhere- MatlabVersion is either R2006a or R2006b- platform is:  win32 for Windows  glnx86 for Linux  glnxa64 for Linux 64-bit  sol2 for Solaris- and ext is "dll" for Windows and "so" for the remaining platforms.The file you download should be renamed to matlablink.so or matlablink.dll, depending on the platform.

Summary: TCPIP, Serial, and GPIB can use 100% CPU with rapid fopen(), fwrite(), and fclose() calls
Description: Applications that use fopen,fwrite, and fclose in quick succession with TCPIP, UDP, Serial, or GPIB device objects can trigger 100% CPU usage in MATLAB.  The following MATLAB-code shows a typical case, executing a rapid call sequence. s=serial('COM1'); % For example.fopen(s);fwrite(s,'a');    % Writing shorter data appears to trigger this more often.fclose(s);                  % note 100% CPU usage in some cases
Workaround: Use pause to give MATLAB sufficient time to ensure that the device object is in a proper state before closing. s=serial('COM1');fopen(s);fwrite(s,'a');pause(0.1);      % pause ensures device object is in proper statefclose(s);If the CPU usage is already at 100%, use an additional call to fopen(), and optionally fclose(), to return the CPU usage to normal.

Summary: The 2.5 MHz Readout speed for the QImaging adaptor has been misspelled.
Description: The 2.5 MHz value for the Readout speed for the QImaging adaptor is misspelled as '2M5' instead of '2.5M'.

Summary: Trimming a polygon to a quadrangle can error when vertices exactly fall on a bounding line
Description: When maptrimp  or geoshow  trims vertices of a polygon to a cylindrical or conic map projection, an error can result for certain cases involving vertices that lie exactly on a bounding line. (For geoshow , this can happen either when displaying a polygon geostruct or when specifying a DisplayType  of 'polygon' .)
Workaround: To work around this issue, replace the trimPolygonToVerticalLine.m function file in your current installation with the newer version in the attachment, as follows:Exit MATLAB.Make a backup copy of the following file. matlabroot\toolbox\map\mapproj\private\trimPolygonToVerticalLine.mmatlabroot represents the name of your MATLAB installation directory, as returned by the matlabroot function.For example, rename the existing file, adding "old" to the file extension, trimPolygonToVerticalLine.m.OLD.Copy the version of trimPolygonToVerticalLine.m from the attachment into the directory specified in the previous step.Restart MATLAB.Run the rehash toolbox command at the MATLAB prompt.Also see bug report  359399 for information about a related polygon trimming issue.

Summary: Using the right click context menu to restore a library link? inside a subsystem causes a duplicate block to be inserted
Description: Clicking on empty space in a subsystem to restore a library link creates a second copy of the block in the model.
Workaround: Right click on the block itself to restore the library link.

Summary: Print dialog window for DEPLOYPRINT not modal for MATLAB Compiler 4.4
Description: On Windows platforms, the DEPLOYPRINT command prints asynchronously, meaning that the command returns before printing is complete. This may produce unexpected application behavior, as MATLAB's PRINT command is synchronous.
Workaround: In release R2006a:Remove the "&" on line 167 of toolbox/compiler/depoly/deployprint.m.In release R2006b:Remove the "&" on line 180 of toolbox/compiler/deploy/deployprint.m.

Summary: Unable to capture error information when using engOutputBuffer
Description: MATLAB does not put error output to engOutputBuffer  when engEvalString  is called.
Workaround: There is a bug in MATLAB Engine in MATLAB 7.3 (R2006b) where error information is not captured in the engine output buffer. As a workaround, use the lasterror function in an engEvalString command after calling your MATLAB code.For example, consider the following C-code that calls MATLAB code (mybuggymfile.m) containing errors:Engine *ep;char outputBuffer[BUFFER_SIZE];...engOutputBuffer(ep, outputBuffer,BUFFER_SIZE);engEvalString(ep, "lasterror('reset')");engEvalString(ep, "mybuggymfile");engEvalString(ep, "lasterror");The call to engEvalString(ep, "lasterror");captures error information (including line numbers) generated when running mybuggymfile. For information about the lasterror function, at the MATLAB command line typedoc lasterrorNote the following line in the code snippet: engEvalString(ep, "lasterror('reset')");This command resets the lasterror state so that the subsequent call to lasterror does not contain errors which occurred prior to calling mybuggymfile.

Summary: function setm errors when called on an hggroup returned by geoshow
Description: Since R2006b (Mapping Toolbox Versions 2.4 and 2.5), the geoshow  function returns a handle to an hggroup  when displaying sets of vector features, but the setm  function errors when given an hggroup  handle as input.
Workaround: To work around this issue, replace the setm.m function file in your installation with the version in the attachment, as follows:    Exit MATLAB.    Make a backup copy of the following file.    MATLABROOT\matlab\toolbox\map\mapdisp\setm.m    MATLABROOT represents the name of your MATLAB installation directory, as returned by the matlabroot function.    For example, rename the existing file, adding "old" to the file extension: setm.m.old        Copy the version of setm.m from the attachment into the directory specified in the previous step.    Restart MATLAB.    Run the rehash toolbox command at the MATLAB prompt.

Summary: Setting a parameter on a block in a deleted subsystem may cause MATLAB to crash
Description: Using the set_param command  to set a parameter on a block or other object in a subsystem that has been deleted may cause Matlab to crash.

Summary: S-Function Builder block dialog box can fail to display discrete sample times correctly
Description: The S-Function Builder block dialog box fails to display the correct values for its Sample mode  and Sample time value  parameters under the following conditions: You specify the S-Function Builder block's Sample mode  as Discrete . You specify the S-Function Builder block's Sample time value  as an S-function parameter. If you close and then reopen the S-Function Builder block dialog box, its Sample mode  parameter specifies Inherited  and its Sample time value  parameter is disabled.
Workaround: After you specify a discrete sample time using an S-function parameter in the S-Function Builder block dialog box, build the S-function before closing the dialog box. Note that this issue does not affect the S-function code once you build it.

Summary: Problems printing in compiled applications on Windows.
Description: MATLAB figure windows that set their Units property to Centimeters or Points cannot be printed when deployed in a compiled application on any version of Microsoft Windows. The application will display the following error text:??? Undefined function or variable "oldPaperUnits".
Workaround: Modify /toolbox/compiler/deploy/deployprint.m by inserting:    oldPaperUnits = '';immediately after the line:   paperUnitsPattern = '(centimeters)|(points)';   This insures the variable oldPaperUnits always has a value.

Summary: Using sl_customization can cause MATLAB to hang under certain conditions
Description: Using the Simulink Customization feature on Microsoft Windows to disable or hide standard Simulink menu items that have corresponding toolbar buttons can cause MATLAB to become unresponsive. Menu items affected by this problem include File->New->Model , File->Save , and Simulation->Start .
Workaround: Either avoid disabling or hiding menu items that correspond to toolbar buttons in the Microsoft Windows version of Simulink or reenable this functionality as follows:Download the attached mex files.Save them in $MATLAB\toolbox\local.Open simulinkrc.m in the MATLAB editor.Replace % Customization managerslcustomizeon line 34 with: % Customization managerif ispc() && isequal(version('-release'),'2006b') && exist('idlefix','file') == 3    % Make sure sl customization is enabled for default menu items.    % This must be run before the first model is opened.    idlefix;endslcustomizeRestart MATLAB.

Summary: Embedded MATLAB function block overflows the stack
Description: The Embedded MATLAB function block uses the stack as much as possible, with a limit of 200,000 bytes. The 200,000 byte limit is too high for TI C2000 applications.The consequences of the stack overflowing are unpredictable when the generated code is running on the target, but in many cases a stack overflow leads the program counter to an illegal ISR trap.
Workaround: Download the attached ZIP archive patch_335822.zip and the patch installer patch_installer.m to your MATLAB root directory. Use matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt to determine your MATLAB root directory.After you download the patch and installer files, start MATLAB and enter the following command at the MATLAB command prompt to install the patch.  patch_installer('patch_335822.zip', 'install') Applying patch patch_335822.zip  changes the product to limit the amount of stack the Embedded MATLAB function block can use which avoids the stack overflow.

Summary: Why does the Data Acquisition Toolbox not output all samples from the analog output object created on my NI PCI-6110 board at low sample rates?
Description: I have noticed that an analog output object on a National Instruments PCI-6110 board does not output the queued number of samples at low sample rates.In particular, the final samples can be missed, though the AO object reports outputting all queued samples via "SamplesOutput." I am using sample rates below 1000 Hz and observe this behavior.
Workaround: This is not a bug with the Data Acquisition Toolbox, but rather a hardware limitation of the PCI-6110 board.In our experience, the minimum effective sampling rate for the board is 1000 Hz. Consequently, setting the sampling rate to low values may cause some samples not to be output.As a work-around, set the sample rate to higher values so that all the samples are output.

Summary: FI inputs to Embedded MATLAB functions may display incorrectly in the debugger
Description: If an input to an Embedded MATLAB Function block is of fixed-point type, the value of the input variable may display incorrectly when execution stops in the debugger. This error may occur when you display the value of the fixed-point input variable after halting program execution in a subfunction of the Embedded MATLAB function. In rare cases, MATLAB crashes in this situation. The values of fixed-point input variables do display correctly when you halt program execution in the top-level Embedded MATLAB function.  You can display a variable in the debugger, either by typing its name on the command line or by hovering over the variable with the mouse cursor.
Workaround: Do not display the value of a FI input to an Embedded MATLAB Function block when you stop program execution in a subfunction.

Summary: input command can cause MATLAB to use 100% of CPU time
Description: Use of the input  command sometimes causes MATLAB to consume 100% of CPU time.  The reported cases have involved a script or function which does some drawing followed immediately by the input command. For example,plot(peaks);input('Enter text '); Occasionally a sequence like this is sufficient to cause the problem by itself, although usually some further interaction with the figure is necessary.To diagnose the problem, look at the Task Manager on Windows or use a command such as top  on UNIX to see how the CPU time is being spent.  (Note: if you have more than one CPU, the bug manifests itself as the use of 100% of the time of one of the CPUs, not all of them).
Workaround: In most cases, the problem resolves itself when input is entered in response to the input command.Sometimes MATLAB remains unresponsive even after input is entered. This often happens when other GUI actions are taken in between the appearance of the input prompt and responding to the input prompt.  Users should avoid using other parts of the GUI while an input prompt is waiting for input.Closing or hiding the workspace browser reduces (but does not eliminate) the incidence of this bug.Running MATLAB with the -nodesktop or -nojvm switches reduces (but does not eliminate) the incidence of this bug.

Summary: Embedded MATLAB fixed-point with debugging off gives wrong answers
Description: Embedded MATLAB code using fixed-point arithmetic may give incorrect answers when simulated with debugging turned off or when compiled using Real-Time Workshop.   This bug occurs when Embedded MATLAB code uses non-constant fixed-point expressions having non-native sizes ˇŞ for example, if your target supports 8, 16, or 32-bit char , short , and int  data, but your Embedded MATLAB code uses fixed-point quantities with odd sizes like 12 or 24 bits.  In this case, the optimization fails to clear top bits between the odd size and the closest native size container. This bug affects Embedded MATLAB Function blocks in Simulink, Embedded MATLAB functions in Stateflow, Truth Table blocks, and the code generated via Real-Time Workshop from any of these sources.
Workaround: This bug occurs in a specific optimization. You can turn off this optimization by issuing the following command:sf('Feature','Peephole','off')You must issue this command each time you start MATLAB. We do not recommend turning off this optimization unless you think you are affected by this bug.

Summary: Compilation error when file or directory name begins with "u"
Description: When using MATLAB Builder for Java, the compilation step will fail if any directory name or file name that begins with 'u' appears in a path passed as part of a command line argument to MCC. The Java compiler interprets the string "\u" as an escape sequence that receives special processing when present inside a Javadoc comment. The error given by the compiler is: "Unicode escape is invalid".  This is only a problem on the Windows platform (since it uses the backslash as its path separator character).
Workaround: Edit the generated .java files to remove the sequence '\u' from all comments, or rename the offending directories/files to not begin with 'u'.

Summary: Code not generated for state reset for action subsystem
Description: If an action subsystem's Action port block specifies Reset  as the value of its States when execution is resumed  parameter, Real-Time Workshop may fail to generate code to perform the state reset. This bug does not affect normal mode simulation, it occurs only in generated code.  Assuming adequate simulation coverage, you can detect the bug by comparing the results of normal mode simulation with the results of executing the generated code.
Workaround: There is no workaround.

Summary: Memory leak in geotiffinfo
Description: Function geotiffinfo  generates a small memory leak of about 78 KB per call when using the GeoTIFF library. The leak occurs only when reading CSV-formatted files from the directory toolbox/mapproj/projdata/epsg_csv . The library functions allocate memory for tables to temporarily store CSV data, but after traversing the tables fail to free memory holding the data.
Workaround: If you are using Version 2.4 of the Mapping Toolbox (R2006b), you can apply the following workaround, in which you replace the gtifinfo.mex* and gtifinfo.mex*.csf files in your current installation with the updated versions in the attachment, as follows:Exit MATLAB.Rename each of the following two files as a precaution:Rename matlabroot/toolbox/map/mapformats/private/gtifinfo.mex* (where * varies according to your machine's architecture) totoolbox/map/mapformats/private/gtifinfo.mex???*.orig.Rename matlabroot/toolbox/map/mapformats/private/gtifinfo.mex*.csf (where * varies according to your machine's architecture) totoolbox/map/mapformats/private/gtifinfo.mex*.csf.orig.matlabroot represents the name of your MATLAB installation directory, as returned by the matlabroot function.Copy the version of gtifinfo.mex* that is appropriate for your installation into the directory specified in the previous step.Copy the version of gtifinfo.mex*.csf that is appropriate for your installation into the directory specified in the previous step.Restart MATLAB.Run the rehash toolbox command at the MATLAB prompt.

Summary: bitand, bitor, and bitxor can cause wrong answers and segmentation violations
Description: The bitand , bitor , and bitxor  functions can produce a wrong answer, and in some cases a segmentation violation, when one of the operands is the result of a cast from a high-precision fi  object (over 48 bits) to a lower-precision fi  object. For example:   A = fi(0.1, 1, 64, 63);     % High-precision fi object  B = fi(A, 1, 16, 15);       % A is cast to lower-precision fi object B  C = fi(-0.1, 1, 16, 15);    % fi object with same precision as B  D = bitor(B,C) produces the wrong answer  bin(D) = 1111001100110011 The correct answer for the bitwise OR of  bin(B) = 0000110011001101  bin(C) = 1111001100110011 is  bin(D) = 1111111111111111
Workaround: To work around this problem, cast the high-precision fi object into a double before the cast. For example:  A = fi(0.1,1,64,63);           % High-precision fi object  B = fi(double(A),1,16,15);     % A is cast to a double before casting to fi  C = fi(-0.1,1,16,15);          % fi object with same precision as B  D = bitor(B,C)produces the correct answer   bin(D) = 1111111111111111

Summary: Model containing Embedded MATLAB function crashes while executing on target
Description: Using Embedded MATLAB Function blocks in a Simulink model can cause the generated code to fail during execution on your target. State Flow Coder generates the code for Embedded MATLAB Function blocks. In the generated code, State Flow Coder specifies an internal stack size limit that is too generous for embedded targets.The code generated from the Embedded MATLAB Function block in your model application may create large local arrays which crash the application when it runs on your target.
Workaround: If you are using an Embedded Matlab Function in your model, add the following Matlab function to the InitFcn callback of your model:sfc('coder_options', 'maxStackUsage', 512);This function sets the internal stack size limit for the State Flow Coder to 512 bytes thereby avoiding the creation of large arrays on the stack.

Summary: imwrite crashes or produces incorrect data when writing sparse data
Description: The imwrite  function produces inconsistent results when asked to write sparse data.  Either the data written to the file will be corrupt, or you may see a segmentation violation.  Here is a typical crash report, but the actual functions in the stack may be different: Stack Trace:  [0] png.mexw32:_FillRow8(0x0fd92220, 0, 1, 0x106c31a0) + 138 bytes  [1] png.mexw32:_WritePNG(0, 0x00d0d8d0, 0, 1) + 1473 bytes  [2] png.mexw32:_mexFunction(0, 0x00d0d8d0, 17, 0x00d0d930) + 113 bytes
Workaround: Use the full command to convert sparse data before calling imwrite.

Summary: Error when generating code for a Stateflow chart that calls Function-Call Subsystem multiple times
Description: The Real-Time Workshop build process might fail to generate code for a model with Stateflow charts and Function-Call Subsystems under all of the following conditions: The model contains a Stateflow chart with function-call output events. One of the function call output events is invoked multiple times in the Stateflow chart. The Function-Call Subsystem blocks only contain a Constant block connected to an Outport block.  The build process reports the failure message in the MATLAB Command Window similar to the following: Unable to find $FIELD_NAME within the CallSiteInfo scopeError: File: $MATLAB_ROOT\rtw\c\tlc\lib\syslib.tlc  ?
Workaround: In the Function-Call Subsystem blocks that only contain a Constant and Outport block, set the Real-Time Workshop system code parameter to Inlined.

Summary: Integrate and Dump block applies offset in each integration period in accelerated mode
Description: The Integrate and Dump block gives different integration results in normal mode and accelerated mode when a nonzero offset is specified and the Output intermediate values  checkbox is selected. The value specified in Offset  should be the number of samples that will be ignored by the block during the first integration period only. This result is correctly seen in the normal mode of simulation. However, in accelerated mode, this offset is ignored in each integration period, leading to an incorrect result.
Workaround: The following workaround applies to versions 3.2, 3.3, and 3.4 of Communications Blockset, corresponding to releases R14sp3, R2006a, and R2006b of MATLAB.To work around this issue, download the attached version of the TLC-file, matlab/toolbox/commblks/commmex/tlc_c/scomintegdump.tlc, and follow these steps:1. Quit MATLAB2. Rename the following TLC-file: matlabroot\toolbox\commblks\commmex\tlc_c\scomintegdump.tlc(where matlabrootis the MATLAB root directory on your machine, as returned by typing matlabrootat the MATLAB command prompt.) Rename the file to scomintegdump.tlc.old 3. Download the attached file, scomintegdump.tlc. Place the new file in the same directory mentioned in step 2. 4. Restart MATLAB 5. After restarting MATLAB, issue the following command at the MATLAB prompt: rehash toolboxcache Note: You must delete the previously built DLLs, and run the simulation again, which should cause a rebuild to occur. It is important to ensure that the model rebuilds with the updated TLC file for the changes to take effect.

Summary: Stateflow simulation fails when MEX compiler is Open Watcom 1.3
Description: When trying to simulate Stateflow models using the Open Watcom 1.3 compiler, a "MAKE Error" occurs and the model fails to simulate.
Workaround: There are two workarounds:Use mex -setup to choose a different MEX compiler (such as lcc or MSVC 7.1).Apply a patch as described below.Installation instructions  Log in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.  Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps.  Exit MATLAB.  Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder.  Make a backup copy of each of the files listed in the .zip file. To see the contents of the .zip file:       Windows Systems*: Open the .zip file and look at the contents.    Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh):unzip -l <MATLAB>/<.zip file>      Extract the files from the .zip file.       Windows Systems*:          Open the .zip file.      Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.            Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh):         cd <MATLAB>      unzip <.zip file>          Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.*Notes for Windows Systems:  If you see 'Error: Access is denied' when attempting to extract the .zip file, you  may need to start WinZip in administrator mode. Do this by navigating to  WinZip.exe or the WinZip Start menu entry, right-clicking on it, and selecting  Run as administrator.  From  within WinZip, find and open the downloaded .zip file.  Extract  the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.

Summary: Update diagram performance degradation for model containing Model blocks
Description: If a model contains a significant number of Model blocks, the time required to perform an update diagram might increase in recent releases.

Summary: File->Save As command saves Concatenate blocks incorrectly in some cases
Description: Saving an 2006b model as an 2006a model incorrectly saves Concatenate blocks in Horizontal matrix concatenation  and Vertical matrix concatenation  modes as obsolete Matrix Concatenation blocks. This is not correct because there is no change to Concatenate block from R2006a to R2006b. Consequently, Concatenate blocks should be saved as-is when saving an R2006b model as an R2006a model.
Workaround: This bug has no impact on the functionality of the saved model because Concatenate blocks in matrix modes behave the same as Matrix Concatenation blocks with equivalent settings. However, you should still correct the problem as follows because Concatenate blocks can be faster in some cases.If the R2006b model does not contain Concatenate blocks in Vector concatenation mode, consider saving the R2006b model as an R14SP3 model. This still saves the Concatenate blocks in matrix modes as Matrix Concatenation blocks in the R14SP3 model, but loading the R14Sp3 model into R2006a correctly forwards the Matrix Concatenation blocks to Concatenate blocks.Otherwise, run the attached script on the saved R2006a model to replace blocks with links to simulink_obsolete/Matrix Concatenation with equivalent Concatenate blocks.

Summary: Incorrect reporting of error: "cannot determine sample time"
Description: When a virtual block, e.g. a subsystem Inport block, changes the sample time of a signal, an error such as the one below can be reported even though it should not be. Cannot determine a sample time for block 'sample_time_problem/tlss/task_block/x1'. Either specify the sample time of this block (a suggested rate is [0.0001, 0], the greatest common divisor of the input rates) or specify sample times for the blocks that drive this block's inputs.
Workaround: Specify the sample time on the block that is erroring out or on some nonvirtual block, such as a Rate Transition block, that is inserted on the signal driving the block.

Summary: Computing the state-space modal canonical form using CANON is not returning the correct structure for the A matrix.
Description: When using CANON to compute the modal canoncial form, the 2x2 blocks on the diagonal of the A matrix may not have the correct structure of [a, b; -b, a] where the complex eigenvalues are a+ib and a-ib. For example:sys = tf(1,[1 4 9 14 8]); csys = canon(sys,'modal'); csys.a ans =   -0.5000   -1.8190         0         0    2.0616   -0.5000         0         0         0         0   -2.0000         0         0         0         0   -1.0000 The expected answer is csys.a ans =   -0.5000    1.9365         0         0   -1.9365   -0.5000         0         0         0         0   -2.0000         0         0         0         0   -1.0000
Workaround: To correct this issue, copy the attached file into the directory listed below and overwrite the original file. (matlabroot)/toolbox/control/control/@ss

Summary: Empty axes results from contouring a uniform Z matrix with contour or contourf
Description: When making a contour plot of uniform ZData using the following code,X=1:20;Y=1:20;Z=zeros(20);contourf(X,Y,Z) I receive an empty axes.

Summary: The brighten function fails if 'IntegerHandle' or 'HandleVisibility' is set to 'off'
Description: If a figure is created with either the 'IntegerHandle'  or 'HandleVisibility'  property is set to 'off' , as in the following codefig = figure('IntegerHandle','off','HandleVisibility','off');ax = axes('Parent',fig);surf(ax,peaks); calling the brighten  function to change the luminance of that figure, such asbrighten(fig,0.75) will have no visible effect on the figure.
Workaround: To work around this issue, call the brighten function on the colormap rather than the figure:map = get(fig,'Colormap');map = brighten(map,0.75);set(fig,'Colormap',map);

Summary: Second definition of same anonymous function silently fails
Description: When I create two anonymous functions with identical definitions, as in the following example,   a = @(x)sin(x);   b = @(x)sin(x); the second anonymous function b  does not get assigned.  The whos  command shows that b  has silently failed to be defined:    b(1)   ??? Undefined function or method 'b' for input arguments of type 'double'. There is also a subtle variant of this bug that has but one explicit definition. If a script calls a MEX function with the handle of an anonymous function as the argument prhs[0] , and the MEX function calls mexEvalString  to assign that function handle (in the form of a string) to a new variable, that assignment silently fails.
Workaround: One way to work around the first issue is to insert a space character in the definition of the second function:   f = @(x) x(1);   g = @(x) x( 1); A workaround to the second issue is to call mexCallMATLAB with the function name specified as 'feval' and pass it two inputs - the function handle and the function handle's input.

Summary: mode applied to an empty input returns empty instead of NaN
Description: Most descriptive statistics functions, such as mean  and median , return NaN  when applied to an empty matrix.  The mode  function returns an empty result, [] , instead of NaN .

Summary: Show when hovering option fails in multitasking mode
Description: Hovering the cursor over a block can cause Simulink to display a tooltip listing the block's port values incorrectly as xx.xx  instead of the actual values. This problem occurs under the following conditions: The Model Editor's Port Values->Show when hovering  menu item is selected. The model specifies a fixed-step solver. The model operates in multitasking mode.  Note  A model operates in multitasking modeif its Tasking mode for periodic sample times  option is set to MultiTasking  or is set to Auto  and the model contains blocks operating at different rates.

Summary: Memory might be insufficient for large arrays after Help or Web browser has been open
Description: MATLAB might produce an Out of memory  error when attempting to create a very large array. While the array size that produces this condition can vary, a rough guideline is an array of doubles on the order of 10,000,000 elements. This condition occurs when the MATLAB Help browser or Web browser has been opened in the session. Other MathWorks tools use the Web browser and therefore can also cause the problem, such as the Profiler and cell publishing to HTML.
Workaround: If you need to work with very large arrays and encounter this error, close the Help browser, Web browser, and any tool that uses the Web browser. Then restart MATLAB and do not use the Help browser or Web browser in that session until you have completed your work with the large arrays.If you want to use the Help browser, Web browser or other tool that uses the Web browser, and you need to work with large arrays, you can use the R2006a (MATLAB 7.2) version of the browser. To do so, run the following statement in MATLABcom.mathworks.mlwidgets.html.HTMLRenderer.setUseWebRenderer(false);and then restart MATLAB.

Summary: Simulation input of Hardware Interrupt block doesn't work
Description: Function Call subsystems driven by the C281x or C280x Hardware Interrupt blocks can not be called in simulation using the simulation input of the block. The Hardware Interrupt S-function is not enabled for simulation input capabilities.
Workaround: Download the attached patch installer patch_installer.m and the ZIP archive patch_334044_R2006x.zip corresponding to the MATLAB version you want to patch.  This patch is available for MATLAB releases R2006a and R2006b.    Start MATLAB and type ver at the prompt to verify which version of MATLAB you are using.    Enter the following command at the MATLAB prompt to install the patch:        For R2006a:      patch_installer('patch_334044_R2006a.zip', 'install')         For R2006b:      patch_installer('patch_334044_R2006b.zip', 'install')

Summary: mlint: hyperlinks to line numbers sometimes missing in output
Description: When you run the mlint  function, each output statement includes a line number, which is a hyperlink. Sometimes the hyperlinks are missing so that you cannot click the line number to go to that line in the Editor/Debugger.
Workaround: Open the file in the Editor/Debugger, select Go  >  Go To , and enter the line number shown in the mlint output to which you want to navigate.

Summary: 6DoF ECEF blocks return incorrect results
Description: Using the 6DoF ECEF blocks in a model returns incorrect results.
Workaround: Download one of the attached files to resolve the problem. The visible changes include:Accounting for the rotation of the North-East-Down (NED) frame in angular rates (omega).Accounting for the rotation of the Earth-Centered Earth-Fixed (ECEF) frame in angular rates (omega).Updating the postion in the ECEF Frame (Xecef) calculation to remove some numerical instability.Updating the quaternion to define the rotation from ECEF frame to body frame.With the update, the 6DoF ECEF blocks now use the references: "Stevens, B. L., and F. L. Lewis, Aircraft Control and Simulation, Second Edition, John Wiley & Sons, New York, 2003" and "McFarland, Richard E., A Standard Kinematic Model for Flight simulation at NASA-Ames, NASA CR-2497". The following references for the 6DoF ECEF blocks are no longer valid: "Stevens, B. L., and F. L. Lewis, Aircraft Control and Simulation, John Wiley & Sons, New York, 1992" and "Zipfel, P. H., Modeling and Simulation of Aerospace Vehicle Dynamics, AIAA Education Series, Reston, Virginia, 2000".Installation instructionsLog in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps.Exit MATLAB.Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder.Make a backup copy of each of the files listed in the .zip file: /toolbox/aeroblks/aeroblks/aerolib6dof2.mdlTo see the contents of the .zip file: Windows Systems*: Open the .zip file and look at the contents.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh):unzip -l <MATLAB>/<.zip file>Extract the files from the .zip file. Windows Systems*: Open the .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh): cd <MATLAB>unzip <.zip file>Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.*Notes for Windows Systems:If you see ˇ°Error: Access is deniedˇ± when attempting to extract the .zip file, you may need to start ˇ°WinZipˇ± in administrator mode. Do this by navigating to WinZip.exe or the WinZip Start menu entry, right-clicking on it, and selecting ˇ°Run as administratorˇ±.From within WinZip, find and open the downloaded .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.

Summary: Error when starting compiled GUI with ActiveX control
Description: When starting the compiled application, the user may see a dialog box with the message:Assertion failed!Program: <application name>File: win32/PostThread.cppLine: 193Expression: 0 < fActivationCount This only affects applications with ActiveX components compiled for the Windows platform.
Workaround: To workaround this problem, download the attached class files (ActiveXCanvas.class, ActiveXCanvas$1.class, and ActiveXCanvas$2.class) and place them in [MATLABROOT]\java\patch\com\mathworks\hg\peer\All MATLAB and MCR installations must have the patch applied to correct this error.

Summary: dicominfo errors unexpectedly with certain retired attributes
Description: Before R2006b, using dicominfo  to read a DICOM file that contained a retired attribute not listed in our data dictionary produced this message: ??? Error using ==> medformats\private\dicom_read_attr>read_eltError using ==> medformats\private\dicom_read_attr>find_attr_detailsAttribute (0018,9445) was not found in the data dictionary. Starting in R2006b, running dicominfo  on the same file yields this error: ??? Error using ==> structField name must be a string vector.
Workaround: These messages occur because dicominfo expects public attributes ˇŞ that is, attributes with even group numbers ˇŞ to appear in the DICOM data dictionary.Users of R2006b should download and extract the R2007a version of dicominfo.m, make a backup copy of the R2006b version installed in $MATLAB/toolbox/images/medformats (where $MATLAB represents your MATLAB installation directory), copy the R2007a version there, and restart MATLAB.This updated version of dicominfo.m is not compatible with R2006a or earlier versions of the Image Processing Toolbox. Users with older versions can modify the DICOM data dictionary used by dicominfo to include the unknown attribute. For information on updating the data dictionary, see the dicom-dict.txt file in the $MATLAB/toolbox/images/medformats directory.

Summary: Opening a model with the Model Browser enabled fails to reopen subsystems
Description: When opening a model with the Model Browser enabled, Simulink opens the toplevel system only. The correct behavior is to reopen also the subsystems that were open the last time the model was saved.

Summary: The Square Root IQN block from the C28x IQMath Library gives wrong simulation results for Qm.24 values
Description: The Square Root IQN block returns incorrect simulation results for Qm.24 input data, where m is the data word length. Qm.24 format uses 24 bits to represent the fractional part of the input data (24 bits to the right of the binary point).In Simulink, Qm.24 corresponds to any Simulink signed fixed-point format that uses 24 bits to represent the fraction length of the data.
Workaround: To fix this problem, perform the following steps.Download the attached patch installer patch_installer.m and the ZIP archive that corresponds to the MATLAB version to patch. The attached files include ZIP archives for patching MATLAB releases R2006a and R2006b. Start MATLAB and enter ver at the MATLAB prompt to verify the version of MATLAB you are using. In MATLAB, navigate to the directory that contains the patch installer and ZIP file that you downloaded in step 1.Enter one of the following commands at the MATLAB prompt to install the patch: To patch R2006a:  patch_installer('patch_333732_R2006a.zip', 'install') To patch R2006b:  patch_installer('patch_333732_R2006b.zip', 'install') Close and restart MATLAB.

Summary: Integer Delay and Tapped Delay blocks: compile warning or link error can result from missing include statement
Description: When you use the Integer Delay block or the Tapped Delay block in a model, the code that Real-Time Workshop generates for the model can call the utility function memcpy() , but fail to include the header file string.h , which declares the function. This can occur when the following conditions exist: The Remove root level I/O zero initialization  and Remove internal state zero initialization  options on the Optimization  pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box are set. No other block that calls memcpy()  exists in the model. The input to the Integer Delay block or the Tapped Delay block is?non-scalar and contiguous; or the input is configured as a frame-based signal.  When these conditions exist, a compile warning or link error, such as the following, can occur during the build process: model.c(78) : warning C4013: 'memcpy' undefined; assuming extern returning int
Workaround: Determine your MATLAB root directory by entering the matlabroot command at the MATLAB command prompt.Quit MATLAB.Rename matlabroot/toolbox/simulink/fixedandfloat/tlc_c/sfix_udelay.tlc to matlabroot/toolbox/simulink/fixedandfloat/tlc_c/sfix_udelay.tlc.old.Download the attached file sfix_udelay.tlc and place it in the directory matlabroot/toolbox/simulink/fixedandfloat/tlc_c.Restart MATLAB.

Summary: Subsystems: error can occur if mutiple subsystem builds are done from the same model
Description: Building more than two different subsystems in a model might cause the following error:??? Error using ==> rtw\private\ssgencode>LocalSetupCodeNodeError using ==> horzcatCAT arguments dimensions are not consistent.Error in ==> matlabroot \toolbox\rtw\rtw\rtwprivate.p>rtwprivate at 12   [varargout{1:nargout}] = feval(function_name, varargin{1:end});??? Error using ==> rtwprivate ssgencode BuildError using ==> rtw\private\ssgencode>LocalSetupCodeNodeError using ==> horzcatCAT arguments dimensions are not consistent.??? Error while evaluating com.mathworks.mwt.MWButton ActionPerformedCallback

Summary: multibandread returns incorrectly oriented data when BIP is specified with a nonstandard MATLAB data type
Description: The multibandread  function will return incorrectly oriented data when you specify the interleave format argument as 'bip'  (Band-Interleaved-by-Pixel) and set the precision argument to a nonstandard MATLAB type, such as "float" or "short."  For example, instead of returning the data with dimensions 10-by-20-by-3, multibandread  will return the data as 20-by-10-by-3.
Workaround: Specify a standard MATLAB datatype in the precision argument.  For example, instead of specifying "float" use "single," instead of "short" specify "uint16", and so on.

Summary: Model Discretizer: using File > Load model opens the model but throws an exception in the MATLAB Command Window
Description: In the Model Discretizer GUI, when you select File > Load model  and select any model in the current directory, the model opens. However, the MATLAB Command Window shows the following exception:  Exception in thread "AWT-EventQueue-0" java.lang.NullPointerException at com.mathworks.toolbox.mdldisc.DiscTreeListener.hiliteNode(DiscTree.java:331) at com.mathworks.toolbox.mdldisc.LoadModelAction.actionPerformed(DiscAction.java:485) at javax.swing.AbstractButton.fireActionPerformed(Unknown Source) at javax.swing.AbstractButton$Handler.actionPerformed(Unknown Source) at javax.swing.DefaultButtonModel.fireActionPerformed(Unknown Source) at javax.swing.DefaultButtonModel.setPressed(Unknown Source) at javax.swing.AbstractButton.doClick(Unknown Source) at com.mathworks.mwswing.plaf.MBasicMenuItemUI.doClick(MBasicMenuItemUI.java:1145) at com.mathworks.mwswing.plaf.MBasicMenuItemUI$MouseInputHandler.mouseReleased(MBasicMenuItemUI.java:973) at java.awt.Component.processMouseEvent(Unknown Source) at javax.swing.JComponent.processMouseEvent(Unknown Source) at java.awt.Component.processEvent(Unknown Source) at java.awt.Container.processEvent(Unknown Source) at java.awt.Component.dispatchEventImpl(Unknown Source) at java.awt.Container.dispatchEventImpl(Unknown Source) at java.awt.Component.dispatchEvent(Unknown Source) at java.awt.LightweightDispatcher.retargetMouseEvent(Unknown Source) at java.awt.LightweightDispatcher.processMouseEvent(Unknown Source) at java.awt.LightweightDispatcher.dispatchEvent(Unknown Source) at java.awt.Container.dispatchEventImpl(Unknown Source) at java.awt.Component.dispatchEvent(Unknown Source) at java.awt.EventQueue.dispatchEvent(Unknown Source) at java.awt.EventDispatchThread.pumpOneEventForHierarchy(Unknown Source) at java.awt.EventDispatchThread.pumpEventsForHierarchy(Unknown Source) at java.awt.EventDispatchThread.pumpEvents(Unknown Source) at java.awt.EventDispatchThread.pumpEvents(Unknown Source) at java.awt.EventDispatchThread.run(Unknown Source)

Summary: When using an Analog Output object with the NI PCI-6014 I receive regeneration and underflow errors.
Description: When using a National Instruments PCI-6014 and creating an analog output object, I receive these errors:Error event occurred at 10:55:38 for the object: nidaqmxDev1-AO.NIDAQmx error: The generation has stopped to prevent the regeneration of old samples.Your application was unable to write samples to the background buffer fast enough toprevent old samples from being regenerated.To avoid this error, you can do any of the following:1. Increase the size of the background buffer by configuring the buffer.2. Increase the number of samples you write each time you invoke a write operation.3. Write samples more often.4. Reduce the sample rate.5. Change the data transfer mechanism from interrupts to DMA if your device supports DMA.6. Reduce the number of applications your computer is executing concurrently.In addition, if you do not need to write every sample that is generated, you can configurethe regeneration mode to allow regeneration, and then use the Position and Offset attributesto write the desired samples.Task Name: MWDAT0038Status Code: -200290  orError event occurred at 09:28:03 for the object: nidaqmxDev1-AO.NIDAQmx error: Onboard device memory underflow. Because of system and/or bus-bandwidthlimitations, the driver could not write data to the device fast enough to keep up withthe device output rate.Reduce your sample rate, alter the data transfer method (from interrupts to DMA), use a product with more onboard memory, or reduce the number of programs your computeris executing concurrently.Task Name: MWDAT0091Status Code: -200016
Workaround: It has been determined that this is a limitation of using the PCI-6014 with the NI-DAQmx driver. To prevent receiving these errors, do not exceed 1000 Samples/second for one channel and 500 Samples/second for two channels.

Summary: Axes limits are no longer present in code generated to recreate figures
Description: As of MATLAB 7.3 (R2006b), the code generated from figure windows using Edit -> Generate MATLAB-file  no longer sets the axes limits. This is not a bug; it is a deliberate change to avoid setting limits that users did not intend in certain kinds of displays.
Workaround: You can restore the prior behavior of code generation to include axes limits using the following steps: Note that you need to remove comments from lines which set axes limits from MATLAB-files generated using the workaround: Rename the file $MATLAB/toolbox/matlab/codetools/private/mcodeDefaultConstructor.m to $MATLAB/toolbox/matlab/codetools/pivate/mcodeDefaultConstructor.m.old, where $MATLAB is the root directory of your MATLAB installation as returned by running the command matlabrootat the MATLAB command prompt.Download the attached file "mcodeDefaultConstructor.m" to the above location.Run the command rehash toolboxcacheAfter you generate code for a figure the resulting file, createfigure.m, opens in the editor. Uncomment the lines below the comment lines % Uncomment the following line to preserve the X-limits of the axesand % Uncomment the following line to preserve the Y-limits of the axesSave the MATLAB-file createfigure.m.

Summary: Retrieving a design causes design requirements to disappear
Description: When you use the SISO Design Tool, retrieving a design from the Design History node causes requirements to disappear from the list on the Design Requirements tab of the Response Optimization node.
Workaround: Download the attached Zip file and extract it to this directory:   matlab\toolbox\sloptim\sloptguis\@srosisotoolgui\@requirementpnl

Summary: Session logs from the Test & Measurement Tool might be missing quotation marks
Description: While using the Test & Measurement Tool (tmtool), MATLAB-files generated under the Session Log tab can produce incorrect code missing single quotes, as in the following line:     fprintf(obj1, 'disp('hello')');  The expected code should have additional single quotes, as shown below:    fprintf(obj1, 'disp(''hello'')');
Workaround: You can edit the saved file to add the missing single quotes.

Summary: isfield function returns the wrong result when used on fields of a class
Description: Testing the field of a class using isfield  returns an incorrect value of true :    x = inline;   isfield(x, 'expr')   ans =        1 In MATLAB 7.1 (R14SP3) and earlier, this correctly returned a value of false .

Summary: Printing from Editor/Debugger sometimes has incorrectly colored text
Description: When printing from the Editor/Debugger, if the File > Page Setup  option for Syntax highlighting  on the Layout  tab is set to the default, Black and white text , some text might print with the wrong color. This happens if you have changed the on-screen Desktop tool colors  for text (via File > Preferences > Colors ) to something other than black. For an MATLAB file, most of the text prints in black, but certain characters (for example, 1 , & , and = ) print in the text color you specified via preferences. For a plain text file, all characters print in the text color you specified via preferences.
Workaround: Select File > Page Setup. On the Layout tab, for Syntax highlighting, select Colored text, and then print to a color printer.If a color printer is not available, select File > Preferences > Colors. For Desktop tool colors, set the Text color to black, and then use black and white printing in the Page Setup dialog box.

Summary: Missing shared library dsp_sim_common
Description: The Link for ModelSim HDL Cosimulation block is dependent on dsp_sim_common shared library, which is installed only with the Signal Processing Blockset.
Workaround: If you don't have the Signal Processing Blockset, replace these files in the MATLABROOT/toolbox/shared/hdllink/ directory with the attached versions:autopopulate.mex???shdlcosim.mex???shdltovcd.mex???Check the existing files in your directory for the appropriate version (???) to download:a64 = Linux 64-bitglx = Linuxsol = Solarisw32 = Windows

Summary: Error when running Simulink model that contains OPC Toolbox 2.0 library blocks
Description: This problem can occur with a Simulink model containing OPC Toolbox library blocks when the model needs to communicate with two servers which are both on the same host. If the name of one of the servers is a substring of the other server name (for example, the one server name is Server1 and the other is Server1a), the following error occurs when the simulation starts.  Cannot perform this operation on vectors of OPC Toolbox objects'

Summary: No error message when there is not enough disk space to create a .CTF file
Description: If there isn't enough disk space to generate the .CTF file, MATLAB Compiler does not throw an error warning the user. However, the generated .CTF file has a size of zero bytes.
Workaround: Freeing up disk space and re-compiling would accurately generate the correct .CTF file. However, it is difficult for the user to pinpoint the exact problem since no error is generated during the compilation.

Summary: Exported global variable name: incorrect code if you use tid or controlPortIdx
Description: Real-time Workshop uses the strings tid  and controlPortIdx  to generate code. If you use these as exported global variable names, Real-time Workshop might produce incorrect output.
Workaround: Do not use tid or controlPortIdx as exported global variable names.

Summary: impulse function may give inaccurate results when the rational function has large poles
Description: The demo called "Modeling a High-Speed Backplane (Part 1: Measured 4-Port S-Parameters to a Rational Function Model)" describes how to use the RF Toolbox impulse  function and the MATLAB filter  function to calculate the output signal that a rational function model produces in response to a given input signal. This approach may produce inaccurate results if the poles of the rational function have large values.
Workaround: Use the attached timeresp function instead of the impulse and filter functions to calculate the output signal. This is shown in the attached demo "Modeling a High-Speed Backplane (Part 1: Measured 4-Port S-Parameters to a Rational Function Model)." To install the files: Determine your MATLAB root directory by entering the matlabroot command at the MATLAB command line. Quit MATLAB. Download timeresp.m and place it in the directory matlabroot\toolbox\rf\rf\@rfmodel\@rational Rename matlabroot\toolbox\rf\rfdemos\rational_differential.m to matlabroot\toolbox\rf\rfdemos\rational_differential.m.oldDownload rational_differential.m and place it in the directory matlabroot\toolbox\rf\rfdemos. Restart MATLAB. Enter rehash toolboxcache at the MATLAB command line.For help using timeresp, type help rfmodel.rational.timeresp at the MATLAB command line.

Summary: Fix to save and load for anonymous functions created from other anonymous functions
Description: Anonymous functions created from other anonymous functions do not save or load correctly.
