For example, in the following MATLAB code,   a = @(x)@(y) x+y;  % The primary anonymous function   b = a(1);          % This creates a function b, that is a special case of function a   c = b(2);          % Just a test to check that the functions evaluate correctly   save example.mat   % Save workspace   clear all;         % Clear workspace   load example.mat   % Attempt to load the saved file The load  command generates the warning   Warning: Could not find appropriate function on path loading function handle >@(y) x+y
Workaround: There are no available workarounds.

Summary: FlightGear animation object play method shows unexpected behavior when used in a loop
Description: Calling the FlightGear animation object play  method more than once causes the FlightGear flight simulator display to flicker.
Workaround: Download the attached file to remove the flicker. To use the update:Installation instructionsLog in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps.Exit MATLAB.Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB>/toolbox/aero/aero/@Aero/@FlightGearAnimation folder.Make a backup copy of each of the files listed in the .zip file. /toolbox/aero/aero/@Aero/@FlightGearAnimation/play.pTo see the contents of the .zip file: Windows Systems*: Open the .zip file and look at the contents.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh):unzip -l <MATLAB>/toolbox/aero/aero/@Aero/@FlightGearAnimation<.zip file>Extract the files from the .zip file. Windows Systems*: Open the .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB>/toolbox/aero/aero/@Aero/@FlightGearAnimation folder.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh): cd <MATLAB>/toolbox/aero/aero/@Aero/@FlightGearAnimationunzip <.zip file>Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB>/toolbox/aero/aero/@Aero/@FlightGearAnimation folder.*Notes for Windows Systems:If you see ˇ°Error: Access is deniedˇ± when attempting to extract the .zip file, you may need to start ˇ°WinZipˇ± in administrator mode. Do this by navigating to WinZip.exe or the WinZip Start menu entry, right-clicking on it, and selecting ˇ°Run as administratorˇ±.From within WinZip, find and open the downloaded .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB>/toolbox/aero/aero/@Aero/@FlightGearAnimation folder.

Summary: dicominfo cannot open a data dictionary specified by a UNC path
Description: dicominfo cannot open a data dictionary that has been specified by UNC path. For example, code similar to the following would trigger this error: info = dicominfo('myfile.dcm','dictionary','\\network\dicom\my-dictionary.txt');??? Unable to open dictionary file (\\\\network\\dicom\\my-dictionary.txt) for reading.
Workaround: To workaround this issue, replace the version of the dicominfo function in your current installation with the updated version contained in the attached file.Exit MATLAB.Make a backup copy of the following file. matlabroot\toolbox\images\medformats\dicominfo.mmatlabroot represents the name of your MATLAB installation directory, as returned by the matlabroot function. For example, rename the existing file, adding "old" to the file extension, dicominfo.m.OLD.Download the attached file, extract dicominfo.m, and store it in the directory specified in the previous step.Restart MATLAB.Run the rehash toolbox command at the MATLAB prompt.

Summary: Uncompilable double reference generated by Real-Time Workshop
Description: Real-Time Workshop can generate an incorrect double reference that looks like &&table_data[0][index]  when the model contains an Interpolation using PreLookup block and the following are all true:    The Interpolation method  of the lookup block is Linear .     The lookup block is placed in a masked subsystem with the table data defined via the mask parameter.     The subsystem is configured and utilized such that it leads to the generation of a reusable function (Real-Time Workshop system code  of the subsystem can be either Reusable function  or Auto ).     The Inline parameters  optimization is enabled.  The correct reference should look like &(&table_data[0])[index] .

Summary: The data conversion method ToArray incorrectly converts a MATLAB sparse array to a native .NET array
Description: If the following MATLAB code:      function out= getSparse        a= [4 2 0 0 3;6 0 5 7 0];                out= sparse(a);  is compiled into a .NET component, using the following MCC command:      mcc -B 'dotnet:sparse,sparseclass,1.1,private' getSparse   then the following code results in a nativeArray with all elements equal to zero:     sparseclass sparse= new sparseclass();    sp = (MWNumericArray)sparse.getSparse();    double[,] nativeArray= (double[,])sp.ToArray(MWArrayComponent.Real);
Workaround: Index the MATLAB sparse array directly as follows: sparseclass sparse= new sparseclass(); sp= (MWNumericArray)sparse.getSparse(); MWNumericArray e1= sp[1,1]; MWNumericArray e2= sp[1,2]; MWNumericArray e5= sp[1,5]; MWNumericArray e6= sp[2,1]; MWNumericArray e7= sp[2,2];

Summary: Plots in "anfis from the Command Line" doc example do not match actual results
Description: Running the example in the "anfis from the Command Line" section of the User's Guide produces plots that do not match the documentation.

Summary: False error message: "This model does not contain any target preference blocks."
Description: The false error message - "This model does not contain any target preference blocks" is posted under specific conditions:During code generation, you try to select and activate another simulink model On the Initialization  tab of a block's or a subsystem's mask, there is a call to the MATLAB gcs  command  These circumstances cause the false error message to be displayed seemingly erratically: This problem can be seen the first time you try to generate code for a model but not the next times. Reversly, the problem can be seen the second time you try to generate code for a specific model but never the first time.
Workaround: Try to not select or activate a different model while simulink is generating code.Do not call the MATLAB gcs command from the Initialization commands of the block or subsystem mask.

Summary: get on a sparse matrix returns NULL for elements with zeros
Description: When accessing the elements of a sparse-format object derived from MWNumericArray, using the get() method, locations with zero value return NULL.  Instead, they should return an object of type java.lang.Double or java.lang.Boolean with value 0.0 or false, respectively.
Workaround: Copy the attached version of the nativemcl75 shared library into /bin/ on the development machine and /runtime/ on a deployment machine. This patch fixes this and other Builder for Java 1.0 issues.

Summary: Java look and feel modified after library initizlization
Description: Instantiating an object of a class generated by Builder for Java in a Swing-based user application may modify the look-and-feel settings, resulting in inconsistent look-and-feel across Swing windows.
Workaround: The application programmer can work around this by always creating the Builder for Java object before the look-and-feel is set and any Swing windows are created.  Alternatively, the look-and-feel settings can be saved before creating a Builder for Java object and restored afterwards.

Summary: Chart input struct member cannot be used as transition condition expression
Description: If you use a chart input struct member as a transition condition expression ˇŞ for example, [input .validity ] , where validity   is a boolean member of struct input   ˇŞ the following error occurs:Chart Input Data 'input' on left hand side of an expression.
Workaround: Change the singleton condition [input.validity] to the relational expression [input.validity != 0].

Summary: Discontinuity in translational and rotational friction blocks
Description: The velocity-force and velocity-torque characteristic curves of Translational Friction and Rotational Friction blocks in the Foundation/Mechanical library exhibit discontinuities at the threshold velocity. This could cause computational failures when the velocity of simulated bodies crosses the threshold velocity.
Workaround: To work around this issue, download the attached platform-specific version of the friction_transl_gen.mex* and friction_rot_gen.mex* files. Here are the steps:   Quit MATLAB  Rename the following files: $MATLAB/toolbox/physmod/network_engine/library/mechanical/translational/extra/friction_transl_gen.mex*   $MATLAB/toolbox/physmod/network_engine/library/mechanical/rotational/extra/friction_rot_gen.mex*   (where $MATLAB is the MATLAB root directory on your machine, as returned by typing  matlabroot  at the MATLAB Command Prompt, and * designates your specific platform.)  Rename the files to friction_transl_gen.mex*.old and friction_rot_gen.mex*.old, respectively.  Download the attached platform-specific files. Place the new files in the same directories mentioned in step 2.  Restart MATLAB.

Summary: Why is the TriggerFrameDelay property not honored when the TriggerType property is Manual?
Description: When I set the TriggerFrameDelay  property on a videoinput object configured to use manual triggering, the property is not honored. This is a bug in the toolbox and will be fixed in a future release.

Summary: Difficulty receiving sparse matrics in JAVA using toArray()
Description: Calling toArray on a sparse MWNumericArray may return incorrect data if there are empty columns. For example:MATLAB code:*****************************function out = returnSparsea = [4 0 0 0 3;0 0 5 7 0];out = sparse(a);*****************************JAVA client code:*****************************Object []obj = g_data.returnSparse(1);MWNumericArray act_sparse = (MWNumericArray)obj[0];double[][] sparse_data = (double[][])act_sparse.toArray();*****************************If data contained in sparse_data is incorrect, the correct data in the first row [4 0 0 0 3] is returned.The data returned in the 2nd row [5 5 5 7 0] is incorrect.
Workaround: Copy the patched version of the nativemcl75 shared library appropriate for your platform to your /bin and /runtime directories.

Summary: Using National Instruments USB-6251 to produce Analog Output, I get a Runtime error popup window.
Description: Using NI USB-6251 to produce Analog Output causes a Microsoft Visual C++ Runtime Library error popup that says:Runtime Error!Program C:\Applications\MATLAB\R2006a\bin\win32\MATLAB.exeThis application has requested the Runtime to terminate in an unusual way.Please contact the application's support team for more information.
Workaround: To work around this issue, download the attached version of the DLL, MWNIDAQMX.DLL, as follows:Quit MATLAB.Rename the following DLL: $MATLAB\toolbox\daq\daq\private\MWNIDAQMX.DLL (where $MATLAB is the MATLAB root directory on your machine, as returned by typingmatlabrootat the MATLAB Command Prompt.) Rename the file to MWNIDAQMX.DLL.old.Download and extract the attached file, MWNIDAQMX.DLL. Place the new file in the same directory mentioned in step 2.Restart MATLAB.

Summary: normxcorr2 can return values outside the range [-1,1] for certain inputs.
Description: When certain inputs are used, normxcorr2  returns a result outside the expected range of [-1,1].
Workaround: To workaround this issue, replace the version of the normxcorr2 function in your current installation with the updated version contained in the attached file.Exit MATLAB.Make a backup copy of the following file. MATLABROOT\toolbox\images\images\normxcorr2.mMATLABROOT represents the name of your MATLAB installation directory, as returned by the matlabroot function. For example, rename the existing file, adding "old" to the file extension, normxcorr2.m.OLD.Download the attached file, extract normxcorr2.m, storing it the directory specified in the previous step.Restart MATLAB.Run the rehash toolbox command at the MATLAB prompt.

Summary: Applying an invalid value in a data object property dialog may hang MATLAB
Description: Entering an invalid value in the property dialog box of a Simulink data object may put MATLAB in a bad state. For example, setting the Value  property of the mpt.Parameter to an invalid MATLAB expression in the Model Explorer and hitting the Apply  button may cause the dialog view to crash and split vertically.
Workaround: Ensure that the values entered in a data object property dialog are valid before applying them.

Summary: Sparse Element-wise multiplication uses too much memory.
Description: Element-wise multiplication of an all zero sparse matrix by another matrix results in a matrix with more space for nonzeros than necessary.  For example, the statement   B = sparse(2000,2000).*eye(2000); results in a sparse matrix with room for 4000000 nonzero elements, although every element of B  is zero.Similarly,   C = sparse(2000,2000).*speye(2000); results in a matrix with room for 2000 nonzero elements.
Workaround: There are no known workarounds.

Summary: Error occurs when?modifying control architecture using the SISO Design Tool after?a sample time conversion
Description: The following error occurs when using the SISO Design Tool?to modify?control architecture after performing a sample time conversion: Import Error: The loop components must be either all continuous or discrete
Workaround: Start a new SISO Design Tool session and select the new architecture before importing the system data.

Summary: hdf5write creates corrupt character datasets when passed multi-dimensional character arrays
Description: When given multi-dimensional character arrays, hdf5write  processes the array but produces corrupt datasets. In the following example, the variable c  is a multi-dimensional cell array. c={'asdfasdfasdfasdfa','qwerqwerqwerqwer'} c=char(c) hdf5write('ASI11.h5', '/c',c);  hdf5write  now returns an error when passed a multi-dimensional array.

Summary: Automated IMC tuning returns wrong answer when IMC configuration is selected in SISOTOOL
Description: Configuration 5 in SISOTOOL represents a typical IMC structure, where G1 is the true plant, G2 is the approximation and C is the IMC controller. User can use the IMC tuning method in the 'Automated Tuning' tab to design C. Instead of G2, G1 minus G2 is used as the internal model in IMC by mistake. As a result, the tuning method returns a wrong IMC controller C.
Workaround: Download the attachment and unzip its content to a temporary directory. Replace MATLABROOT/toolbox/control/ctrlguis/@sisogui/@AutomatedTuning/buildPanel_IMC.m and MATLABROOT/toolbox/control/ctrlguis/@sisogui/@AutomatedTuning/refreshcomp.m.

Summary: Draw Shapes block might not draw antialiased lines properly
Description: The Draw Shapes block does not draw antialiased lines properly if the following three conditions are met:1. The Use antialiasing check box is selected. 2. The input to the Pts port is a vector, [r1,c1,r2,c2].3. The input to the Pts port meets the following conditions:   a. c2 < c1   b. r2 > r1   c. abs(r2-r1) > (c2-c1)
Workaround: To workaround this issue, clear the Use antialiasing check box.

Summary: Functions mapshow and geoshow may plot polygons in the wrong axes
Description: If an axes is specified when displaying polygon data with mapshow  or geoshow , polygons should be constructed in that axes. Instead, they are constructed in the current axes, then re-parented. Worse, the re-parenting step leaves the edge-lines of the polygons behind in the current axes. When the specified axes is  the current axis, there is no issue. However, if an axes is specified and it is not current, the results in both axes will be incorrect.
Workaround: To workaround this issue, replace the mappolygon.m and Polygon.m.m function files in your current installation with the newer versions in the attachment.Exit MATLAB.Make a backup copy of the following files. toolbox/map/map/private/mappolygon.mtoolbox/map/map/@MapGraphics/@Polygon/Polygon.mmatlabroot represents the name of your MATLAB installation directory, as returned by the matlabroot function.For example, rename the existing file, adding "old" to the file extension, mappolygon.m.OLD.Copy the versions of mappolygon.m and Polygon.m.m from the attachment into the directories specified in the previous step.Restart MATLAB.Run the rehash toolbox command at the MATLAB prompt.

Summary: Bioinformatics Toolbox functions dnds.m and dndsml.m return complex numbers.
Description: Bioinformatics Toolbox function dnds.m  or dndsml.m  returns complex numbers. This is caused by input sequences that are too divergent or not codon-aligned (have frame shifts). dnds.m  and dndsml.m  assume input sequences are codon-aligned. When sequences are too divergent, the Jukes-Cantor and/or the Kimura Two-parameter models are not adequate to account for multiple substitutions at the same site; this is also known as model saturation .
Workaround: If you have Bioinformatics Toolbox version 2.1.1 or later, you can update the functions dnds.m and dndsml.m and you can get the new utility function seqinsertgaps.m by using the following procedure.1. In the MATLAB Command Window type which dnds.mThis displays the location of the file dnds.m.2. Rename dnds.m to dnds.m.old and rename dndsml.m to dndsml.m.old3. Download the updated files attached to this bug report to the directory identified with the which command.4. In the MATLAB Command Window type rehash toolboxdnds.m and dndsml.m have improved warning traps that aid in detecting potential problems with the input sequences. Also, in cases where saturation is reached, the functions return NaNs. The updated functions also contain examples that illustrate how to use the new utility function seqinsertgaps.m to assure that codon-aligned sequences are used as inputs.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: right-click build subsystem might use different sample time for root input than that used for the model
Description: When all of the following conditions exist and you use the right-click build subsystem feature to generate code, the generated code might use sample time information that is different from what is indiciated in the parent model: The subsystem for which you use the right-click build subsystem feature is inside a triggered function-call subsystem that is triggered periodically. One of subsystem's input signals is a virtual bus signal. Sources of the virtual bus signal have different sample times than the periodic trigger rate of the function-call subsystem. Rate transition diagnostics of the tasking mode set for the model (Multitask rate transition  or Single task rate transition  on the Diagnostics>Sample Time  pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box) is turned off.  This occurs most commonly when the model is configured for single-tasking mode.
Workaround: Enable rate transition diagnostics for the tasking mode in use for the model. The default for Multitask rate transition is error. The default for Single task rate transition is none. If Tasking mode for periodic sample times on the Solvers pane is set to SingleTasking, enable diagnostics for signle-tasking rate transitions by setting Single task rate transition on the Diagnostics>Sample Time pane to warning or error.If you enable rate transition diagnostics, ensure that Rate Transition blocks are inserted explicitly in the model where needed, or select the Automatically handle data transfers between tasks option on the Solvers pane to instruct Simulink to insert them implicitly as needed.

Summary: German Windows locale settings cause arrow key presses to insert "^" character
Description: When Windows locale settings are set to German and certain Simulink dialogs are opened, pressing the left or right arrow keys in an editable widget causes a "^" character to be inserted, instead of the cursor moving left or right as expected.
Workaround: Configure Windows to use English locale settings.

Summary: BUILDMCR fails on Windows XP 64
Description: Running BUILDMCR on a computer running the Windows XP 64-bit operating system fails to build the MCRInstaller.zip file, and results in this series of errors: ??? Error using ==> buildmcr>find_filesDirectory C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2006b\sys\java\jre\win32 does not exist.Error in ==> buildmcr>match_files at 691    fileList = [ fileList ; ...Error in ==> buildmcr at 303    additional_file_list_win32 = [additional_file_list_win32 ; ...
Workaround: To work around this problem, backup your existing BUILDMCR file, then replace your existing BUILDMCR with the attached file.

Summary: Code generation may fail if initial state loaded from workspace
Description: During code generation for a model that loads its initial state from the workspace, the TLC error "Unable to find FirstRootIdx within the DWork scope" may occur, as in the following example: Error: File: D:\Applications\MATLAB\R2006a\rtw\c\tlc\lib\blocklib.tlcLine: 753 Column: 44Unable to find FirstRootIdx within the DWork scope
Workaround: When generating code, do not load the initial state from the workspace.To avoid the load, unselect the configuration parameter "Initial state"on the "Configuration Parameters" dialog "Data Import/Export" tab.

Summary: Optimization bug can cause accelerated simulation or code generation to fail
Description: An optimization bug can cause simulation to fail when using the Simulink Accelerator, or code generation to fail using Real-Time Workshop. The optimization improves generated code by eliminating unnecessary temporary variables for muxed signals. When the bug occurs, code generation ceases and the following error message appears, followed by a stack trace: Error using ==> rtwgenUnexpected or internal error encountered in "in CGIR:.\rtwcg_utils\rtwcg_vcat_scal.cpp line 489:newStartIdx <= r->getFinalIndex()".
Workaround: The optimization that causes this error can be disabled using the MATLAB script in the attached zip file:rtwcgVCatScalarization(0);After disabling the optimization, retry the accelerated simulation or regenerate the code.

Summary: Controllable Friction Clutch in Unidirectional Mode Sometimes Rotates in Wrong Direction
Description: The Controllable Friction Clutch with Directionality  set to Unidirectional  and with zero input pressure can sometimes rotate in the direction it should not, i.e., backwards.
Workaround: Provide a very small pressure signal to the Controllable Friction Clutch, instead of zero. The pressure input can be as small as you want as long as it is greater than zero.  You input the pressure signal through the P inport on the block.

Summary: Quitting the more function might not stop writing to diary nor stop a running MATLAB file
Description: These are two situations when quitting more  might produce unexpected behavior: When more  is on, and diary  is on, if you type q  to terminate more , the diary  filename might contain text that did not display in the Command Window. This extra text would have appeared if more  had been off, or if you had not terminated more . When more  is on, if you run an M-file that displays text output and then continues processing, typing q  terminates more  and text display stops, as expected. However, the MATLAB file might continue processing. In previous versions, quitting more  also stopped processing.
Workaround: There is no known workaround that prevents the diary filename from including extra output.To terminate more and stop processing for a running MATLAB file, use Ctrl+C instead of q.

Summary: Documentation incorrectly states that TriggerCondition property is fixed at PositiveEdge for National Instruments Analog Output objects.
Description: In the documentation for Data Acquisition Toolbox versions prior to R2006b, and the R2006b documentation on the product CD, the TriggerCondition  property states the following for National Instruments analog output objects when the TriggerType  is set to HwDigital :For AO objects, TriggerCondition is fixed at PositiveEdge. This is incorrect -- in this case, TriggerCondition  is fixed at NegativeEdge .

Summary: Error when assigning experimental data to a matrix-valued signal in a Transient Data panel.
Description: When I assign experimental data to a Simulink model that has a matrix-valued signal, I get the following error:  ??? Error using ==> catCAT arguments dimensions are not consistent.Error in ==> speimporttable.iotable.edit>localImportFromWorkspace at 212  importSelector.workpanel.import(importSelector.importtable);??? Error while evaluating javax.swing.JButton ActionPerformedCallback
Workaround: When the signal (data) contains multiple channels stored as a matrix, pasteData.m calculates the total number of channels incorrectly. To fix this error, follow this procedure: Open the file pasteData.m in your existing MATLAB installation.  This file resides in the following directory:$MATLABROOT\toolbox\slestim\slestguis\@speimporttable\@iotable\pasteData.mwhere $MATLABROOT represents the name of your MATLAB root directory, as returned by the matlabroot function. Replace the following text in line 77:sum(cell2mat(get(dataObj,{'Dimensions'})))- ...withsum(cellfun(@prod, get(dataObj,{'Dimensions'})))- ...

Summary: arrayfun on nested functions not working correctly.
Description: The arrayfun  function does not work correctly when trying to nest anonymous functions. For example, the function shown here is nested and returns an incorrect answer of 11:   adders_b = arrayfun(@(x)@(y)x+y, 1:10, 'UniformOutput', 0);   adders_b{1}(1)
Workaround: There is no workaround at this time.

Summary: The MPL PATI PWM measure block returns incorrect maximum counts
Description: The MPL PATI PWM measurement block might return an overflow count that is negative. This will occur when the input stops long enough for the 32-bit counter to overflow. With a 20 MHz reference source, the counter will overflow in approximately 107 seconds.
Workaround: As a workaround:If the block output becomes negative, consider the output to be an infinite result. This is the same as the result you would achieve with a zero frequency input. Note that a negative count is completely unphysical; you should be able to easily catch such a value and deal with it.

Summary: Farrow filter reference page has incorrect signal flow diagram and properties
Description: The reference page for farrow provides a signal flow diagram for a fixed-point Farrow filter. The diagram is wrong. It shows a direct-form filter rather than the Farrow filter.  Also, some fixed-point Farrow filter properties are not included on the list of properties for the fixed-point Farrow filter and some properties are included that do not apply to the Farrow filter.
Workaround: The attached file farrow_fixed_point_signal_flow.gif provides the correct signal flow diagram. Also, the figure shows the data format properties (word and fraction lengths) for the filter properly.

Summary: Dialog box functions do not work when DefaultFigureWindowStyle is set to 'docked'
Description: Various dialog box functions do not work when you define a DefaultFigureWindowStyle  that is set to 'docked' . The affected functions are: dialog, errordlg, helpdlg, inputdlg, msgbox, questdlg, and warndlg. Instead of displaying a dialog box, these functions return an error.

Description: When you attempt to move a file using the MOVEFILE function, MATLAB, in some cases, creates a copy of the original file instead of deleting it.  This occurs if the original file, which is in use and therefore locked, cannot be deleted.

Description: When using the HDF Import Tool (hdftool ) to view an HDF file, certain fields that were present in versions of this tool prior to MATLAB R2006a are no longer displayed. These fields appeared in the metadata pane, when the top-level (file) node is selected.

Summary: Uncompilable code: Array parameter with non-Default custom storage class
Description: The problem can occur when an array parameter is used in a block, such as a lookup table block, and the parameter is specified with a Simulink data object with:     RTWInfo.StorageClass  set to Custom      CustomStorageClass  set to something other than Default   In this case, the generated code for the block may be uncompilable due to an incorrect double reference, for example: &&table_param[0][tableStartIdx]       which should have been one of the following:     &table_param[tableStartIdx]         &(&table_param[0])[tableStartIdx]
Workaround: No workaround is available for this bug. The MathWorks strongly recommends that you avoid this bug by upgrading to a newer release.

Summary: Changing the parent of a uipanel and subsequently deleting the panel might cause MATLAB to terminate
Description: If you assign a new parent (a different figure) to a uipanel containing a uicontrol, the uicontrol might not be correctly?parented to the new figure. This happens when the uipanel is contained (nested) within another uipanel. If you delete the uipanel (or the new parent figure)?and then?delete the old parent figure, MATLAB?might crash.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Nested For Iterator subsystem might generate incorrect code
Description: A nested For Interator subsystem generates multiple loop-indexing variables with the same name, resulting in name shadowing. Real-Time Workshop might generate incorrect code due to the name shadowing.

Summary: Bug in the implementation of Berlekey-Massey algorithm (berlekamp.m) affecting Reed-Solomon decoder results
Description: The Reed-Solomon decoder in Communications Toolbox 3.1 (R14SP2) sometimes corrects an error pattern to an invalid codeword. An example of this can be seen when running the following command for an RS(7,3) code: [Mdec,NumCorr,CorrC] = rsdec(gf([0 0 0 0 1 1 3],3),7,3) The following result is obtained:Mdec = GF(2^3) array. Primitive polynomial = D^3+D+1 (11 decimal)Array elements =      4     0     0NumCorr =     1CorrC = GF(2^3) array. Primitive polynomial = D^3+D+1 (11 decimal)Array elements =     4     0     0     0     1     1     3 The result indicates a correction of 1 symbol error and the resulting corrected codeword is [4 0 0 0 1 1 3] , which is not a valid codeword. The correct codeword for an input of the symbols [4 0 0] is: rsenc(gf([4 0 0],3),7,3) ans = GF(2^3) array. Primitive polynomial = D^3+D+1 (11 decimal)Array elements =      4     0     0     5     4     5     1
Workaround: There are no workarounds.

Summary: Incorrect code generation: Uncompilable code can occur for wide function call subsystems attached to an ISR
Description: Real-Time Workshop can generate uncompilable code for a function call subsystem with a wide control input port under following circumstance: The first element of the wide function call input port is connected to a regular function call generator such as an S-function or a Stateflow chart with function call output events. The second or higher element of the wide function call input port is  connected to an s-function that is configured to attache its function call output to an ISR (interrupt service routine).
Workaround: No workaround is available for this bug. The MathWorks strongly recommends that you avoid this bug by upgrading to a newer release.

Summary: Persistent variable not updated correctly on persistent function call
Description: In some cases, a persistent variable may not update correctly when modified in a recursive function call.
Workaround: Type 'feature accel off' at the MATLAB command prompt to disable the accelerator.

Summary: Exiting MATLAB fails if in debug mode and if the file has unsaved changes
Description: If you debug an MATLAB file, modify it in the Editor/Debugger but do not save your changes, and then quit MATLAB, MATLAB does not terminate. A dialog box opens prompting you to save changes to the file. If you click Yes , another dialog box opens prompting you to exit debug mode before saving the file. If you click OK , MATLAB does not complete quitting.
Workaround: After exiting debug mode and saving the file, quit MATLAB again.

Summary: save may interpret argument to -regexp flag as filename instead of pattern
Description: To save MATLAB data to a file using the -regexp  option, type     save filename -regexp expr1 expr2 ... To save to a file using the default filename, you should be able to use     save -regexp expr1 expr2 ... in which case MATLAB should save to a file named matlab.mat .  This does not work due to a bug in the save  function.  Instead, this command uses the first regular expression argument expr1  as the filename and any remaining input arguments starting with expr2 as the regular expressions.  This bug makes it impossible to use the -regexp  flag with patterns and also save to the default filename.

Summary: Problems printing the percent character (%) in an error message
Description: Printing an error message containing the percent character (%) may result in garbled output, or crash MATLAB. For example, running this script should produce the following output: a = [1 2];b = a(3); % Testing??? Attempted to access a(3); index out of bounds because numel(a)=2.Error in ==> foo at 2b = a(3); % Testing The last line of the error message includes the second line of the script, and that line contains the % character. As a result, either garbled characters are printed, or, in some cases, MATLAB may crash. Note the problem occurs only if the % character appears in an error message. It is not a problem to use the % character as usual in MATLAB programming.
Workaround: Fix the error in the MATLAB code, since if there is no error, there is no error message.Try to eliminate the use of the % character from the line which produces the error. This may be difficult if it is used for formatting in an sprintf or similar command.

Summary: Bus signal logs may be incomplete
Description: Simulink may not log all the data in a bus signal under the following conditions: The signal logging option is selected on the bus signal's Signal Properties  dialog box. One of the bus's components originates from a Selector block. The Selector block reorders or partially selects the input signal.  In this case, the log omits some of the Selector signal data.

Summary: Array Editor rounds down values of scalar floating-point variables
Description: The Array Editor rounds down values of scalar floating-point variables. For example x = pix =    3.1416openvar x The Array Editor opens, showing the value of x  as 3  instead of 3.1416 .
Workaround: As an easy, temporary workaround, view the value another way, such as:Type the name of the variable in the MATLAB Command Window.View the value in the Workspace Browser.To permanently correct the problem in the Array Editor, download the attached file, and use it to replace the existing version, which is matlabroot/toolbox/matlab/codetools/arrayviewfunc.m.

Summary: Linearization not available for machines inside Atomic Subsystems that minimize algebraic loops
Description: MATLAB crashes if you try to linearize a model containing a SimMechanics machine inside of an Atomic Subsystem with the Minimize algebraic loop occurrences  option selected.
Workaround: Deselect the Minimize algebraic loop occurrences check box on the subsystem parameters dialog for all Atomic Subsystems in the hierarchy that contains SimMechanics machines.

Summary: Getting the value of a discrete state in a triggered referenced model can cause MATLAB to crash
Description: Executing the command: Simulink.BlockDiagram.getInitialState('model')  crashes MATLAB if 'model'  references function-call models that have blocks with discrete states, e.g., Unit Delay, Discrete-Time  Integrator, etc.

Summary: Bioinformatics function RMABACKADJ returns bad parameter estimates on some datasets.
Description: Bioinformatics Toolbox function RMABACKADJ returns bad parameter estimates on some datasets. The function displays a "Divided by zero" warning, and the background adjustment plot does not show up when using the 'Showplot' option. The function returns NaNs and negative numbers.
Workaround: If you have Bioinformatics Toolbox version 2.3 or later, you can update the function RMABACKADJ by using the following procedure.1. In the MATLAB Command Window type which rmabackadj.mThis displays the location of the file rmabackadj.m.2. Rename rmabackadj.m to rmabackadj.bak.3. Download the updated file attached to this bug report to the directory identified with the which command.

Summary: Output of savepath corrupted under certain circumstances
Description: On UNIX, the output of the savepath  function is corrupted if any directory on the MATLAB path contains the MATLAB root as a subdirectory. Error example 1: Consider a case where matlabroot  is set to /local/matlab  and there exists a subdirectory under matlabroot /work  with a full pathname of matlabroot /work/matlabroot  . Executing the following MATLAB commands produces    cd /local/matlab/work   addpath /local/matlab/work/local/matlab   savepath('x.m')   x   ??? Error: File: /local/matlab/work/x.m Line: 17 Column: 1   A MATLAB string constant is not terminated properly. and the file x.m  contains lines like those shown here:    ...   p = [...   %%% BEGIN ENTRIES %%%     matlabroot,'/work     matlabroot,':', ...     Error example 2: Consider a directory /tmp/work  and a subdirectory with a full pathname of /tmp/work/matlabroot  . Executing the following MATLAB commands produces    cd /tmp/work   addpath /tmp/work/local/matlab   savepath('x.m')   x   ??? Error: File: /tmp/work/x.m Line: 17 Column: 1   Unexpected MATLAB operator. and the file x.m  contains lines like those shown here:    ...   p = [...   %%% BEGIN ENTRIES %%%     /tmp/work     matlabroot,':', ...           In both cases, matlabroot  should be substituted only if it occurs at the beginning of a matlabpath  path entry and nowhere else in the path entry.

Summary: For the C281x Timer Block the calculation of Timer period does not include a prescaler
Description: In the C281x Timer Block, the description of the Timer period  parameter is incorrect. The description states that the term clock cycles  refers to the high-speed peripheral clock on the C281x chip. This description is missing the timer control input clock prescaler of 128.
Workaround: The parameter Timer period is set in clock cycles. The length of the clock cycle is calculated as follows:SYSCLK(150MHz) > HISPCLK (1/2) > TimerControlInputClockPrescaler (1/128)which means that the system clock frequency of 150MHz is divided by the high speed peripheral clock prescaler of 2, and then further divided by the timer control input clock prescaler of 128. The resulting frequency is .586MHz. So, the length of one clock cycle is:1/.586MHz = 1.706 micro seconds.Now, keeping in mind that each clock cycle equals 1.706 micro seconds, set the Timer period to the number of clock cycles - 0 to 65535.

Summary: dted fails to snap out input limits to west and south
Description: When given latlim  and lonlim  input, function dted  needs to snap the limits outward to DTED cell boundaries.  This works properly to the north and east, but may fall short by one column on the west and/or one row to the south.
Workaround: To work around this issue, replace the version of the dted function in your current installation with the updated version in the attachment, as follows:Exit MATLAB.Make a backup copy of the following file. matlabroot/toolbox/map/mapformats/dted.mmatlabroot represents the name of your MATLAB installation directory, as returned by the matlabroot function.For example, rename the existing file, adding "old" to the file extension, dted.m.OLD.Copy the version of dted.m from the attachment into the directory specified in the previous step.Restart MATLAB.Run the rehash toolbox command at the MATLAB prompt.

Summary: MATLAB Builder for JAVA component throws Couldn't load Look and Feel message when used in client application on 64-bit Linux platform.
Description: When a component built using MATLAB Builder for JAVA is used in a client application on 64-bit Linux platform, the following warning message is displayed: ************************************* Couldn't load Look and Feel: java.lang.ClassNotFoundException: com/jgoodies/looks/plastic/Plastic3DLookAndFeel *************************************  This warning only occurs on 64-bit Linux, and does not affect the functioning of the user application.  It can safely be ignored.
Workaround: To workaround this problem, use the following code in the JAVA client application before the MATLAB Builder for JAVA generated component is instantiated: ************************************* System.setProperty("mathworks.jgoodies.enable","false"); *************************************

Summary: RTW build error for two Communications Blockset application-specific demos
Description: Generating an RTW build for the two application demos (commwcdmaphlayer.mdl , commwcdmamuxandcoding.mdl ) will error.
Workaround: This is due to an incorrect file inclusion in the build process by the file matlabroot/toolbox/commblks/commblksdemos/commwcdma_ratematching.c.An updated version of this file is included in the attached .zip file to avoid the error. Please update this file in the matlabroot/toolbox/commblks/commblksdemos/ folder.

Summary: Acquisition times out when collecting 2.14 billion samples and using the SampleAcquiredFcn callback.
Description: I have an infinite acquisition but after running for many hours I receive an error message:A timeout occurred during GETDATA.It seems to happen after I've acquired approximately 2.14 billion samples. With a sample rate of 50,000 samples/second, the timeout occurs at about 12 hours. With a sample rate of 500,000 samples/second, it occurs at about 1.2 hours.I'm using the SamplesAcquiredFcn callback to control when getdata is called.
Workaround: This problem is caused by overflowing an internal counter that is a 32-bit integer, which can represent only 2.14 billion (2.14e9) samples. Collecting 50,000 samples/second for 12 hours is 50000*60*60*12 = 2.16e9 samples.You can work around this problem by stopping the current acquisition before it reaches 2.14e9 samples and immediately starting it again.Here is an example of code that stops and restarts the acquisition before it reaches 2.14e samples. It uses the SamplesAcquiredFcn callback.acquisitionSeconds=60;SampleRate = 50000;ai=analoginput('nidaq','Dev1');set(ai,'SampleRate',SampleRate);chan = addchannel(ai,0);set(ai,'SamplesPerTrigger',Inf);set(ai, 'SamplesAcquiredFcnCount',ai.SampleRate*acquisitionSeconds);set(ai, 'SamplesAcquiredFcn', {@dealWithData,acquisitionSeconds});start(ai);wait(ai,Inf);%*************************************************function dealWithData(ai,time_fields,secduration)alldata = getdata(ai,ai.SampleRate*secduration);% do something with the data here%******START WORKAROUND CODE*****if ai.SamplesAcquired > 2e9  % stop/restart before overflow 32-bit integer    stop(ai);    start(ai);end%******END WORKAROUND CODE*****

Summary: MATLAB fails to find an overloaded method using a different case
Description: If you call a MATLAB file method using a method name that agrees with the MATLAB file name except in letter case (e.g., calling method myfunc.m  as MyFunc ), MATLAB may not be able to find the file. This bug is limited only to MATLAB's built-in types, like double  and struct . You should not be affected by it when working with a user-defined type. For example, assume directory /test  is on the path, and user has a method like the following:     /test/@double/meth.m Calling the method as Meth(1)  in this case fails to find the file @double/meth.m . And, if you happen to have a MATLAB file named meth.m  on the path, MATLAB will incorrectly invoke that function instead. To summarize, this bug happens when the following conditions are met:The method you are calling is one of the MATLAB pre-defined built-in types.The method is a MATLAB file method.You call the method using a different letter case.
Workaround: Use the exact method name to invoke it.

Summary: C28x DMC library Ramp Control block outputs wrong values when used with memory optimization
Description: C28x DMC library Ramp Control block outputs wrong values when used in configurations where Simulink performs optimization on memory.Some block settings are incompatible with Simulink optimizations, causing errors in the output values.
Workaround: Close MATLAB Browse to the following directory: [matlabroot]\toolbox\rtw\targets\tic2000\tic2000blks  Backup the following file by renaming it: tidmcrampcntl.mexw32   Copy the attached file into the following directory: [matlabroot]\toolbox\rtw\targets\tic2000\tic2000blks  Open MATLAB At the command prompt, type: rehash toolboxcache

Summary: packNGo function: files from _sharedutils directory missing from generated zip file
Description: If the following conditions exist for a model, the packNGo  function fails to include shared utility header files in the compressed file that it generates: The model uses a shared utility library. Real-Time Workshop generates shared utility header files, but no source files (for example, *.c), and places them in the shared utilities directory. You use the packNGo  function to find and package all files needed to build an executable image.

Summary: dicomwrite returns error message about missing Group field
Description: With dicomwrite , setting the 'CreateMode'  parameter to 'Copy'  enables writing DICOM files for many Information Object Definitions (IOD).  But under certain circumstances, it can produce the following error message: ??? Reference to non-existent field 'Group'.Error in ==> etest at 3dicomwrite(I,'test',info,'CreateMode','copy');

Two known cases are: If you change the Digital DATCOM structure parameter in the Digital Datcom Forces and Moments block from one that is not in the MATLAB workspace to one that exists in the MATLAB workspace, the block generates a warning, but will operate normally. The warning should be similiar to the following: Warning: When displaying: "mddatwarn2/Digital DATCOM Forces and Moments", the expression "ports(12).type" could not be evaluated. Summary: Incorrect report of unresolved symbol on custom code structure field with LCC compiler
Description: When you use the LCC compiler, Stateflow incorrectly reports structure data elements as unresolved symbols when accessed in a Stateflow chart by custom C code pointers. For example, suppose myptr  is a custom C code pointer to structure data which contains the array field ele . When you try to access ele  using myptr  ˇŞ for example as myptr->ele[0]  ˇŞ Stateflow cannot resolve the symbol ele .
Workaround: Use one of the following workarounds:Choose a different mex compiler (other than LCC). To setup a mex compiler, use the following command: >> mex -setupTurn on Stateflow coder option ignoreUnresolvedSymbols before building and simulating the model. Use the following command: >> sfc('coder_options','ignoreUnresolvedSymbols',1)

Description: On Windows, an RTW build for three product demos (commcdma2000 , commwcdmaphlayer , and commwcdmamuxandcoding ) will fail if matlabroot  (where MATLAB is installed on your system, as determined by running the matlabroot  function in the MATLAB Command Window) contains white space.

Summary: User-controlled memory mapping demo does not work
Description: The demo User-Controlled Memory Mapping demo employs the new Memory Sections  option under Real-Time Workshop. A required file, csc_registration.m , that defines the various memory sections in the demo is not included in the directory \toolbox\rtw\targets\tic6000\tic6000demos\@tic6000demospkg . This missing file causes the demo not to work properly.
Workaround: Download the attached ZIP archive tic6000_patch_329409.zip and the TIC6000 patch installer tic6000_patch.m. After you download the patch and installer, start MATLAB and enter the following command at the MATLAB command prompt to install the patch.  tic6000_patch('tic6000_patch_329409.zip', 'install')  Patch tic6000_patch_329409.zip installs csc_registration.m file required for the demo to run correctly.

Summary: S-functions: different simulation results for S-function generated by right-click build
Description: An S-function block simulates differently than its original subsystem under the following conditions:You generate the S-function by executing a right-click build on the subsystem. The subsystem contains a function-call subsystem that is not the child of another function-call subsystem. The reset state of the abovementioned function-call subsystem is set to inherit .
Workaround: Change the reset state of the function-call subsystem to reset or held.

Summary: coewrite exports filter coefficients incorrectly for unsigned filters
Description: coewrite  does not consider the value of the Signed  property for the filter object when you export the filter object to a XILINX .COE  file. When you export the filter with coewrite , the coefficients in the output .coe  file are not correct.

Summary: Optimized ERT targets do not work with Generate an example main program off
Description: When using an optimized ERT target, if you clear the  Generate an example main program  option (on the Real-Time Workshop /Template  pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box or Model Explorer), the generated code will not be able to compile with the default static ert_main.c  shipped in the product.  You will likely encounter an error similar to the following:S:\A1\matlab\rtw\c\ert\ert_main.c(105) : fatal error C1189: #error :  The terminate function is required by ert_main.c. You must update ert_main.c to suit your application needs, or select the 'Terminate function required' option. NMAKE : fatal error U1077: 'cl' : return code '0x2'
Workaround: You can work around this problem in the following ways:Select the Generate an example main program optionSupply a custom static ert_main.c that does not require a model_terminate functionSelect the standard ERT target with no auto-configuration

Summary: Axis labels obscured when legend position is "SouthOutside" or "WestOutside"
Description: When a legend is placed outside the axes in the 'SouthOutside'  or 'WestOutside'  positions, it overlaps axis labels. The following code demonstrates this:plot(rand(100,1))xlabel('X label')ylabel('Y label')legend('first','location','southoutside')

Summary: Recursive Embedded MATLAB function calls cause errors in Stateflow
Description: Embedded MATLAB functions in Stateflow that call themselves may hang.  You may not be able to recover by typing Ctrl-C or stopping the model via the UI.  Recursive function calls are not yet supported.  They are intended to error.
Workaround: Do not write recursive Embedded MATLAB functions.  Use loops for iteration.

Summary: PlotFcns error when docked
Description: The plot functions in the Optimization Toolbox and Genetic Algorithm and Direct Search Toolbox that are specified with the PlotFcns  option are not currently dockable. If one or more Plot Tools panes are opened after the figure window is open, the figure becomes docked. Optimization solvers error when the already opened figure is used to plot any of the plot functions.
Workaround: To work around this issue, set the 'WindowStyle' property of the figure to 'normal' before running the solver again. The following command will set the 'WindowStyle' to 'normal':>>set(gcf,'WindowStyle','normal')

Summary: Relay block gives incorrect answer with scalar input and vector output parameters
Description: When the Relay block's Output when on  or Output when off  parameter is a vector and its input is a scalar, the block outputs only the first element correctly.
Workaround: Expand the scalar input to vector input.

Summary: Assignment into a large block of a sparse matrix does not work properly.
Description: Assigning into a block of a sparse matrix which has more than 2^31 - 1  elements does not work properly, and may in fact result in indexed deletion.  For exampleA = sparse(131072,131072,1);B = sparse(131072,65536,1);A(:,1:2:131072) = B; results in indexed deletion.  That is, after this assignment operation, the size of A  is 131072  by 65536 .  Note that B  has 2^33  elements (but exactly one nonzero).
Workaround: Use a for loop to break the assignment into blocks which are smaller than 2^31 elements.  For example,A = sparse(131072, 131072, 1);B = sparse(131072, 65536, 1);chunksize = 65536/4;for k = 0:3  startB = k*chunksize + 1;  finishB = (k+1)*chunksize;  start = 2*startB - 1;  finish = 2*finishB - 1;  A(:,start:2:finish) = B(:,startB:finishB);endis equivalent toA(:,1:2:131072) = B;

Summary: Using "name" as a mask parameter in a library block breaks models that use the block
Description: Using "name" as a mask parameter name in a library block breaks models that use the block. Specifically, reloading the model breaks connections to the block's ports. In general, using built-in block parameter names as mask parameter names causes problems with models.
Workaround: Rename your mask parameters to use names that differ from existing block parameter names.

Summary: Why do I receive an invalid link when no DCAM cameras are connected?
Description: If I do not have any DCAM cameras connected, when I type:info = imaqhwinfo('dcam')  in MATLAB, I receive the warning:Warning: No DCAM compliant devices were found. If you have a DCAM compliant device, refer to the DCAM troubleshooting section in the toolbox User Guide for information on setting up your device.  If I then click on the link in the warning, in the help browser I see the error: Warning - Documentation File Not Found MATLAB cannot link to this file: jar:file:D:/MATLAB/MATLABR2006a/help/toolbox/imaq/help.jar!/tshoot5.htmlIf the link is to a documentation file for a product that is not installed, you cannot view it in the Help browser. You can view documentation for that product at Documentation on the MathWorks Web site. Otherwise, the link might be invalid. Please report the problem at https://www.mathworks.com/support/forms/ts/doc.shtml
Workaround: To view the help, see the following URL:https://www.mathworks.com/access/helpdesk/help/toolbox/imaq/f16-75694.html

Summary: Segmentation violation when creating a function model in CAGE
Description: When creating a function model in CAGE, a segmentation violation may appear in the Command Window.  This only happens if you first load a CAGE project file that contains a particular function model and then try to create a new function model with an identical string.
Workaround: Two possible workarounds are available:If you want the new function to be in the same file as the existing one, you can duplicate the existing function model and then change its inputs as required.Since this bug is only triggered by using identical strings in two function models, you can add insignificant spaces to the string of the new function model, for example add a space at the end of the string.

Summary: BUSDATE reshapes inputs in 2006b.
Description: Input and output dimensions are not consistent when using BUSDATE in MATLAB 2006b.Old functionality (Pre-2006b): >> date=[730852 730853; 730854 730855]date =     730852    730853    730854    730855>> busdate(date)ans =      730853     730854      730855     730856Functionality change (2006b):>> date=[730852 730853; 730854 730855]date =     730852    730853    730854    730855>> busdate(date)ans =      730853      730855      730854      730856
Workaround: To preserve input/output dimensionality, a workaround would be to reshape the output of BUSDATE.>>dates = [731037 731036; 731030 731031];dates =      731037      731036      731030      731031>>mySize = size(dates);>>bDates = busdate(dates);>>bDates = reshape(bDates, mySize)bDates =      731038      731037      731031      731034Alternatively, the attached version of BUSDATE.m preserves input/output dimensionality. This bug fix will appear in the Financial Toolbox for 2007a.

Summary: Returning an array of discontiguous values from a function-call subsystem can cause a simulation error
Description: Simulating a model that connects a Stateflow chart to a function-call subsystem can cause an invalid function-call connection error under the following circumstances:" The function-call system outputs an array of values some or all of which originate from different nonvirtual blocks. The chart uses the output of the function-call system to compute one or more of its outputs.  Setting the Invalid function-call connection  diagnostic to none  or warning  may cause a one-step delay in returning the function-call output to the Stateflow Chart.See the attached model for an example that illustrates this problem.
Workaround: If a chart uses an array output from a function-call subsystem that it invokes to compute its own output, ensure that the function-call subsytem output originates from a single nonvirtual block in the subsystem.

Summary: Resolving a library link whose? Block Properties dialog box is open can cause an "invalid handle object" error
Description: Opening the Block Properties  dialog box of a library link and then triggering a link resolution operation can cause an "invalid handle object" error. Actions that can trigger resolution of a library link include updating a diagram and querying for LinkStatus.

Summary: No audio playback when using audioplayer on non-Windows platforms
Description: When using audioplayer  to play audio clips that are less than one second long on a non-windows system nothing happens. For example:load handel;ap = audioplayer(y(1:1000),Fs);play(ap);
Workaround: This is caused by the underlying audio implementation that is used by MATLAB. To workaround this issue, always play at least one second of data.  If your sound clip is less than one second, you can pad the clip with zeros to make it one second or longer.

Summary: MAT-file logging may produce incorrect answers when incorrect hardware word sizes are specified
Description: MAT-file logging may produce incorrect answers when the Embedded hardware  word sizes specified on the Hardware Implementation  pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box differ from the actual word sizes for the platform that is running the Real-Time Workshop generated executable.
Workaround: When using MAT-file logging, make sure that the Embedded hardware word sizes on the Hardware Implementation pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box correspond to the word sizes of the target platform.

Summary: whos displays incorrect size for objects stored in MAT-files
Description: The command whos -file  returns an inaccurate size value for objects stored in a MAT-file. You need to load objects into the MATLAB workspace before attempting to determine their size.
Workaround: After an loading object into the workspace, use the whos function to display its size. The whos function then calls the appropriate overloaded size method to display the correct size.

Summary: DPSS function returns inaccurate results
Description: When you enter the following commands to display the 18th sequence  [e v] = dpss(61,12);e(:,18) the first half of the sequence is all zeros. This is incorrect because all sequences should be even or odd around the center.
Workaround: Use the alternative interpolation method [e,v] = dpss(n,nw,'int')where the int string is either 'spline' or 'linear'. See the dpss reference page in the Signal Processing Toolbox documentation for more information.

Summary: Edits to Directory field of HDL Coder Target options are sometimes lost
Description: Edits to the Directory  field of HDL Coder Target options pane in the Model Explorer (or Configuration Parameters dialog box) may be lost in the following case: If you edit the Directory  field without applying the change, and then select one of the Code generation output  radio buttons, the text in the  Directory  field may revert back to its previous setting.
Workaround: Always click the Apply button immediately after editing text in the Directory field. Do this before changing the value of any other field in the dialog box.

Summary: Flaw in default uniform pseudorandom number generator algorithm
Description: The default uniform pseudorandom number generator algorithm used by the rand  function prior to MATLAB V7.4 (the "Subtract-with-Borrow" algorithm) contains a design flaw that leads to small correlations among triples of values that are separated by 15 and 27 steps in the random number generator sequence.  One consequence is that values that are small tend to be followed 27 steps later by values that are larger on average than should be true for independent uniform values.These correlations can create a small bias in some types of Monte-Carlo simulations.
Workaround: For versions of MATLAB prior to V7.4, run the following command at startup (or put it into your startup script): rand('twister',5489); % 5489, or another integer valueThis reinitializes rand to use the Mersenne Twister algorithm instead of the default (prior to V7.4) Subtract-with-Borrow algorithm.  Keep in mind that statements in your code such asrand('state',0);or s = rand('state');...rand('state',s);will cause rand to switch back to using the Subtract-with-Borrow algorithm.

Summary: fortran function errors without the Extended Math Symbolic Toolbox
Description: The fortran  function in the Symbolic Math Toolbox errors unless the user also has a license for the Extended Symbolic Math Toolbox.
Workaround: Instead of running fortran(z), you can run:  maple('with(codegen):'); maple(['fortran(' char(z) ');'])

Summary: Stateflow and Simulink might perform relational operations differently?for?particular fixed-point or integer data
Description: When performing relational operations on inputs of different fixed-point or integer data types, Stateflow might compute results that differ from those Simulink computes. This is more likely to occur on 16-bit targets for which the target's int  type cannot represent the combined range of the relational operator's inputs. Example 1:  Suppose your Simulink model performs the same relational operation using two different techniques, i.e., a Relational Operator block and a Stateflow Chart. In particular, each technique evaluates the expression "U0 >= U1" with the following operands:                         ?             U0              U1                               Value:             1              -1                               Data type:             uint16              int16                 Further, the Hardware Implementation  pane of your model's Configuration Parameters dialog box specifies its int  and long  parameters as 16 and 32, respectively. Stateflow performs the relational operation using C semantics; it converts -1  to uint16  and discards the negative half of the range. However, Simulink's Relational Operator block upcasts its operands to int32  before operating on them. Consequently, Stateflow might compute a result that differs from its Simulink counterpart. Example 2:  Suppose your Simulink model contains three Stateflow Charts, each of which evaluates the expression "U0 >= U1" using the following operands:                         ?             U0              U1                               ?             Value             Data type             Value             Data type                              Chart 1:             2              ufix16_En1              -2              sfix16_En1                               Chart 2:             2              uint16              -2              int16                               Chart 3:             2              ufix16_E1              -2              sfix16_E1                 In Chart 2, Stateflow performs the relational operation on pure integer data types using C semantics. However, in Charts 1 and 3, Stateflow uses Simulink Fixed Point's semantics when executing relational operations. Consequently, Stateflow might compute results that sometimes appear internally inconsistent.
Workaround: Convert values to the same type before performing comparisons, as follows:    Determine the type, based on the range and precision of interest.    Use a Data Type Conversion block to convert inputs to this type.    Configure Relational Operator blocks to enforce same type on inputs.    Use Model Advisor to cross-check proper configuration.

Summary: Wavelet information unreadable in Japanese environment
Description: Wavelet information is garbled and unreadable in the Japanese environment.
Workaround: Overwrite  matlabroot\toolbox\wavelet\wavelet\wreadinf.m with the attached wreadinf.m file.

Summary: The doc function might not work correctly if used is used after viewing other documentation in the Help?browser
Description: If you run the doc  function after viewing a page in the Help browser, the?reference page might not be displayed. Instead, the?previously viewed page remains.?This is especially likely to occur if you view a page from a search result prior to running the doc  function.
Workaround: Access the reference page using the Help browser Contents entry for the product, and its Functions - Alphabetical List.

Summary: Signal & Scope Manager viewers connected to shadow inports cause simulation to crash
Description: Simulating a model with any Signal & Scope Manager viewer connected to any shadow inport crashes Simulink.
Workaround: Connect the Signal & Scope Manager viewer to the master Inport block from which the shadowed Inport block was created.

Summary: makecform does not use consistent whitepoint value for type 'lab2srgb' or 'srgb2lab'
Description: When you call makecform  with a transform type that is 'lab2srgb'  or 'srgb2lab' , and you specify a whitepoint value other than the default, the resulting CFORM is not correct.
Workaround: To workaround this issue, replace the version of the makecform function in your current installation with the updated version contained in the attached file.Exit MATLAB.Make a backup copy of the following file. MATLABROOT\toolbox\images\images\makecform.mMATLABROOT represents the name of your MATLAB installation directory, as returned by the matlabroot function. For example, rename the existing file, adding "old" to the file extension, makecform.m.OLD.Download the attached file, extract makecform.m, storing it the directory specified in the previous step.Restart MATLAB.Run the rehash toolbox command at the MATLAB prompt.

Summary: SW2DTOOL level setting sliders do not display
Description: If you maximize the Stationary Wavelet Transform Denoising 2-D Tool (sw2dtool ) and want to change the number of levels via the sliders on the right hand side, you cannot use those sliders because they are hidden under the De-noise and Residuals buttons.
Workaround: To work around this issue, change the number of levels before maximizing the window. If you need to change the number of levels at a later stage, scale down the window, change the number of levels and then, re-maximize the window.

Summary: Setting the VerticalAlignment property of a textbox annotation to 'Bottom' moves the text outside the box
Description: After creating an annotation textbox  object, the text occupies two different locations when the VerticalAlignment  property is set to 'Bottom' :x = annotation(gcf,'textbox','Position',[0.2 0.3 0.5 0.4],'String','hello');set(x,'VerticalAlignment','Top')set(x,'VerticalAlignment','Bottom')pause;set(x,'VerticalAlignment','Middle')set(x,'VerticalAlignment','Bottom') During the pause, the text actually appears above the box. After the pause, the text moves to the middle, as expected, then moves up slightly when set to 'Bottom' .In addition, when you drag the annotation while the "VerticalAlignment" is set to 'Bottom' , the box moves, but the text remains stationary in the figure.

Summary: Pasting values into normalizer tables does not insert all of the new values
Description: When pasting values into a normalizer table in the normalizer view in CAGE, you may see that only some of the values you paste are changed in the table.  Repeatedly pasting the new values iteratively fills in more of the cells.  This happens because CAGE attempts to apply the pasted values to each table cell one at a time, and at each attempt applies the rule that the normalizer must remain monotonically increasing.  Although the full set of values that you are pasting may meet this criteria, the intermediate sets that have part of the data do not. For example, consider attempting to paste the breakpoints [100, 200, 300, 400] into a normalizer that has breakpoints currently set to [1, 2, 3, 4].  The normalizer breakpoints are altered as follows when the first paste is performed:    Start     Cell 1 paste     Cell 2 paste     Cell 3 paste     Cell 4 paste     End       1 100  1 1 1 1      2 2 200  2 2 2      3 3 3 300  3 3      4 4 4 4 400  400           Rejected      Rejected      Rejected      Accepted          When the paste is repeated, the normalizer breakpoints are altered as follows:    Start     Cell 1 paste     Cell 2 paste     Cell 3 paste     Cell 4 paste     End       1 100  1 1 1 1      2 2 200  2 2 2      3 3 3 300  300 300      400 400 400 400 400  400           Rejected      Rejected      Accepted      Accepted          And so on for further paste attempts, until all of the breakpoints have been successfully set.
Workaround: The following methods of entering numbers into normalizers can be used to work around this pasting problem: Initialize all of the normalizer values to a number that is higher than any of the breakpoints you are pasting (for example, Inf).  This will cause all of the breakpoints to be accepted on the first paste.In the Calibration Manager you can import numbers either from the clipboard or from a calibration file (this includes simple formats such as CSV files).  You can also import from a calibration source by selecting the File->Import->Calibration menu item in CAGE.

Summary: Model using Model blocks may fail to compile RTW generated code
Description: Code generated for an ERT-based Simulink model with Model Reference blocks can fail to compile when both of the following conditions apply:     The IncludeERTFirstTime  configuration parameter is set to off      The Suppress error status in real-time model data structure  configuration option is selected on the Interface  pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box  Some models may generate uncompilable generated code.  The code contains a call to mr_Test001_initialize  with a comma before the first argument, as shown in the following example: /* Model initialize function */void Test002_initialize(void){  /* Model Initialize fcn for ModelReference Block: '/Model' */  mr_Test001_initialize(, 0);}
Workaround: To work around this problem in R2006b:    Backup the file:    MATLABROOT\rtw\c\tlc\mw\modelrefsfcnlib.tlc    to:    MATLABROOT\rtw\c\tlc\mw\modelrefsfcnlib.bak        Copy the attached file to:    MATLABROOT\rtw\c\tlc\mw\modelrefsfcnlib.tlc

Summary: Subsystem checksum can fail to return a value for certain subsystems
Description: Obtaining the second return argument of Simulink.SubSystem.getChecksum  (i.e., details ) will fail in some subsystems when running Simulink Version 6.5 (Release R2006b).?The first return argument (the checksum) is not affected by this bug and will be returned properly.?The error message associated with this bug is:?There are no 'ContentsChecksum' details available for 'fullPathToYourSubsystem '

Summary: Publishing to PowerPoint has empty Overview slide
Description: When publishing to PowerPoint using the cell publishing features in the Editor/Debugger or with the publish  function, the Overview slide is blank, but should list the contents of the presentation.
Workaround: To work around this problemNavigate to matlabroot/toolbox/matlab/codetools/private, where matlabroot is the directory in which MATLAB is installed.Make a copy the file mxdom2ppt.m and rename it mxdom2ppt_orig.m.Edit mxdom2ppt.m, deleting line 434breakin this section of code:for j = 1:cellNodeList.getLength   cellNode = cellNodeList.item(j-1);   child = cellNode.getFirstChild;   while ~isempty(child)      if isequal('steptitle',char(child.getNodeName))         title = char(child.getFirstChild.getData);         textRange = textRange.InsertAfter('');         textRange.ParagraphFormat.Bullet.Type = 'ppBulletUnnumbered';         textRange.Text = title;         textRange = newLine(textRange);      end      child = child.getNextSibling;      break   endendSave the file.

Summary: Function Call Tutorial and Function Call Examples demos error out when MATLAB is installed in a directory containing spaces in the directory name
Description: Two demos--Function Call Examples (ccsfunctioncalldemo ) and Function Call Tutorial (functioncalltutorial )--error out when you install MATLAB in a directory whose path contains folder names with spaces.The demos create a library file in the directory [MATLABROOT]\work\LinkForCCSDemos_v[ver] . In the Build Option Archiver  tab in CCS, Link for CCS enters the library file name and path in the Output Filename  field.When the MATLABROOT  path entry in the directory path [MATLABROOT]\work\LinkForCCSDemos_v[ver]\[library_filename]  has spaces in it, CCS changes the [MATLABROOT] string in the Output Filename  field. Because CCS modified the path, the build process which uses the path in the Output Filename  field fails because CCS cannot find the correct path.

Summary: Equivalent model is not always created, under the Powergui block, in R2006b
Description: This problem typically arises when you have one or more circuits without any measurement blocks. The equivalent circuit is not created for these, under the mask of the Powergui block. An error message is issued in some cases. In a model with two identical circuits, the bug can also sometimes manifest itself with zero output for the first, and correct output for the second.  In any SimPowerSystems model, there must be as many equivalent circuits, seen under the mask of the Powergui block, as independent circuits.
Workaround: Locate this file and replace it with the attached file:$matlabroot/toolbox/physmod/powersys/powersys/private/powersolve.p

Summary: Cannot create a standalone application with C/C++ main using DEPLOYTOOL GUI
Description: I am not able to add a C or C++ function containing a main  function with deploytool . I also cannot compile this code into a standalone application.
Workaround: The deploytool GUI currently does not support creating standalone targets where the main gateway function is defined in external C/C++ code instead of within an M-function.As a workaround, you can create a shared library using deploytool and then use mbuild to create the standalone application. Do this by issuing the command as follows: On Windows:mbuild c_driver_code.c path_to_lib_stub/libMyLib.lib On UNIX, Linux, and MAC:mbuild c_driver_code.c -Lpath_to_lib -lMyLibThe above code assumes that the name of the shared library that you created with the GUI is libMyLib. path_to_lib is the directory where the library is located on the file system. In the GUI, you can find this information from the General tab of the Settings window.

Summary: java.lang.ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException
Description: Sometimes java.lang.ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException  appears in the Command Window after a directory change followed by double clicking to open a file in the Current Directory browser.
Workaround: Ignore this message. Run clc from the Command Window to clear the display.

Summary: Doc block: non-English comments generated in C code for Doc blocks can be garbled
Description: Real-Time Workshop Embedded Coder can include garbled text in non-Enghlish comments it generates in C code for the Doc block. This bug results from MATLAB changing from native character encoding to UTF16 encoding.
Workaround: Determine your MATLAB root directory by entering the matlabroot command at the MATLAB command prompt.Quit MATLAB.Rename matlabroot\rtw\targets\mpt\private\ec_mp_global_comments.m  to matlabroot\rtw\targets\mpt\private\ec_mp_global_comments.m.old.Rename matlabroot\simulink\blocks\docblock.m to matlabroot\simulink\blocks\docblock.m.old. Rename matlabroot\simulink\simulink\slsaveassup.m to matlabroot\simulink\simulink\slsaveassup.m.old.Download the attached files and place them in the appropriate directories.Place ec_mp_global_comments.m in matlabroot\rtw\targets\mpt\private.Place docblock.m in matlabroot\simulink\blocks.Place slsaveassup in matlabroot\simulink\simulink.Restart MATLAB.

Summary: Command Window: cannot scroll or use tab completion when xPC Target 3.1 Explorer window is open
Description: In xPC Target, when you open the xPC Explorer and select a Target PC object in the xPC Target Hierarchy window, you cannot scroll in the MATLAB Command Window. Instead, the cursor moves to the last entry. In addition, tab completion options disappear just after they display.
Workaround: Select a different node in the xPC Target Hierarchy window before scrolling the MATLAB Command Window.

Summary: Electrical frequency is incorrectly calculated when using synchronous machine speed input
Description: The rotor speed wm  in the measurement output of the Synchronous Machine block is incorrectly calculated when using the machine with the Mechanical load  parameter set to speed w .

Summary: Model reference simulation target may fail to compile
Description: If a referenced model uses a large number of workspace parameters, the simulation target may fail to compile when using the Microsoft Visual C compiler.  The error returned by the compiler is: error C2026: string too big, trailing characters truncated

Summary: The textscan?function cannot read negative numbers packed without delimiters
Description: If you have an ASCII data file that is densely packed (i.e., it has?no spaces or other delimiting characters) and you attempt to parse one or more negative numbers in that file using the textscan  function, textscan  terminates the parse prematurely, stopping at the last item before the negative number.For example, if?a dense file data.txt  contains the sequential numbers 2, 4, and -8, and you attempt to parse these numbers?in 2-character fields as shown below, instead of getting the expected string "0204-8", you receive only the data up to the last item before the negative number.???? fid = fopen('data.txt'); ??? data = textscan(fid,'%2d%2d%2d'); ??? fclose(fid);?
Workaround: There is no complete workaround. You may be able to read in the data as strings (that is, using the %s format of textscan) and then convert that data to numbers.

Summary: Instrument Driver Editor and makemid function are unable to parse the Aeroflex ifr341x driver
Description: Instrument Control Toolbox is unable to parse the Aeroflex V02.30 ifr341x driver.After installing the appropriate drivers, the Instrument Driver Editor (midedit graphical tool) produces a File Format Error dialog stating "An error occurred while reading the file."   Similarly, makemid('ifr341x')  produces the following error while importing an Aeroflex ifr341x driver:>> makemid('ifr341x') ??? Error using ==> makemidThe driver function panel could not be read. As a result, no MATLAB instrument driver is generated.
Workaround: If you are using a version of Instrument Control Toolbox that cannot make this MATLAB Instrument Driver, you can use the attached file, ifr341x.mdd.

Summary: Get Error "MCC: The maximum continuous-mode sample rate has been exceeded" on Measurement Computing devices at lower sample rates than expected.
