Description: If you attempt to acquire a large number of points on 8 channels at rates above 6250 samples/sec on the Measurement Computing USB-1616FS, you get an error saying that the max continuous sample rate has been exceeded. This is incorrect. You should be able to achieve 17000 samples/sec on eight channels with this device. There are simlar limitations on the USB-1608FS/PMD-1608FS.For example:ai = analoginput('mcc',0)  % modify for your device IDaddchannel(ai,0:7);ai.SamplesPerTrigger = 100000;ai.SampleRate = 7000;start(ai);??? MCC: The maximum continuous-mode sample rate has been exceeded.The total points acquired cannot be greater than FIFO size. See device specifications for more information.
Workaround: You can work around this issue by changing the configuration file for your USB-1616FS or USB-1608FS/PMD-1608FS.To change the configuration of the USB-1616FS:Quit MATLAB.Change to the directory $MATLAB/toolbox/daq/daq/private, where $MATLAB is the directory where you installed MATLAB.Back up the file mwmcc.ini to another name such as mwmcc.ini.bak.Open mwmcc.ini and find the text [USB-1616FS].In that section, find the line AIMaxContSR = 50000, and change it to AIMaxContSR = 200000.Find the text [USB-1608FS].In that section, find the line AIMaxContSR = 50000, and change it to AIMaxContSR = 100000.Find the text [PMD-1608FS].In that section, find the line AIMaxContSR = 50000, and change it to AIMaxContSR = 100000.Save and close the file.Restart MATLAB.

Summary: MAT-file names cannot begin with a number
Description: None of the Wavelet Tool GUIs save any signals as MAT files if the filename you specify begins with a number. No error message or warning is produced to indicate that there was a problem with the Save operation.  The Signal Extension Tool, in particular, displays a 'Saving' message that never goes away.
Workaround: To work around this issue, save MAT files with filenames that do not begin with a number.

Summary: Simulation sometimes crashes or hangs when data logging is turned on
Description: Simulink sometimes crashes or hangs during a simulation with an error similar to the following: "Caught unexpected exception of unknown type"  The following conditions need to be true to see this error: Right-click data logging is enabled for a signal. The signal is in a conditionally executed subsystem. Limit Data Points  is turned on and set to a number between 1 - 9.
Workaround: To work around this issue, turn off Limit Data Points or save more than 10 data points.

Summary: MATLAB Compiler causes compile-time warnings on Win64.
Description: When using MCC to compile a function on Windows 64-bit version, warnings about conversion from size_t  to int  are displayed when Visual C/C++ compiles the generated C/C++ code. For example, compiling the function myfun.m : myfun.m function myfun    disp('hello') >> mcc -mv myfun  produces these results: myfun_main.c myfun_main.c(25) : warning C4267: 'return' : conversion from 'size_t' to 'int', possible loss of data myfun_main.c(31) : warning C4267: '=' : conversion from 'size_t' to 'int', possible loss of data myfun_main.c(32) : warning C4267: '=' : conversion from 'size_t' to 'int', possible loss of data myfun_main.c(34) : warning C4267: '+=' : conversion from 'size_t' to 'int', possible loss of data

Summary: Current Measurement block in series with Mutual Inductance block produces Index exceeds matrix dimensions error
Description: If you have a model that contains a Current Measurement block that is connected in series with a Mutual Inductance block with the Type of mutual inductance  set to Generalized Mutual Inductance , you might get the following error message: Index exceeds matrix dimensions.
Workaround: To work around this issue, download the attached version of the MutualInductanceBlock.p file. Here are the steps:   Quit MATLAB.  Rename the following file: $MATLAB\toolbox\physmod\powersys\powersys\private\MutualInductanceBlock.p  (where $MATLAB is the MATLAB root directory on your machine, as returned by typing  matlabroot  at the MATLAB Command Prompt.)  Rename the file to MutualInductanceBlock.p.old  Download the attached file. Place the new file in the same directory mentioned in step 2.  Restart MATLAB.

Summary: If a model is already in memory Simulink will not error if you try and load a model of the same name from a different directory
Description: It's impossible to open/load/simulate 2 models simultaneously with exactly the same names, but different absolute path.Affected commands are open_system/load_system/sim correspondingly.Previously simulink, in attempt to open/load/simulate model with the name that is equal to one of the already loaded model, but withdifferent absolute path, just opens already loaded model (not the one thatuser requested).we changed that behavior: simulink issues error SL_LoadModelFullNameConflictin the such attempt.

Summary: Optimizations of muliplication by 0.0 and division of 0.0 prevent NaNs
Description: When a floating point value is multiplied by 0.0, there are special cases where the resulting product is not always zero.  When 0.0 is multiplied by NaN, Inf, or -Inf, the product should be NaN.  Similarly, for the division operation 0.0/denominator, the quotient should be NaN and not 0.0, when the denominator is 0.0 or NaN.An optimization was introduced in R14FCS that replaced some floating point multiplications by 0.0 with a constant of value 0.0.  A similar operation replaced some divisions of 0.0 with the constant value 0.0.  Both of these optimized results are incorrect when the result should be NaN.  These optimizations could affect both single- and double-precision floating-point multiplications and divisions.

Summary: Enabled Gate processes gate-closing events incorrectly
Description: When the Enabled Gate block processes a gate-closing event for which you have specified an event priority, the block behaves as if you had not specified an event priority. As a result, you might see a sequence of simultaneous events that does not agree with the priorities you have specified.This problem does not affect gate-opening events in the same block.
Workaround: Use a Signal Latch block to process relevant events. Instead of connecting the enable signal directly to the en signal input port of the Enabled Gate block, connect the enable signal to the wtr and in signal input ports of a Signal Latch block. Then connect the out signal output port of the Signal Latch block to the en signal input port of the Enabled Gate block.Configure the Enabled Gate block as follows: Clear the Specify event priority for gate opening and closing check box.Configure the Signal Latch block as follows:Set Initial memory value to 0 or 1, depending on whether you want the gate to be closed or open at the beginning of the simulation.Set Write to memory upon to Trigger from port wtr. Set Trigger type to Either.Select Specify event priority for writing to memory.Set Event priority for writing to memory to your desired priority for the gate-closing event.Set Read from memory upon to Write to memory event.Select Report memory value upon read event, out.

Summary: Abs block: returns wrong answers for code generation when specified with large or very small input values
Description: When you specify large or very small input values with the Abs block, Real-Time Workshop generates code that returns wrong answers. However, simulation results are correct.

Summary: Dialog box appears during xlsread
Description: When reading an Excel spreadsheet with the xlsread  function, a dialog box may appear stating, "workbook contains links to other data sources". The dialog is similar to the one that Excel itself displays. This unexpected interruption can be a problem if you are using xlsread  in a program. This dialog is generated when both of the following are true:The file you are reading with xlsread  contains links Excel is set up on your system to ask whether links should be updated
Workaround: Open Microsoft Excel, and select Options from the Tools menu. At the top of the Options window, click the Edit tab.  Make sure that the "Ask to update automatic links" checkbox is unchecked.

Summary: AWGN Channel Block may cause an error when its symbol period is equal to sample time of input
Description: When the AWGN channel block is used in a model (e.g. "modelname") and the symbol period parameter value is set equal to the sample time of the data coming into the block, the AWGN block produces the following error for certain values of symbol period.Error reported by s-function 'scomawgnchan2' in 'modelname/AWGN Channel/Dynamic AWGN'.The symbol period parameter value should exceed the inherited sample time of the block. This happens only when the sample time (of data) and symbol period (of AWGN Channel Block) are set to be equal and are represented by infinite (recurring) decimals such as 1/12. In this case, the actual (machine-precision) values of the two parameters may not exactly be equal and thus an error is received. However, when the two parameters are set to equal a finite decimal such as 1/10, this error does not occur.

Summary: MATLAB might crash while processing pending events
Description: MATLAB might crash with a segmentation violation in libuij.dll  while processing pending events, which can happen during figure creation, when executing a drawnow  command, or while loading a Simulink system. When this happens, a stack trace is generated with the top frame of the stack reading:libuij.dll:_UIJ_AreThereWindowShowsPending

Summary: National Instruments USB-9215A not supported.
Description: When I attempt to use analoginput  with my National Instruments USB-9215A, I get an error, even though the device is listed as supported:AI = analoginput('nidaq','Dev1');??? Error using ==> analoginput.analoginputError using ==>analoginput.analoginput>localCreateAnalogInputObjectThis device is not recognized by the toolbox. However, many times,support for new hardware can be added. Visit The Mathworks web site andsearch on the name of your device for more information.
Workaround: To work around this issue, perform these steps to add support for this device:Quit MATLAB. Find the following file: $MATLAB\toolbox\daq\daq\private\mwnidaqmx.iniwhere $MATLAB is the MATLAB root installation directory obtained by issuing:matlabrootat the MATLAB Command Prompt.Make a copy of this file and rename it to mwnidaqmx.ini.old. Open the file mwnidaqmx.ini in a text editor, and add the following lines at the end of the file: [USB-9215A]hasAnalogInput   = truenumAIChannels    = 4hasAnalogOutput  = falsehasDigitalIO     = false[USB-9215A (BNC)]hasAnalogInput   = truenumAIChannels    = 4hasAnalogOutput  = falsehasDigitalIO     = falseSave the file mwnidaqmx.ini and restart MATLAB.

Summary: Macintosh: Intel-based R2006b beta might display DATEVEC warning at startup
Description: The beta release of MATLAB R2006b for Intel-based Macintosh platforms might display the following warning at startup:Warning: Usage of DATEVEC with empty date strings is not supported. Results may change in future versions. > In datevec at 95   In datenum at 89   In reporterrorlogs>getLogFiles at 212   In reporterrorlogs>getFilesToSend at 142   In reporterrorlogs at 26   In matlabrc at 241  You can ignore this warning; it is harmless and will be fixed for the final release.

Summary: Gain block returns incorrect error message regarding support of boolean signals
Description: Suppose your Simulink model contains a Gain block whose input is a boolean signal. If you use inheritance rules instead of specifying explicitly the data types associated with blocks in your model, Simulink might return an error message that begins as follows: Gain does not accept 'boolean' signals. This error message is inaccurate because Gain blocks support boolean input signals.
Workaround: To work around this issue, ensure that you specify explicitly the data types associated with blocks that supply boolean signals to Gain blocks. For example, if a Gain block in your model receives a boolean signal from a Subsystem block, use one of the following workarounds:    Specify boolean as the output data type for the Outport block in the subsystem.    Use a Signal Specification block to specify boolean as the data type of the signal that the Gain block receives.    Use a Simulink.Signal object to specify boolean as the data type of the signal that the Gain block receives.

Summary: Multiport Switch block generates incorrect HDL code when index input has boolean data type
Description: The HDL code generated for the Multiport Switch block is incorrect if a boolean signal is connected to the control (index) input of the block.
Workaround: To avoid this problem, use an index that is not boolean (for example, sfix(2), uint8, int8).

Summary: SimMechanics Visualization Menu Items Issue False Warnings when (Un)selected
Description: If, in the SimMechanics menu of the SimMechanics visualization window, you select or deselect any menu item which can be enabled or disabled, the following warning appears at the MATLAB command line:Warning: UMTOGGLE function is obsolete.  This warning is not genuine.
Workaround: There is no workaround, but the warning has no implications for any functionality.

Summary: CCSDSP command fails with error message
Description: When you issue the commandcc=ccsdsp you get an error message that Link for CCS could not start a MATLAB component:??? Error using ==> ccs.ccsdsp.initializeCcsdsp>DetectConfigErrorsCould not start the MATLAB component in CCS.Please use Link for CCS FAQ to troubleshoot the problem.
Workaround: Link for CCS uses a plugin component that needs to be registered with CCS. If the plugin is not registered, you get the following            error messages when you invoke CCSDSP:                  Example 1:   cc = ccsdsp   Could not start the MATLAB component in CCS.   Please use Link For CCS FAQ to troubleshoot the problem.Example 2:   cc = ccsdsp   Could not start the MATLAB component in CCS.         To fix this problem, perform the following steps:         1. Register the Link for CCS components         2. Enable the Link for CCS component         To register the Link for CCS components         The following instructions show you how to register the components required by Link for CCS.                                    Note: To register the components, you must have write permission to modify the registry. If you do not have this permission,                  look for someone who has (e.g. system administrator) and have this person perform the registration.                                             1. Close all instances of CCS.         2. Close MATLAB.         3. Open a Microsoft Windows Command Prompt by clicking Start and then Programs > Accessories > Command Prompt.                  4. In the Command Prompt, enter the following commands to register the LinkCCS.dll and MWCCSStu.ocx components. a. regsvr32 [MATLABROOT]\bin\win32\LinkCCS.dll where [MATLABROOT] represents the root directory of your MATLAB installation. Entering the above command opens a dialog box. Verify that the following message appears.    DllRegisterServer in [MATLABROOT]\bin\win32\LinkCCS.dll succeeded b. regsvr32 [MATLABROOT]\toolbox\ccslink\bin\win32\MWCCSStu.ocxEntering the above command opens a dialog box. Verify that the following message appears.    DllRegisterServer in [MATLABROOT]\toolbox\ccslink\bin\win32\MWCCSStu.ocx succeededAfter you register the components, you must enable the CCS component before you can use Link For CCS.         To enable the Link For CCS component         1. Open MATLAB         2. Enter cc = ccsdsp at the MATLAB prompt.                  CCS starts and prompts that "New components were detected".                  3. Click Yes to enable components for all compatible CCS releases.

Summary: Specifying a very high frequency in the PI section line block could lead to unstable simulations
Description: If you try to run a model containing a PI section line block with a Frequency used for RLC calculations  that is very high (for example, 50e6), combined with a line length that is relatively short (for example, 0.5 Km), you could see an error message similar to the following one: Derivative input 1 of 'model1/powergui/EquivalentModel1/State-Space' at time 5.301833251281511e-012 is Inf or NaN.  Stopping simulation.  There may be a singularity in the solution.  If not, try reducing the step size (either by reducing the fixed step size or by tightening the error tolerances).
Workaround: Since it is very difficult to predict what are the line parameters that lead to unstable simulation, you shoud review the parameters you specified in the PI section line block. To help you in this process, you can use the power_lineparam graphical user interface for computing RLC line parameters. At the MATLAB prompt, type: power_lineparam

Summary: Segmentation violation when calling a method in a custom built COM server using invoke
Description: Using a COM server built in Visual C++ 6.0 with a GetDispatch  method defined for the default interface, you can create the COM object from MATLAB. However, when you call the GetDispatch  method as shown below, you get a segmentation violation.test=actxserver('MyTestCOMServer.Application')invoke(test,'GetDispatch')
Workaround: There is no workaround.

Summary: Generated MATLAB code from dfittool can be incorrect
Description: Under some conditions, the MATLAB code generated by the dfittool  function may fail. These conditions include: Use of a frequency vector with a distribution (e.g., the beta distribution) that does not accept a frequency vector in its fitting function (e.g., betafit ) Use of the generalized Pareto distribution, which has a threshold parameter that is not estimated and must be specified Use of the binomial distribution, which has an N  parameter that is not estimated and must be specified
Workaround: For a problem using the beta distribution with a frequency vector, it is possible to modify the generated code to use the mle function instead of the betafit function to perform the fit. The mle function accepts a frequency vector. Here is an example:p_ = mle(Data_, 'dist','beta', 'alpha',0.05, 'cens',[], 'freq',Freq_);  % Fit Beta distribution For other issues, the required change in the generated code depends on the specific problem. For example, it may be necessary to enter the generalized Pareto threshold parameter, or the binomial N parameter, directly into the code.

Summary: Errors in the YLim property when running the FFT Analysis tool of Powergui
Description: If you try to use the FFT Analysis tool on a simulation signal that is (1) not sampled at fixed step and (2) originates from a Scope block with its Limit data points to last  option selected, there is a risk that you get the following error message when you click Display : Error using ==> set Bad value for axes property: 'YLim' Values must be increasing and non-NaN.
Workaround: To work around this issue, unselect the Limit data points to last option of the Scope block from where the simulation signal originates, run a new simulation, then reopen the FFT Analysis tool to get a valid signal to analyze.

Summary: Model reference not supported
Description: The Embedded Target does not support referenced models.
Workaround: If your application requires the ability to reference models, you may wish to use a version of the Embedded Target released prior to R2006b.

Summary: RTF and Word output formats do not include images
Description: If you import images via the Title Page, Figure Snapshot, or Simulink Snapshot component, these images may not be included in reports in RTF or Word format.
Workaround: Download the attached file db31.dsl. Here are the steps:Quit MATLAB.Rename the following file:$MATLAB\sys\jade\docbook\print\db31.dsl(where $MATLAB is the MATLAB root directory on your machine, as returned by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB Command Prompt).Rename the file to db31.dsl.old.Download the attached file. Place the new file in the same directory mentioned in step 2. Restart MATLAB.

Summary: Incorrect simulation results when the output time vector from one simulation is passed as an input to another run
Description: When the output time vector from one simulation is passed as an input to another run, the second simulation may produce incorrect results due to incorrect handling of zero crossings. For an example, try this: >> open_system('sh_circuit_sa_cylinder'); >> t = sim('sh_circuit_sa_cylinder'); >> sim('sh_circuit_sa_cylinder',t);   The second simulation run produces incorrect outputs on the scopes.
Workaround: The problem can sometimes be avoided by restricting the minimum step size on the first run and parsing the output time vector before passing it to the subsequent simulations: >> open_system('sh_circuit_sa_cylinder'); >> hmin = 1e-13 * str2num(get_param(gcs,'StopTime')); >> t = sim(gcs,[],simset('MinStep',hmin)); >> t ( diff(t) < hmin ) = [];  >> sim(gcs,t); This will help the solver in proper zero-crossing detection.

Summary: Why do some formats of my DCAM camera not work with the Image Acquisition Toolbox?
Description: I have a DCAM-compatible firewire camera. Several formats or modes of this camera work with Image Acquisition Toolbox, but the formats Y16_1024x768  and Y16_1600x1200  do not work. I am able to access the device with the videoinput  command, but the start , getsnapshot , and preview  commands all error out with the error "Device failed to start."

Summary: Using the Lookup Table Editor might cause MATLAB to hang
Description: Suppose you use the Lookup Table Editor to edit the breakpoints or table data associated with any of the blocks from Simulink's Lookup Tables library. Editing values in the Lookup Table Editor and its dialogs, and then closing the model while the Lookup Table Editor remains open might cause MATLAB to hang.
Workaround: To work around this issue, before closing a model, close the Lookup Table Editor and its dialogs in the reverse order in which you opened them.

Summary: Notebook's figures sometimes display incorrectly
Description: When MATLAB figures are rendered into a Word document via Notebook, they might display in a way that the axis is in top left corner rather than centered in the figure.
Workaround: To work around the problem, perform the following steps before running Notebook:In MATLAB, select File > Preferences > Figure Copy Template > Copy Options.Under Size, select the Match figure screen size check box, and click OK.Close all figure windows.Run Notebook as usual. The figures should now display correctly.

Summary: ltln2val errors on NaNs and points outside grid
Description: Function ltln2val  errors when the input latitude-longitude vectors contain NaN and/or specify locations outside the limits of the data grid.
Workaround: To work around this issue, replace the version of the ltln2val.m function in yourcurrent installation with the updated version in the attachment, as follows:Exit MATLAB.Make a backup copy of the following file. matlabroot/toolbox/map/map/ltln2val.m matlabroot represents the name of your MATLAB installation directory, as returned by the matlabroot function.For example, rename the existing file, adding "old" to the file extension, ltln2val.m.OLD.Copy the version of ltln2val.m from the attachment into the directory specified in the previous step.Restart MATLAB.Run the rehash toolbox command at the MATLAB prompt.

Summary: Hyperlinks in Figure Summary Table may point to wrong figures
Description: Under some circumstances, hyperlinks in a Figure Summary Table can point to the wrong figures. Example: A report file generates figures and creates a Figure Summary Table for them. It then closes the figures and then generates another set of figures and another Figure Summary Table for the new figures. The hyperlinks in the second Figure Summary Table point incorrectly to the first set of figures. The following code illustrates the way such a report is set up to be generated: %First set of figuresfor(i=1:1:10)h = figure(i);plot(h,x,y)end%Report Generator puts the figures into the report%Report Generator generates the first Figure Summary Tableclose all%Second set of figuresfor(i=1:1:10)h = figure(i);plot(h,x,y)end%Report Generator puts the figures into the report%Report Generator generates the second Figure Summary Table The links in the Figure Summary Table are based on the figure numbers. When you generate the second set of figures after closing the first set, the new figures also have the same figure numbers as the first set (figure numbers start from 1). This causes the misdirected links.
Workaround: To work around this issue, keep track of the last figure number generated in the first set of figures and then generate the secong set of figures such that their figure numbers start from where the first set ended.

Summary: SimStruct: GRT target references rtModel when specified to use Simstruct
Description: As of Real-Time Workshop Version 5.0 (R13), by default, GRT targets use the data structure rtModel  instead of SimStruct  to store information about the root model. You should be able to reverse this behavior by doing the following: Specifying GenRTModel = 0  in the system target file. Removing the symbol definition for USE_RTMODEL from the template makefile.  However, when GenRTModel  is set to 0 and you use the GRT target, references to rt_model  appear throughout the generated code.
Workaround: To work around this issue in R2006b release, download a new version of the file $MATLAB\rtw\c\tlc\mw\setuplib.tlc. $MATLAB is the MATLAB root directory identified in step 1.Identify your MATLAB root directory by entering the command matlabroot at the command prompt.Rename $MATLAB\rtw\c\tlc\mw\setuplib.tlc to $MATLAB\rtw\c\tlc\mw\setuplib.tlc.old.Download the attached file to $MATLAB\rtw\c\tlc\mw\setuplib.tlc.

Summary: Open callback does not execute on copied block
Description: Opening a block and then copying the block may disable the open callback of the block.
Workaround: Do not open block before copying the block.

Summary: Lines between cells of tables created with the text function using LaTeX commands are not visible
Description: When I create a ruled table with LaTeX commands,cs = '\begin{tabular}{ l | c | r | } \hline \multicolumn{3}{|c|}{Stuff} \\ A & B & C \\  1 & 2 & 3 \\ 4 & 5 & 6 \\  7 & 8 & 9 \\ \hline \end{tabular} '; figure; text(0.1,.5,cs,'Interpreter','latex')  the content of the table is correctly drawn, and the table is formatted correctly except that the hline  horizontal lines do not appear.
Workaround: This problem is under investigation. It appears to be a bug in how the LaTeX interpretor generates DVI instructions, rather than a rendering bug.No workarounds exist for this issue.

Summary: Creating an object causes a deep copy of input argument in MATLAB 7.0 (R14)
Description: MATLAB 7.0 (R14) introduced a memory bug in object creation. For example, in the following code:% inside class constructor myclass.mastruct.field = ones(10000);obj = class(astruct, 'myclass');  when obj  is created, it makes a deep copy of astruct , instead of using a shallow copy of astruct  which is more memory efficient.This bug does not cause your program to give the wrong answers, but it could slow your program's performance if you create a big object in your code.
Workaround: There is no workaround for this bug.

Summary: Signal & Scope Manager viewer scroll mode does not work in External Mode
Description: The Signal & Scope Manager viewer in scroll mode does not work properly when uploading signal data from a target in External Mode.  Specifically, the time offset of the viewer is incorrect, and the signal displayed by the viewer may contain some flat lines that are not part of the actual data.
Workaround: In prior releases, to use a Signal & Scope Manager viewer in External Mode without encountering this bug, open the Parameters dialog for the viewer and uncheck the Scroll box.

Summary: Command Window not showing output after running desktop function
Description: When you start MATLAB on UNIX with the nodesktop  startup option, or when you start it via an ActiveX interface on Windows, and then run the desktop  function, the desktop opens, but output does not appear in the desktop Command Window as expected. Output instead appears in the console window from which you ran the desktop  function.Note that on Windows, the nodesktop  startup option is not supported.

Summary: FIG-files with legends created by CFTool do not reload legend
Description: When reloaded into MATLAB, a figure file created from the Curve Fitting Tool using the Print To Figure  menu item fail to load the legend. The warning displayed isWarning: Unrecognized object type: scribe.legend.   In hgload at 151   In openfig at 80   In open at 141   In uiopen at 181
Workaround: Turn off the legend in the CFTool window or remove the uicontainer in the figure that Print To Figure creates before saving the figure:uic = get(gcf,'Children') set(get(gcf,'CurrentAxes'),'Parent',gcf) set(get(uic,'Children'),'Parent',gcf)

Summary: Setting axes DataAspectRatioMode property manual can cause problems when zooming
Description: If you create a plot that is wider than it is high and and set its DataAspectRatioMode  property to 'manual' , it will distort when you attempt to zoom in on it. The following code illustrates the issue:load clownimage(X)colormap(gray)set(gca,'DataAspectRatioMode','manual') If you now zoom in on the plot, the axes shrink, eventually reducing to a single line.

Summary: Checking for product updates using Check for Updates menu sometimes fails
Description: The Check for Updates  item in the Help  menu does not always work if you are using a proxy server to connect to the Internet.
Workaround: To workaround this problem, add these two lines to your matlabrc.m file or your startup.m file.import com.mathworks.mlwidgets.html.*;com.mathworks.mlwidgets.html.HTMLPrefs;

Summary: Current on the secondary side of an ideal linear transformer is 180 degrees out of phase
Description: There is a bug in the implementation of the Linear Transformer block, which results in the current on the secondary side of an ideal transformer to be 180 degrees out of phase. For example, you have a model of an AC voltage source driving an ideal linear transformer, which is driving an inductor. When you simulate this model, the current leads the voltage by 90 degrees instead of lagging the voltage by 90 degrees.
Workaround: To work around this issue, set the reactance of the primary and secondary side of the transformer to be a small non-zero number and the magnetization resistance to be a very large number. See the attached model for an example.

Summary: If block expressions support only single and double data types
Description: The documentation fails to state that if you use any data type other than double or single in the If block's conditional expression, Simulink halts the simulation and displays an error message. For example, the following expressions error out:  u1 < int8(2)    u1 >= int16(3) + x   where x  is a workspace variable whose data type is not single or double
Workaround: Use other Simulink blocks to evaluate the conditional expression and enter the result as one of the inputs to the If block.

Summary: License server runs out of file descriptors.
Description: When running the license server on a Unix system, serving clients running on either Linux or Windows systems, the license manager may stop serving license keys. This can happen when serving licenses to MATLAB Distributed Computing Engine (MDCE) clients (called worker nodes) running on Linux or Windows. When this happens, the license manager writes the following message to the debug log file:This license server system can handle no moreconcurrent clients since it is out of file descriptors.We recommend splitting your licenses among morelicense server systems and using a license-file listso FLEXenabled applications can checkout from more servers.Although, more license server systems will improve response.Please see www.macrovision.com or your vendorfor more information.
Workaround: This is a bug in FLEXnet 10.8.0.1.To workaround this problem, you can use any of the following options:Restart the license manager when this error happens.Move the license manager to a Windows system.Replace the lmutil, lmrgd, and lm_matlab files located in the MATLABROOT/etc/$ARCH directory with the updated versions attached. MATLABROOT represents your MATLAB installation directory, as returned by the MATLABROOT command.If you choose to download and untar new versions of these files, choose the tar file that matches the system on which you are running the license manager.Solaris: sol2.tar64-bit Linux: glnxa64.tar32-bit Linux: glnx86.tar

Summary: Application crashes when invoking a second GUI from COM GUIDE object
Description: This bug can appear if all of the following conditions occur:You create two or more separate COM components using the MATLAB Builder for .NET Each of these components contains GUIDE UI components Your application loads two or more of these components Your application invokes one of the GUIs using the component and then attempts to invoke another GUI using another one of the components.
Workaround: To work around this problem, create just one COM component that contains both of the GUIs. At run time, load and use only this single component to invoke GUIs.

Summary: Profiler reports incorrect results on certain multicore AMD processors
Description: The Profiler reports incorrect results, such as large negative numbers, on certain multicore processors (both AMD and Intel) with power management features enabled.The Profiler uses the time stamp counter (TSC) to measure performance. The multicore chips associated with incorrect Profiler results have a separate TSC for each core, but the counters might not stay synchronized. When the Profiler measures an interval of time, it might get inconsistent answers depending on which core the TSC is queried from.
Workaround: As a workaround, use the real time-based clock option in the Profiler rather than the time stamp counter-based clock. To do so, run the following statement before profiling:profile -timer real

Summary: Callback functions can display previous warnings
Description: In Instrument Control Toolbox, an instrument object call to instrcb.m can repeatedly rethrow the last warning. This has been observed even for warnings unrelated to instrcb.m.  A typical case of this behavior might occur when using the serial port with callback functions, as in the following code:   warning('This is a test warning.');   disp('You should not see test warning again.');   s = serial('COM1');   set(s,'OutputEmptyFcn',@instrcallback)   fopen(s)   fprintf(s,'*IDN?','async')   stopasync(s)   fprintf(s,'*IDN?','async')   stopasync(s)   fprintf(s,'*IDN?','async')   stopasync(s)   fclose(s)   delete(s)   clear s  This code results in the following output:    Warning: This is a test warning.   You should not see test warning again.   OutputEmpty event occurred at 17:32:42 for the object: Serial-COM1.   Warning: This is a test warning.   > In instrcb at 46   OutputEmpty event occurred at 17:32:51 for the object: Serial-COM1.   Warning: This is a test warning.   > In instrcb at 46   OutputEmpty event occurred at 17:32:55 f  Notice that the test warning is erroneously repeated, even though there are no subsequent warnings after the test warning.
Workaround: To prevent these erroneous warning messages, copy the attached file into toolbox\matlab\iofun as instrcb.m using the following instructions according to your platform: On Windows: Save the attached file into C:\TEMP. Then, in MATLAB, enter the commands  >> cd(matlabroot);  >> copyfile('C:\TEMP\instrcb.m', 'toolbox\matlab\iofun\instrcb.m');On UNIX: Save the attached file into /tmp. Then, in MATLAB, enter the commands  >> cd(matlabroot);  >> copyfile('/tmp/instrcb.m', 'toolbox/matlab/iofun/instrcb.m');

Summary: Help inconsistent for multiple class directories in a directory
Description: Running help classname  might display inconsistent results if there are two or more class directories in a directory on the MATLAB search path. When the current directory is the parent directory of a directory containing two or more class directories, the class methods and contructor display for only one of the classes, the first in the directory ordering. For other cases, only the constructor displays. For example, if polynom1  and polynom2  are classes in   D:\Applications\MATLAB\R2006b\work\ then  addpath D:\Applications\MATLAB\R2006b\work\  cd D:\Applications\MATLAB\R2006b\    % parent directory  help polynom1  ... methods and constructor ...  help polynom2  ... constructor only ... If you change directory to the given directory then again only one of the classes will show the class methods as well as the constructor. However, this time it is the last in the directory ordering. Continuing with the example above, we then have:  >> cd work  >> help polynom1  ... constructor only ...  >> help polynom2  ... methods and constructor ...
Workaround: There is no easy workaround that produces a brief description for each method. You can create and run this MATLAB file to display a full description for each method.function classhelp(name)% Show help for all methods of an MATLAB OOPS class called NAME.% It is assumed to be in a directory on the MATLABPATH.%d = fileparts(which(name));dparent = fileparts(d);oldpwd = pwd;try  l = dir([d filesep '*.m']);   % Find all methods  cd(dparent);                  % Move to the parent of the class  for i=1:length(l)    help([name filesep l(i).name]);   end  cd(oldpwd);catch  cd(oldpwd);  error('classhelp failed'); end

Summary: Saving model with new name (via Save As...) causes subsequent HDL code generation failure
Description: When a model is saved under a different name using the Save As...  command, internal data in memory used by Simulink HDL Coder is not updated properly. This causes the HDL code generator to display error messages and then terminate code generation.
Workaround: To  work around this problem: after saving the model under a different name, close the model and reopen it.

Summary: MATLAB fails to start: version `GLIBC_2.3.3' not found
Description: Our system requirements for x86 Linux (glnx86) state that we require version 2.3.2 or better of the GNU C library (glibc 2.3.2).The Debian distribution we use internally claims to be glibc 2.3.2, but internally contains a very small bit of glibc 2.3.3. This can cause MATLAB to fail during startup on older glibc 2.3.2 machines with the error:  Can't load '/usr/local/MATLAB/bin/glnx86/libmwbuiltins.so':   /lib/libdl.so.2: version `GLIBC_2.3.3' not found
Workaround: There are two potential ways to avoid this problem.Upgrade to a newer Linux distribution.Replace the libmwbuiltins.so library with one that does not require glibc 2.3.3.  Be sure to choose the version that matches the release of MATLAB that you are using, either R2006a or R2006b.The first option may not be feasible for all users.The second option has only one very minor drawback in that the version command will no longer support the -modules option. Most users will not miss this minor feature.To replace the builtins library, back up your old binaries:   cd $MATLABROOT/bin/glnx86   mv libmwbuiltins.so libmwbuiltins.so.save   mv libmwbuiltins.so.csf libmwbuiltins.so.csf.saveVerify the integrity of the new binaries to ensure that you did not corrupt them during download:  % cksum libmwbuiltins*  4226648574 659672 libmwbuiltins.so.R2006a  2002139265 732336 libmwbuiltins.so.R2006b  3896695380 109097 libmwbuiltins.so.csf.R2006a  4002013493 134977 libmwbuiltins.so.csf.R2006band then put the new binaries into place:  % mv libmwbuiltins.so.$RELEASE libmwbuiltins.so  % mv libmwbuiltins.so.csf.$RELEASE libmwbuiltins.so.csfwhere $RELEASE is either R2006a or R2006b, as appropriate for your installation.

Summary: The database function fails for some forms of JDBC database URLs
Description: Running database  to connect to a database via a JDBC driver fails for certain forms of the databaseurl  argument, returning the URL  field as empty and returning the error 'IO exception: NL Exception was generated'  in the Message  field, as in this example: url = [jdbc:oracle:thin:@'...'(description=(address_list=(address=(protocol=tcp)(port=1111)(host=mmmmm.nn.com)))' ...'(connect_data=(sid=MMMMM)(SERVER=DEDICATED)))' ];conn = database('mmmmm', 'username', 'password', 'oracle.jdbc.driver.OracleDriver', url );conn =       Instance: 'mmmmm'      UserName: 'username'        Driver: []           URL: []   Constructor: [1x1 com.mathworks.toolbox.database.databaseConnect]       Message: 'IO exception: NL Exception was generated'        Handle: 0       TimeOut: []    AutoCommit: 'off'          Type: 'Database Object' The expected output for this example is conn =      Instance: 'mmmmm'      UserName: 'username'        Driver: 'oracle.jdbc.driver.OracleDriver'           URL: 'jdbc:oracle:thin:@mmmm:1111:'   Constructor: [1x1 com.mathworks.toolbox.database.databaseConnect]       Message: []        Handle: [1x1 oracle.jdbc.driver.T4CConnection]       TimeOut: 0    AutoCommit: 'on'          Type: 'Database Object'
Workaround: You can work around the problem using the attached file. To do so, follow these instructions: Navigate to matlabroot/java/jar/toolbox, where matlabroot is the directory in which you installed MATLAB, as determined by running matlabroot in the Command Window.In that directory, rename the file database.jar to database.jar.old.Download the attached file, database.jar, into the matlabroot/java/jar/toolbox directory.Restart MATLAB.

Summary: Unexplained failure in engClose and engOpen commands
Description: When you call engClose , it closes the existing MATLAB automation server. MATLAB takes a few seconds to shut down its process. During this time if you call engOpen  again, it will attach to the MATLAB session that was closing. This will result in an invalid engine pointer.
Workaround: Two ways to work around this are: Use engOpenSingleUse, so that you get a new instance of MATLAB.Wait a few seconds until the previous MATLAB is shutdown completely.

Summary: Slower training algorithms
Description: Some networks train slower in R2006a than they did in previous versions of the Toolbox.The training algorithms are general in this release at the expense of optimization. As an enhancement, we are considering developing this optimization with the required support for generalized training algorithms in a future release.
Workaround: There is no work around at this time.

Summary: Error when assigning a structure or a cell array to a single output variable.
Description: From an N element structure array S, you should be able to assign 1 to N outputs. For example, ifs.a = 1;   s(2).a = 2;   s(3).a = 3;   s(4).a = 4; then the command[x1 x2 x3 x4] = s.a producesx1 =     1x2 =     2x3 =     3x4 =     4 However, the command[x1] = s.a generates the error??? Illegal right hand side in assignment. Too many elements.  Likewise, for the cell arrayx={1,2,3,4;5,6,7,8} the commanda=x{:,1} generates the error??? Illegal right hand side in assignment. Too many elements.
Workaround: There is no workaround.

Summary: MATLAB generated EPS files with LaTeX markup cannot be inserted into some applications.
Description: The font information for figures that include LaTeX markup that MATLAB embeds into encapsulated postcript (EPS) files is positioned incorrectly. This prevents some applications, such as Microsoft Word, from being able to read the EPS file.

Summary: Set commands for MDD drivers result in errors if property type is Boolean
Description: If an IVI-COM device object is created from a MDD file, which is generated using MAKEMID or MIDEDIT, set  commands will result in errors if the property is a Boolean type. An example of this behavior would be: >> obj = icdevice('TekScope.mdd');>> connect(obj)>> disp = get(obj,'Display')>> get(disp,'AutoBright')  ans =  on  >> set(disp,'AutoBright',0)??? Parameter must be a non-empty string.
Workaround: To work around this issue, use MIDEDIT to modify the set code:if strcmpi(propertyValue,'on')propertyValue = 1;endif strcmpi(propertyValue,'off')propertyValue = 0;end

Summary: Cannot create a JPEG file specifying a quality setting in nodisplay mode on Linux
Description: When running MATLAB on Linux in the nodisplay mode (starting MATLAB with the -nodisplay  option), if you specify a quality setting when printing to a JPEG file you will receive an error message:    Problem calling GhostScript. System returned error
Workaround: Do not specify the quality setting in the -djpeg device option. Instead of using: print -djpeg90 ...only useprint -djpeg ... You can run MATLAB in display mode if you need to specify the quality parameter.

Summary: UNIX: executing a file resolved using a relative path produces warning or fails if file is a symbolic link
Description: On UNIX, executing a file resolved using a relative path produces a warning or fails if the file is a symbolic link. For example, with this directory structure matlabroot/work/model/matlabroot/work/model/loadtest.mdlmatlabroot/files/paramSets/matlabroot/files/paramSets/params000.mmatlabroot/files/matlabroot/files/params.m -> paramSets/params000.m      (symbolic link) when you execute the link in the Command Window (in R2006b), the link isresolved from a relative path. It produces a warning the first time it is executed, as in this example:cd matlabroot/work/modeladdpath ../filesparamsWarning: Could not find an exact (case-sensitive) match for 'params'.matlabroot/work/files/paramSets/params000.m is a case-insensitive match and will be used instead... An error can occur when executing a callback that contains the commands, as in this example of loading a Simulink model: addpath ../filesparamscd matlabroot/work/modelloadtestWarning: loadtest.mdl, line ...: Error evaluating 'PreLoadFcn' callbackof block_diagram 'loadtest'. Undefined function or variable 'params'.

Summary: Help button does not work in any OPC Toolbox block library dialog
Description: If you click Help  in any OPC Toolbox block dialog, nothing happens.Alternately, if you right-click on an OPC Toolbox block library dialog, and select Help  from the context menu, an empty page is displayed.
Workaround: As a workaround, you can choose the appropriate block name from the "Blocks - Alphabetical List" section of the OPC Toolbox documentation.Or, to quickly access help on each of the blocks, you can type any of these commands at the MATLAB command line:  doc opcconfiguration  doc opc_read  doc opcwrite  doc opcqualityparts

Summary: Incorrect sample time propagation for multi-rate bus signals passing through atomic subsystems with Inport directly connected to Outport
Description: Simulink can have an incorrect sample time propagation when the following conditions hold:A multi-rate bus is passed into an Atomic SubsystemThe path that the bus takes through the Subsystem has the Inport directly wired to the Outport In this case the sample time of all of the signals in the bus should be the same as the sample time of the non-virtual Inport block, because that block does a copy of the signals. Instead, the blocks downstream that operate on the selected signals see the sample time of the orginal blocks building the bus. This can lead to wrong answers, for example in the case of a Constant signal packed into the bus.
Workaround: To work around this issue, insert a Signal Conversion block before the Atomic Subsystem.

Summary: Cannot start GUIDE GUI. Errors are thrown infinitely.
Description: When you start a GUIDE-generated GUI, errors may be generated in an infinite loop and may cause a segmentation violation and MATLAB exit. This could happen if you do either of the following:  Manually delete a GUIDE-generated callback sub-function such as CreateFcn  from the GUI MATLAB-file Change the string value of a callback such as CreateFcn  in the Property Inspector such that no sub-function whose signature matches that resulting callback string can be found in your GUI MATLAB-file
Workaround: If you are using R2006a, download the attached gui_mainfcn.m file and place it in matlabroot\toolbox\matlab\uitools\If you are using R2006b, download the attached R2006b_workaround.zip file. Unzip it and place the resulting gui_mainfcn.m in matlabroot\toolbox\matlab\guide\where matlabroot is the directory returned when you run the matlabroot function..

Summary: Incorrect rounding using imfilter
Description: When filtering a two-dimensional uint8  or uint16  image, imfilter  rounds intermediate floating-point values with a fractional part of 0.5 toward zero instead of away from zero. This occurs on some Intel Architecture platforms on which imfilter  uses the Intel Integrated Performance Primitives Library (IPPL).
Workaround: To workaround this issue, disable the use of the IPPL library for Image Processing Toolbox functions. The reference page for the ippl function explains how to do this.

Summary: Receive "MCC: Count must be integer multiple of packet size for Continuous mode" error on Measurement Computing PCI-DAS4020/12.
Description: When doing a start  measurement on the MCC PCI-DAS4020/12, the following error message displayed:   "??? MCC: Count must be integer multiple of packet size for Continuous mode"
Workaround: To work around this issue, do the following steps:    Quit MATLAB.    Change to the directory $MATLAB/toolbox/daq/daq/private, where $MATLAB is the directory where you installed MATLAB.    Back up the file mwmcc.ini to another name such as mwmcc.ini.bak.    Open mwmcc.ini and find the text "[PCI-DAS4020/12]".    In that section, find the line "PacketSize = 2048", and change it to  "PacketSize = 131072".    Save and close the file.You may also have to configure the contiguous memory option  within Instacal. Contact Measurement Computing for more information.

Summary: User input functions ginput and gtext  can error out if called in an interactive mode
Description: Calling the ginput  or gtext  functions while an interactive mode (such as Pan, Zoom or Rotate3D) is active can cause the following error being printed to the command window:??? Error using ==> getInvalid handle.Error in ==> uisuspend at 34    uistate = struct(...
Workaround: To work around this issue use the following steps:Save a copy of the file $MATLAB/toolbox/matlab/uitools/uisuspend.m (where $MATLAB is the root directory of your MATLAB installation).Modify the file by moving lines 32-33    %Turn off any interactive modes that may be on    scribeclearmode(fig,'');higher up to be before line 23,    chi = findobj(fig);Run the commandrehash toolboxcache

Summary: Verilog code from Relational Operator Block does not produce correct results when rounding is required
Description: Verilog code generated by Simulink HDL Coder for the Relational Operator block may not produce correct results in cases where the two input operands do not have the same data type. Verilog rounding and saturation code  generated in this case does not take Verilog data type inheritance rules into account.
Workaround: To work around this problem, make sure that the two inputs to the block have the same data type. Use a separate Data Type Conversion block, if neccessary.

Summary: How do I use my old Builder for COM (.cbl), BUILDER for Excel (.mxl), and Builder for .NET (.nbl) projects with DEPLOYTOOL?
Description: How do I use my old Builder for COM (.cbl), BUILDER for Excel (.mxl), and Builder for .NET (.nbl) projects with DEPLOYTOOL?
Workaround: The old Builder for COM/Excel/.NET (.cbl, .mxl, .nbl) projects cannot be opened with the new Compiler GUI, DEPLOYTOOL, without modification. These projects need to be converted to new project format (.prj) as follows:  Download the attached utility, BUILDER2PRJ.M, to the matlabroot/toolbox/compiler directory.  Restart MATLAB to ensure that the utility is included in the MATLAB path.  Use BUILDER2PRJ as follows to convert your old project:            builder2prj('oldproject.cbl')               This will convert oldproject.cbl to oldproject.prj that can be opened with DEPLOYTOOL.  For more information on BUILDER2PRJ, run help builder2prj

Summary: Using set_param and add_block to add a block to a masked subsystem can cause problems
Description: Using the add_block  function to insert a block in a masked subsystem whose mask initialization callback also invokes set_param  can cause the block position and size to change randomly.
Workaround: EitherRemove dialog callbacks that execute set_param, orfirst execute the ADD_BLOCK with the block name and in the next statement set the position and parameter settings with set_param.

Summary: Template makefiles: shared utilities make variables (SHARED_*) do not honor MAKEFILE_FILESEP macro
Description: The template makefile (TMF) macro MAKEFILE_FILESEP is used to override the default file separator (/ or \) for file paths in a TMF.  This is useful for such purposes as generating code on a Windows system to be compiled on a platform other than Windows. However, the TMF shared utilities make variables SHARED_SRC, SHARED_SRC_DIR, SHARED_BIN_DIR, and SHARED_LIB do not honor the MAKEFILE_FILESEP value (if defined) in your TMF, and instead use the default file separator.
Workaround: To work around this bug in R2006b, perform the following steps:Note:  In the following instructions, matlabroot represents your MATLAB installation root, as returned by the MATLAB command matlabroot.In your MATLAB installation tree, rename the following files, for example, by adding .old to the end of each file name.matlabroot/toolbox/rtw/rtw/rtw_c.mmatlabroot/toolbox/rtw/rtw/rtw_c.pmatlabroot/toolbox/rtw/rtw/rtwrebuild.mmatlabroot/toolbox/rtw/rtw/rtwrebuild.pmatlabroot/toolbox/rtw/rtw/@RTW/genMakefileAndBuild.mmatlabroot/toolbox/rtw/rtw/@RTW/genMakefileAndBuild.pmatlabroot/toolbox/rtw/rtw/@RTW/@BuildInfo/addKeyValuePair.pmatlabroot/toolbox/rtw/rtw/@RTW/@BuildInfo/updateFileSeparator.pmatlabroot/rtw/c/accel/accel_intel.tmfmatlabroot/rtw/c/accel/accel_lcc.tmfmatlabroot/rtw/c/ert/ert_intel.tmfmatlabroot/rtw/c/ert/ert_lcc.tmfmatlabroot/rtw/c/grt/grt_intel.tmfmatlabroot/rtw/c/grt/grt_lcc.tmfmatlabroot/rtw/c/grt_malloc/grt_malloc_intel.tmfmatlabroot/rtw/c/grt_malloc/grt_malloc_lcc.tmfmatlabroot/rtw/c/rsim/rsim_intel.tmfmatlabroot/rtw/c/rsim/rsim_lcc.tmfmatlabroot/rtw/c/rtwsfcn/rtwsfcn_intel.tmfmatlabroot/rtw/c/rtwsfcn/rtwsfcn_lcc.tmfmatlabroot/rtw/c/modelref/modelrefsim_lcc.tmfDownload the attached files and place them at the locations listed in step 1.Restart MATLAB.Additionally, if you are using model references in your model, go to the Real-Time Workshop pane in the Configuration Parameters dialog box and add the following string to the Make command field, anywhere after make_rtw:  USE_MDLREF_LIBPATHS=1For example, if the existing Make command value was make_rtw, the result would look as follows:  make_rtw USE_MDLREF_LIBPATHS=1

Summary: Matlab might crash on Linux in libnativehg.so
Description: Matlab might crash sporadically on Linux while rendering a figure window. When this occurs, a stack trace is generated with one of the top frames of the stack reading:  libnativehg.so:JNIEnv_::CallVoidMethod(_jobject*, _jmethodID*, ...)
Workaround: To avoid the crash, run Matlab with MToolkit by setting AWT_TOOLKIT environment variable to MToolkit at the command prompt before starting Matlab:setenv AWT_TOOLKIT MToolkitNote that this configuration is generally untested.  One capability that is lost with this configuration is the on-top style of undocked plot tools.  Instead, the plot tools become separate window frames when undocked.

Summary: Poor performance of compiled app accross call boundary
Description: Applications generated on UNIX platforms with the MATLAB Compiler may run more slowly than the MATLAB-code runs in MATLAB. The cause of this decrease in performance is a fixed amount of overhead associated with each MATLAB function call.

Summary: Creating a java Socket object is slow on Windows XP
Description: Creating a java.net.Socket object on Windows XP takes on the order of 5 seconds. Normally this should take a fraction of a second. This is due to a known bug in Sun's 1.5 release of the Java libraries. It is Sun's bug number 5092063. Details can be seen at: http://bugs.sun.com/bugdatabase/view_bug.do?bug_id=5092063
Workaround: Here are 3 work-arounds suggested by Sun. (Note, these assume that the basic cause of the problem is that the jvm does a reverse lookup for a DNS server which isn't actually there). The reverse lookup takes a long time only on machines where the DNS is not properly configured. More precisely when the address for the NameServer points to a non existing DNS server. Because of the nature of DNS, this triggers a long timeout. The reverse lookup is not necessary and it is fixed in the Java Virtual Machine version 1.6, and retrofitted to version 1.5 update 6 and later.  There are a number of workarounds for the problem in earlier versions: 1) Fix the DNS configuration. If the server is reponding, the lookup does not wait until timeout has expired but returns immediately. This is the preferred workaround as it does not require a change in the application. 2) Use Socket s = new Socket(InetAddress.getByName(), ); instead. This does not trigger a reverse lookup 3) The code that triggers the reverse lookup is in the ProxySelector mechanism (when checking if SOCKS should be used or not). If you are sure your application does not need proxies, you can disable the ProxySelector by using ProxySelector.setDefault(null) at the start of the application. However, this workaround also disables the http & ftp proxies.

Summary: Enabled subsystems and resetting states
Description: If a SimMechanics machine is placed inside an enabled subsystem configured to reset states, the code generated by Real-Time Workshop issues an error when the subsystem becomes enabled:Attempting to use multiple instances of SimMechanics.
Workaround: To eliminate the error, overwrite the following file in your MATLAB installation with the attached file.$(MATLAB)/toolbox/physmod/mech/mech/tlc_c/mech_engine.tlcNote that while the attached file fixes this error and allows the simulation to proceed, SimMechanics does not reset its states in enabled subsystems. To reset states programmatically, consider using the SimMechanics commands MECH_SET_STATES and MECH_GET_STATES.

Summary: help function runs slowly
Description: When I use the help  function in the following context:%%%BEGIN CODE%%%help <function>%%%END CODE%%% help  sometimes runs slowly on some machines, taking over 90 seconds to respond. At other times, it runs fast.
Workaround: The problem may be due to virus scan interference. Turn off the virus scanner and see if this resolves the issue.If the help function speed does not improve upon closing the virus scan, try the following.Type edit help at the MATLAB command prompt.Comment out line 336 which states:url = com.mathworks.mlwidgets.help.HelpInfo.getReferencePageUrl([topic '.html']);Save this change.After this change, if there is a doc reference page for a function, the line "Reference page in Help browser: doc " no longer prints out at the end of the help command output.

Summary: Windows XP: changing Display settings causes exception
Description: On Windows XP platforms, changing the Windows Display settings for Appearance while MATLAB is running might cause an exception to appear in the Command Window:Exception in thread "AWT-EventQueue-0"java.lang.NullPointerExceptionat com.sun.java.swing.plaf.windows.WindowsTableHeaderUI$XPDefaultRenderer.paint(Unknown Source)at javax.swing.CellRendererPane.paintComponent(Unknown Source)at javax.swing.plaf.basic.BasicTableHeaderUI.paintCell(Unknown Source)at javax.swing.plaf.basic.BasicTableHeaderUI.paint(Unknown Source...
Workaround: Restart MATLAB to clear the exception from the Command Window. To prevent the problem, change the Windows Display settings before starting MATLAB.

Summary: Simulation of models containing Ideal Switch blocks may slow down significantly
Description: Simulation of a model containing Ideal Switch blocks, which runs fine in previous versions, may slow down significantly when run in V4.1. In this version, the operation of the Ideal Switch block is more sensitive to zero-crossing detection. Consecutive zero crossings slow down the simulation. Eventually an error message can stop the simulation telling you that the maximum number of consecutive zero crossing has been reached.
Workaround: To work around this problem, you can increase the number of consecutive zero crossings allowed in the solver diagnostic controls to continue the simulation, or disable the zero crossing detection of some of the blocks to accelerate the simulation.

Summary: fzero function enters an infinite loop
Description: For certain problems, the fzero  function to has been observed to enter an infinite loop and never terminate. This can occur if the termination tolerance TolX  is set to an inordinately small number, i.e., a number that is significantly lower than eps .
Workaround: To work around this issue, you can set TolX to be a value that is no smaller than the default eps.Setting TolX equal to eps achieves as much accuracy as possible for a numerical method like fzero, while avoiding entering an infinite loop.

Summary: PIL models with math functions using Diab Toolchain fail during SIL stub compilation
Description: When compiling a PIL model that uses math functions with the Diab Toolchain and ISO-C support selected, the generated model.h  header file is not host-friendly as it includes the Diab header file mathf.h . This header file contains Diab ISO-C extensions. This causes the following host SIL compilation error with lcc:fatal error C1083: Cannot open include file: 'mathf.h': No such file or directory
Workaround: There is no workaround to make this work with ISO-C. To resolve this issue you need to set the Target floating-point math environment to ANSI-C. This is done via Configuration Parameters -> Real-Time Workshop -> Interface settings.

Summary: Get "WAIT reached its timeout before OBJ stopped running" when acquiring data on Measurement Computing devices.
Description: Under certain conditions, a Measurement Computing device will start running, but not acquire any data, causing a wait  or getdata  operation to fail. This is more common when doing short acquisitions (under 100ms) at higher rates (over 20,000 samples/second), such as:ai = analoginput('mcc',0);expectedSampleRate = 40000;set(ai,'SampleRate',expectedSampleRate); set(ai,'SamplesPerTrigger',1000); start(ai);wait(ai);??? WAIT reached its timeout before OBJ stopped running.
Workaround: This error can be avoided by acquiring data for a longer period to force the device into continuous acquisition mode. You can trim the excess data when it is returned to MATLAB using getdata.ai = analoginput('mcc',0);expectedSampleRate = 40000;set(ai,'SampleRate',expectedSampleRate); set(ai,'SamplesPerTrigger',10000); start(ai);wait(ai);data = getdata(ai);data = data(1:1000);

Summary: Figure redraws at PaperPosition size after copying to clipboard
Description: If you turn off the option in the Figure Copy Options  dialog to copy at screen size , then select Copy Figure , the figure contents redraw at the PaperPosition  size.
Workaround: Resize the figure window after copying to clipboard. Make the figure resize either manually (click and drag a corner of the figure window with the mouse) or via MATLAB-code, such as% move figure by 1 unit and then move it backset(gcf, 'position', get(gcf, 'position')+1);set(gcf, 'position', get(gcf, 'position')-1);

Summary: Modal dialogs are not truly modal on Unix and Mac
Description: On Unix and Mac, a modal figure window does not block the user from using MATLAB or any other MATLAB windows that are currently open. The user is able to access and use the command window.  Here are two code examples that exhibit this problem: % Create a modal figure figure('WindowStyle','modal')% Create an error dialog and set the modal optionerrordlg('This is a modal dialog','Modal Dialog','modal') This bug affects various MATLAB dialogs and GUIs.
Workaround: The following workaround shows how to block users from executing MATLAB code until the modal dialog is dismissed. However, MATLAB windows, including any currently opened GUIs, remain accessible. To block users from executing MATLAB code through MATLAB scripts or the command window, you can call uiwait and pass in the handle of the modal figure. For example: In the above example, a user can give focus to the command window, but cannot execute any MATLAB code in it until the modal figure f is closed.f=figure('WindowStyle','modal');    uiwait(f);

Summary: Zero initialization of root output of external storage class controlled by wrong option
Description: In Real-Time Workship Embedded Coder, if a root-level outputof a model has an external storage class, such as ExportedGlobal or an external custom storage class, the output's zero initialization should be controlled by the Configuration parameter "Remove root-level I/O zero initialization".  Instead, the zero initialization is controlled by the parameter "Remove internal state zero initialization".

Summary: Sporadic error: "External mode packet buffer too small" with Real-Time Windows Target 2.5.2 (R14SP2).
Description: Real-Time Windows Target 2.5.2 (R14SP2) throws a sporadic error as follows: Error occurred while executing External Mode Mex-file 'rtwinext':External mode packet buffer too small.
Workaround: This issue is under investigation and no fully tested workarounds are available. However, the following are three procedures that may help resolve the problem. This is a machine-dependent issue, and the procedures cannot be guaranteed to work on all machines.Minimize the number of external mode communication channels, such as scopes and displays. Users often attach scopes and displays to signals "just in case".  While this approach is acceptable for simulation, it wastes packet buffer space, which is one of the most critical resources when running in external mode.Reduce the amount of data transmitted through external mode channels. In the External Mode Control Panel, set the Duration (which must be a positive integer) as low as possible. The shorter duration reduces the number of samples in one buffer.If the computer supports hyperthreading, disable hyperthreading in the BIOS setup. Hyperthreading helps few applications. For real-time applications it does not help at all, and can cause additional problems. You should have your System Administrator do this for your machine.
