Summary: Problems in contourfm
Description: There are multiple problems with the function contourfm , which plots filled contours in a map axes:    If the axes limit modes are set to 'manual'  (or axis tight  is set) prior to executing contourfm , the plot disappears if the figure's 'Renderer'  property is set to 'opengl' .     The LineSpec syntax, if supplied, is ignored.     The parameter/value pair syntax incorrectly states that contourfm  uses the line properties specified to draw the contours. The handles returned from contourfm  are type patch , not line , and you cannot set their properties via contourfm .

Summary: MATLAB compatibility issue with Intel Math Kernel Library
Description: MATLAB is not compatible with single precision BLAS routines from the Intel Math Kernel Library which return a single precision floating point number on 64-bit LINUX platforms.

Summary: Edit button in S-function dialog only works after MEX is run
Description: When you create a new C-MEX S-function, you can not open the C function for editing via the S-function dialog in the model until the C function has been compiled using MEX.
Workaround: Open the C function using the MATLAB editor.

Summary: Mixed HDL languages for generated code and test bench are not supported
Description: Simulink HDL Coder does not support use of mixed HDL languages for the generated code and the generated test bench.  You must use the same language (either VHDL or Verilog) for both.  Only minimal error checking is done to try to ensure that the same language is used for both code and test bench. Errors will arise if you try to mix languages.
Workaround: To work around this limitation, you can first generate both code and test bench in VHDL. Then, select Verilog as your target language and generate both code and test bench again.

Summary: Confusing error message when attempting to compile code in private directory
Description: When the current working directory path contains the string "private" and MATLAB-code residing in that directory is compiled, the MATLAB Compiler throws confusing error messages.
Workaround: There are no known workarounds to compile code in private directories except to move the code to a different directory or to rename the directory.

Summary: uigetfile sometimes works incorrectly on Linux platforms.
Description: On some occasions, you may not be able to use the mouse to select a file in the file browser on Linux platforms. This can occur if the file browser is opened using the uigetfile  function or by selecting Open  from the File  menu.
Workaround: You can select the file using the arrow keys on the keyboard, or you can use one of the following workarounds for MATLAB releases prior to Version 7.4 (R2007a).Move the three libXm.so* library files (you may have none, one, two or all three on your system) out of the MATLAB path using one of the following commands. For 32-bit platforms:   mv $MATLAB/sys/os/glnx86/libXm.so* saved/  For 64-bit platforms:   mv $MATLAB/sys/os/glnxa64/libXm.so* saved/where $MATLAB is your MATLAB root directory. If this does not solve the problem, restore the files to the MATLAB path and try the following workaround.Change your Linux LANG environment variable: For csh variants: linux% ( unsetenv LANG ; /path/to/my/matlab ) & For Bourne-shell variants: linux$ ( unset LANG ; /path/to/my/matlab ) & Execute the following command before calling uigetfile:   setappdata(0,'UseNativeSystemDialogs',false)You can have this solution take effect whenever you start MATLAB by adding the this command to your startup.m file. For information about the startup.m file, type the following at the MATLAB prompt.   doc startup

Summary: Multiple calls to libInitialize and libTerminate in compiled code fail
Description: The issue arises in a compiled application when multiple calls to lib Initialize  and lib Terminate  are executed within one pair of calls to mclInitializeApplication  and mclTerminateApplication . For example, the code for a library named "foo" would be of the form: mclInitializeApplicationlibfooInitializelibfooTerminatelibfooInitializelibfooTerminatemclTerminateApplication  In the above example, the second call to libfoo Initialize  fails because the first call to libfoo Terminate  has already shutdown the MCR.
Workaround: To avoid this problem, only call libInitialize (and libTerminate) mcc compiled libraries in your application once.

Summary: TriggerType of HwAnalog gives unexpected error on Measurement Computing devices
Description: On Measurement Computing devices, using a TriggerType  of 'HwAnalog'  results in an error as follows:ai = analoginput('mcc',1);addchannel(ai,0);ai.SamplesPerTrigger = 5000;ai.TriggerType = 'HwAnalog';ai.TriggerConditionans = TrigAboveai.TriggerConditionValueans =     0start(ai)??? Error using ==> daqdevice.startMCC: Low trigger threshold is greater than high threshold
Workaround: Currently, you must use the software analog triggering built into the Data Acquisition Toolbox to perform this operation.ai = analoginput('mcc',1);addchannel(ai,0);ai.SamplesPerTrigger = 5000;ai.TriggerType = 'software';ai.TriggerConditionans = Rising ai.TriggerConditionValue ans =      0start(ai)

Summary: Left click on Uicontrol does not terminate waitforbuttonpress command
Description: The waitforbuttonpress  function stops program execution until a key is pressed or a mouse button is clicked over a figure window. If you set waitforbuttonpress  at the command line, left clicking on a Uicontrol that has its Enable  property set to on  does not cause program execution to resume.
Workaround: Do either of these:Left click anywhere in the figure window except on the Uicontrol.Right click on the Uicontrol.

Summary: Uibuttongroup handle is incorrectly documented in GUIDE generated MATLAB code.
Description: For a uibuttongroup created in GUIDE, the automatically generated comment, % hObject    handle to uipanel1 (see GCBO) is incorrect. hObject  is actually the handle of the selected radio button or toggle button, and not the handle of the uibuttongroup.
Workaround: Use handles.buttongroup-tag as the handle of the uibuttongroup.

Summary: Shared library interface does not support sized arrays in structures.
Description: Any structure with fields containing sized arrays, such as the following example, cannot be used with loadlibrary.   struct badstruct {  char name[30];  int location[2];  ...};
Workaround: Given the following example, choose one of the listed workaround solutions. struct badstruct { char name[30]; int location[2];}Replace the sized array with discrete variables, possibly in another structure. This does not require recompiling the library and can be done by modifying a copy of the header or a generated MATLAB file. See the loadlibrary mfilename option for more information on how to generate a prototype MATLAB file.For example, the location field above could be replaced with:int xloc; int yloc;or struct location {int x; int y};struct goodstruct {   location loc;   ...}Modify the source code to use pointers instead of sized arrays. The following structure works correctly: struct goodstruct {   char* name;   int* location;};Create a helper dll or mex function to convert the structure: struct matlabLocation {   char* name;   int* location;};void LocationToMatlab(badstruct *in; matlabLocation *out){    out->name=&in.name;    out->location=&in.location;}

Summary: Clearing variable from MATLAB workspace can cause a Model Explorer error
Description: Using the MATLAB clear command to clear a variable from the MATLAB workspace can cause the Model Explorer to display an error message at the MATLAB command line if the following sequence of steps was taken You created the variable at the MATLAB command line with a node other than the Base Workspace node selected in the Model Explorer. You selected the Base Workspace node in the Model Explorer. You cleared the variable with the Base Workspace node selected in the Model Explorer.
Workaround: Select the Model Explorer's Base Workspace node before creating variables in the MATLAB workspace.

Summary: Attempts to use Parallel Port in applications created by the MATLAB Compiler fail
Description: If you create an application using the MATLAB Compiler that accesses the parallel port via Data Acquisition Toolbox, you might get this error message "Failure accessing AdaptorInfo",  with this Error ID"daq:daqhwinfo:unexpected"
Workaround: To address this, copy the winio.sys file from the?<yourcompiledexelocation>\<yourcompiledexe>_mcr\bin\win32 directory to the directory that contains the <yourcompiledexe>.EXE file.?? For example, if your application is called "myapp,"? copy the myapp\myapp_mcr\bin\win32\winio.sys?to?the directory that contains the myapp.exe file.

Summary: Figure Undo does not work for most plotting tool actions
Description: The Undo  item on the Figure Edit  menu does not undo most operations performed with the plotting tools. Currently, the only actions that can be undone are zooming, rotating, and manual movement (dragging) of an annotation object.
Workaround: No workarounds exist.

Summary: Profiling for multitasking projects reports unreliable timing information
Description: For a multitasking system, DSP/BIOS STS objects cannot reliably measure thetime spent in all tasks. If a task can be pre-empted, measurementsmight be incorrect. Embedded Target for TI C6000 DSP only includes profiling instrumentation for atomic systems that run at the base sample rate.
Workaround: If you run your multitasking model in singletasking mode, you can get thetiming measurements for all the systems in your model for one iteration. Perform these steps to profile your multitasking system in singletasking mode. Select Tools->Real-Time Workshop->Options.In the Solver category,select SingleTasking mode.Rebuild and execute your model on your C6000hardware.  Your program will probably immediately overrun because singletasking moderequires that all tasks complete within the base sample time.  However, all of the model systems will run once before the program overruns.  When the overrun indication occurs, click Halt in Code ComposerStudio.You must do this because DSP/BIOS sits in a terminal infiniteloop (assuming your overrun action option setting is Notify and halt*).  EnterCCS_Obj.profile('report')at the MATLAB prompt to report the statistics measurements.Now you can observe the timingmeasurements for each subsystem. Remember that the reported percentages arecalculated relative to the base sample time. You must do some arithmetic tofigure out whether a given system will fit in its available time interval.For instance, if your base sample time is 1 second, subsystem A executesevery 3 seconds, the base-rate task takes 0.1 seconds to run, and A takes2.5 seconds to run, your system should execute without overruns inmultitasking mode.* If you change the overrun action option from its default (Notify andhalt) to Notify and continue or None, you can get measurements formultiple iterations of the system.  Also, you will be able to request theprofile report without halting the CPU first (step 4 above).

Summary: Sporadic "Corrupt P-file" error
Description: "Corrupt P-file" error sporadically occurs if the specified locale defines a character other than a period character as a decimal point character.
Workaround: Execute the attached MEX code to resolve the error. 1) Build the MEX code.mex changelocale.cpp2) Execute the MEX code.changelocale

Summary: delaunay, delaunay3, and delaunayn functions create triangles and tetrahedra that have inconsistent orientation.
Description: Delaunay triangles and tetrahedra should respect the following convention: The vertices of a triangle should be oriented in a counterclockwise manner. The base vertices of a tetrahedron should be oriented in a counterclockwise manner when viewed from the fourth vertex.

Summary: Copied file in Current Directory browser has new Last Modified date and time
Description: When pasting a copied file into the Current Directory browser, the Current Directory browser uses current data for the Last Modified date and time of the pasted file. This differs from Windows Explorer and other file manager conventions, which use the date and time of the copied file for the pasted file. As an example, assume myfolder/myfile.ext  has a Last Modified date and time of Sep 24 2001. On August 7, 2006, you copy myfile.ext  from either the Current Directory browser or an OS file manager like Windows Explorer. Paste it into myotherfolder  in the Current Directory browser. Now myotherfolder/myfile.ext  has a Last Modified date and time of Aug 7 2006. If you had pasted myfile.ext  into myotherfolder  in Windows Explorer instead, the Last Modified date and time for myotherfolder/myfile.ext  would be Sep 24 2001.
Workaround: As a workaround, you can copy using the ! (bang) operator and operating system commands, which will preserve the date and time of the copied file.For example, on UNIX!cp file1.txt file2.txtand on Windows!copy file1.txt file2.txt

Summary: Duplicate instances of GUI components are added when running a GUI in GUIDE
Description: When creating or editing a GUIDE GUI, running the GUI from GUIDE may result in duplicate components such as menus, user interface controls (uicontrol s), and toolbars, being added to the run time GUI.
Workaround: This problem can happen if you add code that creates a GUI component directly in your GUI MATLAB-file, especially in a CreateFcn callback. If this is the case, move the GUI component creation code to the opening function, OpeningFcn, callback of your GUI MATLAB-file.

Summary: MAXFVAL does not always equal max(FVAL) in FMINIMAX
Description: The third output argument of fminimax , maxfval , is described in the documentation as the maximum of the function values (provided via fun ) evaluated at the solution x , that is, max(fval) . However, fminimax  returns in maxfval  the objective function value of the reformulated minimax problem that is internally constructed by the algorithm. This value is typically very close, but not necessarily equal to, max(fval) .
